35. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n25: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n26: \\tuatomic \\\"go.uber.org/atomic\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n30: \\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/api/label\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n36: \\tlabelutil \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\\\"\\n37: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n40: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\\\"\\n41: \\tpm \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n44: )\\n45: \\n46: var (\\n47: \\tlog = xds.Log\\n48: \\n49: \\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\\n50: \\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\\n51: )\\n52: \\n53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\\n55: \\n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\\\"K_REVISION\\\", \\\"\\\",\\n57: \\t\\\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\\\").Get()\\n58: \\n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\\n61: \\n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\\n64: \\n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\\n67: \\n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\\n70: \\n71: type Connection struct {\\n72: \\txds.Connection\\n73: \\n74: \\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\\n75: \\t// This is included in internal events.\\n76: \\tnode *core.Node\\n77: \\n78: \\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\\n79: \\tproxy *model.Proxy\\n80: \\n81: \\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\\n82: \\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\\n83: \\n84: \\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\\n85: \\n86: \\ts   *DiscoveryServer\\n87: \\tids []string\\n88: }\\n89: \\n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\\n91: \\treturn &conn.Connection\\n92: }\\n93: \\n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\\n95: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\\n99: type Event struct {\\n100: \\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\\n101: \\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\\n102: \\n103: \\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\\n104: \\tdone func()\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\\n108: \\treturn &Connection{\\n109: \\t\\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\\n110: \\t}\\n111: }\\n112: \\n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\\n114: \\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\\n118: \\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\\n122: \\treturn conn.proxy\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\\n126: \\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\\n130: \\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n131: \\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\\n132: \\tpushEv.done()\\n133: \\treturn err\\n134: }\\n135: \\n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n140: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n141: \\tlog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \\\", stype,\\n142: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\\n143: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n144: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\\n145: \\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t}\\n147: \\n148: \\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\\n149: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n150: \\t\\treturn s.pushXds(con,\\n151: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\n155: \\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\\n156: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n157: \\t\\treturn nil\\n158: \\t}\\n159: \\n160: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n161: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n162: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n163: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n164: \\n165: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n166: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n167: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n168: \\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n169: \\t\\tDelta:  delta,\\n170: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\n173: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\\n174: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n175: \\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\\n176: \\t// computation.\\n177: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n178: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n179: \\t}\\n180: \\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n181: }\\n182: \\n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n185: \\treturn s.Stream(stream)\\n186: }\\n187: \\n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\\n189: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n190: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n191: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n192: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n193: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n194: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n195: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n196: \\t}\\n197: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n198: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n199: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n200: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n201: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n202: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n203: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n204: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n205: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n206: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n207: \\t}\\n208: \\n209: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n210: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n211: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n212: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n213: \\t}\\n214: \\n215: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n216: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n217: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n218: \\t}\\n219: \\n220: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n221: \\tif err != nil {\\n222: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\tif ids != nil {\\n225: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n226: \\t} else {\\n227: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\\\", peerAddr)\\n228: \\t}\\n229: \\n230: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n231: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n232: \\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n233: \\tcon.ids = ids\\n234: \\tcon.s = s\\n235: \\treturn xds.Stream(con)\\n236: }\\n237: \\n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\\n239: // to the tracking map.\\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n241: \\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\\n242: \\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\\n243: \\tif err != nil {\\n244: \\t\\treturn err\\n245: \\t}\\n246: \\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\\n247: \\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\\n248: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\\n249: \\t}\\n250: \\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\\n251: \\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\\n252: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\\n253: \\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\\n254: \\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\\n255: \\tcon.node = node\\n256: \\tcon.proxy = proxy\\n257: \\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\\n258: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\\\")\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\n261: \\t// Authorize xds clients\\n262: \\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\treturn err\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\\n267: \\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\\n268: \\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\\n269: \\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\\n270: \\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\\n271: \\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\\n272: \\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\\n273: \\t// proxy\\n274: \\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\\n275: \\n276: \\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\\n277: \\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\\n278: \\t\\ts.closeConnection(con)\\n279: \\t\\treturn err\\n280: \\t}\\n281: \\treturn nil\\n282: }\\n283: \\n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\\n285: \\tif con.ID() == \\\"\\\" {\\n286: \\t\\treturn\\n287: \\t}\\n288: \\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\\n289: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\\n290: }\\n291: \\n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\\n293: \\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\\n294: \\treturn node + \\\"-\\\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\\n301: \\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\\n302: \\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n304: \\t}\\n305: \\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\\n306: \\tif err != nil {\\n307: \\t\\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\\n310: \\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\\n311: \\tproxy.XdsNode = node\\n312: \\treturn proxy, nil\\n313: }\\n314: \\n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\\n318: \\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\\n319: \\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\\n320: \\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\\n321: \\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\\n322: \\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\\n323: \\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\\n324: \\t\\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\\n325: \\t\\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\\n326: \\t\\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\\n327: \\t\\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\\n328: \\t\\t\\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\\n329: \\t\\t\\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\\n330: \\t\\t\\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\\n331: \\t\\t}\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\\n334: \\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\\n335: \\t\\tproxy.Labels,\\n336: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n337: \\t\\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\\n338: \\t\\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\\n339: \\t\\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\\n340: \\t)\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\\n344: \\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\\n345: \\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\\n346: \\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\\n347: \\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\\n348: \\t\\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\\n349: \\t\\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\\n350: \\t\\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\\n351: \\t\\tif localityLabel != \\\"\\\" {\\n352: \\t\\t\\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\\n353: \\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\treturn nil\\n355: \\t}\\n356: \\treturn &core.Locality{\\n357: \\t\\tRegion:  region,\\n358: \\t\\tZone:    zone,\\n359: \\t\\tSubZone: subzone,\\n360: \\t}\\n361: }\\n362: \\n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\\n366: \\tproxy := con.proxy\\n367: \\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\\n368: \\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\\n369: \\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\\n370: \\t\\treturn err\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\\n373: \\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\\n374: \\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\\n375: \\n376: \\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\\n377: \\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\\n378: \\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \\\"\\\" {\\n379: \\t\\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\\n380: \\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\treturn nil\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\\n386: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n387: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n388: \\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\\n389: \\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\\n390: \\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\\n391: \\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\\n392: \\t\\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\\n393: \\t\\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\\n394: \\t\\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\\n395: \\t\\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\\n396: \\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n397: \\t}\\n398: \\n399: \\t// only recompute workload labels when\\n400: \\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\\n401: \\t// 2. proxy update\\n402: \\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\\n403: \\tif recomputeLabels {\\n404: \\t\\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\\n405: \\t\\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\\n406: \\t}\\n407: \\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\\n408: \\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\\n409: \\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\\n410: \\t// applicable to this proxy\\n411: \\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\\n412: \\tif request == nil {\\n413: \\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n414: \\t} else {\\n415: \\t\\tpush = request.Push\\n416: \\t\\tif request.Forced {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\\n420: \\t\\t\\tswitch conf.Kind {\\n421: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\\n422: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n423: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Gateway:\\n424: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n425: \\t\\t\\tcase kind.Ingress:\\n426: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\\n427: \\t\\t\\t\\tshouldResetGateway = true\\n428: \\t\\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\t\\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\\n430: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n431: \\t\\t\\t}\\n432: \\t\\t}\\n433: \\t}\\n434: \\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\\n435: \\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\\n436: \\t\\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\\n437: \\t}\\n438: \\t// only compute gateways for \\\"router\\\" type proxy.\\n439: \\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\\n440: \\t\\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\\n441: \\t}\\n442: \\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\\n443: \\tif request != nil {\\n444: \\t\\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\\n445: \\t}\\n446: }\\n447: \\n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\\n450: \\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\\n451: \\t\\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\\n452: \\t\\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\\n453: \\t\\tif !event.Healthy {\\n454: \\t\\t\\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\\n455: \\t\\t}\\n456: \\t\\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: }\\n459: \\n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\\n463: // pattern.\\n464: //\\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\\n468: \\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\\n469: }\\n470: \\n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n473: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n474: \\n475: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n476: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n477: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n478: \\t}\\n479: \\n480: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n481: \\tif !needsPush {\\n482: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n483: \\t\\treturn nil\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n487: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n488: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n489: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n490: \\t\\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\\n491: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n492: \\t\\t}\\n493: \\t}\\n494: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n495: \\treturn nil\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\\n499: // order after the types listed here\\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\\n501: \\tv3.ClusterType,\\n502: \\tv3.EndpointType,\\n503: \\tv3.ListenerType,\\n504: \\tv3.RouteType,\\n505: \\tv3.SecretType,\\n506: \\tv3.AddressType,\\n507: \\tv3.WorkloadType,\\n508: \\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\\n509: }\\n510: \\n511: // KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\\n512: var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\\n513: \\n514: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\\n515: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\\n516: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\\n517: \\treturn len(s.adsClients)\\n518: }\\n519: \\n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\\n521: \\tvar connection *Connection\\n522: \\n523: \\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\\n524: \\t\\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\\n525: \\t\\t\\tconnection = v\\n526: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n527: \\t\\t}\\n528: \\t}\\n529: \\n530: \\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\\n531: \\tif connection == nil {\\n532: \\t\\treturn\\n533: \\t}\\n534: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n535: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n536: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n537: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t}\\n540: \\n541: \\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\\n542: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n543: \\t\\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\\n544: \\t\\tStart:  time.Now(),\\n545: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\\n546: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n547: \\t})\\n548: }\\n549: \\n550: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\\n551: // Mainly used in Debug interface.\\n552: func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\\n553: \\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\\n554: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n555: \\t\\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\\n556: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\\n557: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n558: \\t})\\n559: }\\n560: \\n561: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\\n562: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n563: \\tif !req.Full {\\n564: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\\\",\\n565: \\t\\t\\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\\n566: \\t} else {\\n567: \\t\\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\\n568: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\\\",\\n569: \\t\\t\\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\\n570: \\t\\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\\n571: \\n572: \\t\\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\\n573: \\t\\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\\n574: \\t\\t\\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\\n575: \\t\\t}\\n576: \\t}\\n577: \\n578: \\ts.StartPush(req)\\n579: }\\n580: \\n581: // Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\\n582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\\n583: \\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\\n584: \\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n585: \\t\\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\\n586: \\t\\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\\n587: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\\\", currentlyPending)\\n588: \\t\\t}\\n589: \\t}\\n590: \\treq.Start = time.Now()\\n591: \\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\\n592: \\t\\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\\n593: \\t}\\n594: }\\n595: \\n596: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\\n597: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\\n598: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\\n599: \\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\\n600: \\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\\n601: }\\n602: \\n603: func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\\n604: \\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\\n605: \\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\\n606: \\n607: \\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\\n608: \\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\\\", conID)\\n609: \\t\\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\\n610: \\t} else {\\n611: \\t\\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\\n612: \\t\\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\\n613: \\t}\\n614: }\\n615: \\n616: func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\\n617: \\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\\n618: }\\n619: \\n620: // watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\\n621: // watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\\n622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\\n623: \\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\\n624: \\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\\n625: \\t// first add all known types, in order\\n626: \\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\\n627: \\t\\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\\n628: \\t\\t\\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\\n629: \\t\\t}\\n630: \\t}\\n631: \\t// Then add any undeclared types\\n632: \\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\\n633: \\t\\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\\n634: \\t\\t\\tordered = append(ordered, res)\\n635: \\t\\t}\\n636: \\t}\\n637: \\treturn ordered\\n638: }\\n639: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"fmt\"\n19: \t\"strconv\"\n20: \t\"strings\"\n21: \t\"sync/atomic\"\n22: \t\"time\"\n23: \n24: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n25: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n26: \tuatomic \"go.uber.org/atomic\"\n27: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n28: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/peer\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n30: \n31: \t\"istio.io/api/label\"\n32: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n33: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n34: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n35: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n36: \tlabelutil \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/label\"\n37: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n38: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n39: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n40: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n41: \tpm \"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\"\n42: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n43: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n44: )\n45: \n46: var (\n47: \tlog = xds.Log\n48: \n49: \t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\n50: \tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\n51: )\n52: \n53: // Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\n54: var firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n55: \n56: var knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n57: \t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n58: \n59: // DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\n60: type DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n61: \n62: // DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\n63: type DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\n64: \n65: // DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\n66: type DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n67: \n68: // DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\n69: type DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n70: \n71: type Connection struct {\n72: \txds.Connection\n73: \n74: \t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n75: \t// This is included in internal events.\n76: \tnode *core.Node\n77: \n78: \t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n79: \tproxy *model.Proxy\n80: \n81: \t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n82: \tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n83: \n84: \tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n85: \n86: \ts   *DiscoveryServer\n87: \tids []string\n88: }\n89: \n90: func (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n91: \treturn &conn.Connection\n92: }\n93: \n94: func (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n95: \treturn conn.proxy\n96: }\n97: \n98: // Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\n99: type Event struct {\n100: \t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n101: \tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n102: \n103: \t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n104: \tdone func()\n105: }\n106: \n107: func newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n108: \treturn &Connection{\n109: \t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n110: \t}\n111: }\n112: \n113: func (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n114: \treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (conn *Connection) Close() {\n118: \tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n119: }\n120: \n121: func (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n122: \treturn conn.proxy\n123: }\n124: \n125: func (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n126: \treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n127: }\n128: \n129: func (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n130: \tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n131: \terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n132: \tpushEv.done()\n133: \treturn err\n134: }\n135: \n136: // processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n137: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n138: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\n139: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n140: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n141: \tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n142: \t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n143: \tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n144: \t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n145: \t\treturn nil\n146: \t}\n147: \n148: \t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n149: \tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n150: \t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n151: \t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n152: \t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n153: \t}\n154: \n155: \tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n156: \tif !shouldRespond {\n157: \t\treturn nil\n158: \t}\n159: \n160: \trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n161: \t\tFull:   true,\n162: \t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n163: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n164: \n165: \t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n166: \t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n167: \t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n168: \t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n169: \t\tDelta:  delta,\n170: \t\tForced: true,\n171: \t}\n172: \n173: \t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n174: \t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n175: \t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n176: \t// computation.\n177: \tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n178: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n179: \t}\n180: \treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n181: }\n182: \n183: // StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\n184: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n185: \treturn s.Stream(stream)\n186: }\n187: \n188: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n189: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n190: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n191: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n192: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n193: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n194: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n195: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n196: \t}\n197: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n198: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n199: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n200: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n201: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n202: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n203: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n204: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n205: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n206: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n207: \t}\n208: \n209: \tctx := stream.Context()\n210: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n211: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n212: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n213: \t}\n214: \n215: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n216: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n217: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n218: \t}\n219: \n220: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n221: \tif err != nil {\n222: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n223: \t}\n224: \tif ids != nil {\n225: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n226: \t} else {\n227: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n228: \t}\n229: \n230: \t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n231: \ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n232: \tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n233: \tcon.ids = ids\n234: \tcon.s = s\n235: \treturn xds.Stream(con)\n236: }\n237: \n238: // update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n239: // to the tracking map.\n240: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n241: \t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n242: \tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n243: \tif err != nil {\n244: \t\treturn err\n245: \t}\n246: \t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n247: \tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n248: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n249: \t}\n250: \t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n251: \t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n252: \tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n253: \t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n254: \tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n255: \tcon.node = node\n256: \tcon.proxy = proxy\n257: \tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n258: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n259: \t}\n260: \n261: \t// Authorize xds clients\n262: \tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n263: \t\treturn err\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n267: \t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n268: \t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n269: \t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n270: \t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n271: \ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n272: \t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n273: \t// proxy\n274: \tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n275: \n276: \t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n277: \tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n278: \t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n279: \t\treturn err\n280: \t}\n281: \treturn nil\n282: }\n283: \n284: func (s *DiscoveryServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n285: \tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n286: \t\treturn\n287: \t}\n288: \ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n289: \ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n290: }\n291: \n292: func connectionID(node string) string {\n293: \tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n294: \treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n295: }\n296: \n297: // initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n298: // initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n299: // fully initialize the proxy.\n300: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n301: \tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n302: \tif err != nil {\n303: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n304: \t}\n305: \tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n306: \tif err != nil {\n307: \t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n308: \t}\n309: \t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n310: \tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n311: \tproxy.XdsNode = node\n312: \treturn proxy, nil\n313: }\n314: \n315: // setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n316: // must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\n317: func setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n318: \t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n319: \t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n320: \t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n321: \t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n322: \tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n323: \tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n324: \t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n325: \t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n326: \t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n327: \t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n328: \t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n329: \t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n330: \t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n331: \t\t}\n332: \t}\n333: \t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n334: \tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n335: \t\tproxy.Labels,\n336: \t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n337: \t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n338: \t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n339: \t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n340: \t)\n341: }\n342: \n343: func localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n344: \tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n345: \tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n346: \tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n347: \tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n348: \t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n349: \t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n350: \t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n351: \t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n352: \t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n353: \t\t}\n354: \t\treturn nil\n355: \t}\n356: \treturn &core.Locality{\n357: \t\tRegion:  region,\n358: \t\tZone:    zone,\n359: \t\tSubZone: subzone,\n360: \t}\n361: }\n362: \n363: // initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n364: // initProxyMetadata.\n365: func (s *DiscoveryServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n366: \tproxy := con.proxy\n367: \t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n368: \t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n369: \tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n370: \t\treturn err\n371: \t}\n372: \ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n373: \t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n374: \tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n375: \n376: \tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n377: \t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n378: \tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n379: \t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n380: \t}\n381: \n382: \treturn nil\n383: }\n384: \n385: func (s *DiscoveryServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n386: \tproxy.Lock()\n387: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n388: \tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n389: \t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n390: \t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n391: \tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n392: \t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n393: \t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n394: \t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n395: \t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n396: \t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n397: \t}\n398: \n399: \t// only recompute workload labels when\n400: \t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n401: \t// 2. proxy update\n402: \trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n403: \tif recomputeLabels {\n404: \t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n405: \t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n406: \t}\n407: \t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n408: \t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n409: \t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n410: \t// applicable to this proxy\n411: \tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n412: \tif request == nil {\n413: \t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n414: \t} else {\n415: \t\tpush = request.Push\n416: \t\tif request.Forced {\n417: \t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n420: \t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n421: \t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n422: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n423: \t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n424: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n425: \t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n426: \t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n427: \t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n428: \t\t\t}\n429: \t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n430: \t\t\t\tbreak\n431: \t\t\t}\n432: \t\t}\n433: \t}\n434: \t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n435: \tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n436: \t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n437: \t}\n438: \t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n439: \tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n440: \t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n441: \t}\n442: \tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n443: \tif request != nil {\n444: \t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n445: \t}\n446: }\n447: \n448: // handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\n449: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n450: \tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n451: \t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n452: \t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n453: \t\tif !event.Healthy {\n454: \t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n455: \t\t}\n456: \t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n457: \t}\n458: }\n459: \n460: // DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n461: // Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n462: // This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n463: // pattern.\n464: //\n465: // The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n466: // list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\n467: func (s *DiscoveryServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n468: \treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n469: }\n470: \n471: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\n472: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n473: \tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n474: \n475: \tif pushRequest.Full {\n476: \t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n477: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n478: \t}\n479: \n480: \tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n481: \tif !needsPush {\n482: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n483: \t\treturn nil\n484: \t}\n485: \n486: \t// Send pushes to all generators\n487: \t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n488: \twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n489: \tfor _, w := range wrl {\n490: \t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n491: \t\t\treturn err\n492: \t\t}\n493: \t}\n494: \tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n495: \treturn nil\n496: }\n497: \n498: // PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n499: // order after the types listed here\n500: var PushOrder = []string{\n501: \tv3.ClusterType,\n502: \tv3.EndpointType,\n503: \tv3.ListenerType,\n504: \tv3.RouteType,\n505: \tv3.SecretType,\n506: \tv3.AddressType,\n507: \tv3.WorkloadType,\n508: \tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n509: }\n510: \n511: // KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\n512: var KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n513: \n514: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsClientCount() int {\n515: \ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n516: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n517: \treturn len(s.adsClients)\n518: }\n519: \n520: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n521: \tvar connection *Connection\n522: \n523: \tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n524: \t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n525: \t\t\tconnection = v\n526: \t\t\tbreak\n527: \t\t}\n528: \t}\n529: \n530: \t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n531: \tif connection == nil {\n532: \t\treturn\n533: \t}\n534: \tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n535: \t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n536: \t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n537: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n538: \t\t}\n539: \t}\n540: \n541: \ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n542: \t\tFull:   true,\n543: \t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n544: \t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n545: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n546: \t\tForced: true,\n547: \t})\n548: }\n549: \n550: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n551: // Mainly used in Debug interface.\n552: func AdsPushAll(s *DiscoveryServer) {\n553: \ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n554: \t\tFull:   true,\n555: \t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n556: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n557: \t\tForced: true,\n558: \t})\n559: }\n560: \n561: // AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\n562: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n563: \tif !req.Full {\n564: \t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n565: \t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n566: \t} else {\n567: \t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n568: \t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n569: \t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n570: \t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n571: \n572: \t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n573: \t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n574: \t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n575: \t\t}\n576: \t}\n577: \n578: \ts.StartPush(req)\n579: }\n580: \n581: // Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\n582: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n583: \t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n584: \tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n585: \t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n586: \t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n587: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n588: \t\t}\n589: \t}\n590: \treq.Start = time.Now()\n591: \tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n592: \t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n593: \t}\n594: }\n595: \n596: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n597: \ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n598: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n599: \ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n600: \trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n601: }\n602: \n603: func (s *DiscoveryServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n604: \ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n605: \tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n606: \n607: \tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n608: \t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n609: \t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n610: \t} else {\n611: \t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n612: \t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n613: \t}\n614: }\n615: \n616: func (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n617: \treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n618: }\n619: \n620: // watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n621: // watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\n622: func (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n623: \tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n624: \tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n625: \t// first add all known types, in order\n626: \tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n627: \t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n628: \t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n629: \t\t}\n630: \t}\n631: \t// Then add any undeclared types\n632: \tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n633: \t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n634: \t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n635: \t\t}\n636: \t}\n637: \treturn ordered\n638: }\n639: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/ads.go"
  }
}
36. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n20: \\n21: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\\\"\\n28: )\\n29: \\n30: // authenticate authenticates the ADS request using the configured authenticators.\\n31: // Returns the validated principals or an error.\\n32: // If no authenticators are configured, or if the request is on a non-secure\\n33: // stream ( 15010 ) - returns an empty list of principals and no errors.\\n34: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\\n35: \\tc, err := security.Authenticate(ctx, s.Authenticators)\\n36: \\tif c != nil {\\n37: \\t\\treturn c.Identities, nil\\n38: \\t}\\n39: \\treturn nil, err\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\\n43: \\tif con == nil || con.proxy == nil {\\n44: \\t\\treturn nil\\n45: \\t}\\n46: \\n47: \\tif features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck && identities != nil {\\n48: \\t\\t// TODO: allow locking down, rejecting unauthenticated requests.\\n49: \\t\\tid, err := checkConnectionIdentity(con.proxy, identities)\\n50: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n51: \\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized XDS: %v with identity %v: %v\\\", con.Peer(), identities, err)\\n52: \\t\\t\\treturn status.Newf(codes.PermissionDenied, \\\"authorization failed: %v\\\", err).Err()\\n53: \\t\\t}\\n54: \\t\\tcon.proxy.VerifiedIdentity = id\\n55: \\t}\\n56: \\treturn nil\\n57: }\\n58: \\n59: func checkConnectionIdentity(proxy *model.Proxy, identities []string) (*spiffe.Identity, error) {\\n60: \\tfor _, rawID := range identities {\\n61: \\t\\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(rawID)\\n62: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n63: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n64: \\t\\t}\\n65: \\t\\tif proxy.ConfigNamespace != \\\"\\\" && spiffeID.Namespace != proxy.ConfigNamespace {\\n66: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n67: \\t\\t}\\n68: \\t\\tif proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount != \\\"\\\" && spiffeID.ServiceAccount != proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount {\\n69: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t\\treturn &spiffeID, nil\\n72: \\t}\\n73: \\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"no identities (%v) matched %v/%v\\\", identities, proxy.ConfigNamespace, proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount)\\n74: }\\n75: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"context\"\n19: \t\"fmt\"\n20: \n21: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n22: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n23: \n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n25: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/security\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/spiffe\"\n28: )\n29: \n30: // authenticate authenticates the ADS request using the configured authenticators.\n31: // Returns the validated principals or an error.\n32: // If no authenticators are configured, or if the request is on a non-secure\n33: // stream ( 15010 ) - returns an empty list of principals and no errors.\n34: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authenticate(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {\n35: \tc, err := security.Authenticate(ctx, s.Authenticators)\n36: \tif c != nil {\n37: \t\treturn c.Identities, nil\n38: \t}\n39: \treturn nil, err\n40: }\n41: \n42: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorize(con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n43: \tif con == nil || con.proxy == nil {\n44: \t\treturn nil\n45: \t}\n46: \n47: \tif features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck && identities != nil {\n48: \t\t// TODO: allow locking down, rejecting unauthenticated requests.\n49: \t\tid, err := checkConnectionIdentity(con.proxy, identities)\n50: \t\tif err != nil {\n51: \t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized XDS: %v with identity %v: %v\", con.Peer(), identities, err)\n52: \t\t\treturn status.Newf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"authorization failed: %v\", err).Err()\n53: \t\t}\n54: \t\tcon.proxy.VerifiedIdentity = id\n55: \t}\n56: \treturn nil\n57: }\n58: \n59: func checkConnectionIdentity(proxy *model.Proxy, identities []string) (*spiffe.Identity, error) {\n60: \tfor _, rawID := range identities {\n61: \t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(rawID)\n62: \t\tif err != nil {\n63: \t\t\tcontinue\n64: \t\t}\n65: \t\tif proxy.ConfigNamespace != \"\" && spiffeID.Namespace != proxy.ConfigNamespace {\n66: \t\t\tcontinue\n67: \t\t}\n68: \t\tif proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount != \"\" && spiffeID.ServiceAccount != proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount {\n69: \t\t\tcontinue\n70: \t\t}\n71: \t\treturn &spiffeID, nil\n72: \t}\n73: \treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"no identities (%v) matched %v/%v\", identities, proxy.ConfigNamespace, proxy.Metadata.ServiceAccount)\n74: }\n75: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/auth.go"
  }
}

License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\\\"\\n27: \\tanypb \\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\\\"\\n28: \\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n30: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n31: )\\n32: \\n33: var activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\\n34: \\t\\\"config_dump\\\": {},\\n35: \\t\\\"ndsz\\\":        {},\\n36: \\t\\\"edsz\\\":        {},\\n37: }\\n38: \\n39: // DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\\n40: type DebugGen struct {\\n41: \\tServer          *DiscoveryServer\\n42: \\tSystemNamespace string\\n43: \\tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: type ResponseCapture struct {\\n47: \\tbody        *bytes.Buffer\\n48: \\theader      map[string]string\\n49: \\twroteHeader bool\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: func (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\\n53: \\theader := make(http.Header)\\n54: \\tfor k, v := range r.header {\\n55: \\t\\theader.Set(k, v)\\n56: \\t}\\n57: \\treturn header\\n58: }\\n59: \\n60: func (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\\n61: \\treturn r.body.Write(i)\\n62: }\\n63: \\n64: func (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\\n65: \\tr.header[\\\"statusCode\\\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: func NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\\n69: \\treturn &ResponseCapture{\\n70: \\t\\theader:      make(map[string]string),\\n71: \\t\\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\\n72: \\t\\twroteHeader: false,\\n73: \\t}\\n74: }\\n75: \\n76: func NewDebugGen(s *DiscoveryServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\\n77: \\treturn &DebugGen{\\n78: \\t\\tServer:          s,\\n79: \\t\\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\\n80: \\t\\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\\n81: \\t}\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\\n85: func (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n86: \\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\\n87: \\t\\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\\n88: \\t}\\n89: \\n90: \\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\\n91: \\tif err != nil {\\n92: \\t\\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\\n93: \\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\\n96: \\n97: \\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\\n98: \\t\\tName: resourceName,\\n99: \\t\\tResource: &anypb.Any{\\n100: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\\n101: \\t\\t\\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\\n102: \\t\\t},\\n103: \\t}}\\n104: \\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\\n105: }\\n106: \\n107: // GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\\n108: func (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\\n109: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n110: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n111: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n112: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n113: \\tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\\n114: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\\n115: \\t}\\n116: \\n117: \\tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\\n118: \\tif err != nil {\\n119: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\\n120: \\t}\\n121: \\n122: \\tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\\n123: \\n124: \\tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\\n125: \\t\\tName: resourceName,\\n126: \\t\\tResource: &anypb.Any{\\n127: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\\n128: \\t\\t\\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\\n129: \\t\\t},\\n130: \\t}}\\n131: \\treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\\n132: }\\n133: \\n134: func validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\\n135: \\tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\\n136: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\\\", proxy.ID)\\n137: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \\\"authentication required\\\")\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\\n140: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \\\"exactly one debug request is required\\\")\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\treturn nil\\n143: }\\n144: \\n145: func parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\\n146: \\tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\\n147: \\tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\\n148: \\tdebugType := u.Path\\n149: \\tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\\n150: \\tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\\n151: \\t\\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\\n152: \\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \\\"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\\\", identity)\\n153: \\t\\t}\\n154: \\t}\\n155: \\treturn resourceName, nil\\n156: }\\n157: \\n158: func processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\\n159: \\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\\n160: \\tdebugURL := \\\"/debug/\\\" + resourceName\\n161: \\threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\\n162: \\thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\\n163: \\tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\\n164: \\thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\\n165: \\tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\\n166: \\t\\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\\n167: \\t\\tbuffer.Write(header)\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\\n170: \\treturn buffer\\n171: }\\n172: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debuggen.go\"}",

    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"bytes\"\n19: \t\"encoding/json\"\n20: \t\"net/http\"\n21: \t\"net/url\"\n22: \t\"strconv\"\n23: \n24: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n25: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n26: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n27: \tanypb \"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/anypb\"\n28: \n29: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n30: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n31: )\n32: \n33: var activeNamespaceDebuggers = map[string]struct{}{\n34: \t\"config_dump\": {},\n35: \t\"ndsz\":        {},\n36: \t\"edsz\":        {},\n37: }\n38: \n39: // DebugGen is a Generator for istio debug info\n40: type DebugGen struct {\n41: \tServer          *DiscoveryServer\n42: \tSystemNamespace string\n43: \tDebugMux        *http.ServeMux\n44: }\n45: \n46: type ResponseCapture struct {\n47: \tbody        *bytes.Buffer\n48: \theader      map[string]string\n49: \twroteHeader bool\n50: }\n51: \n52: func (r ResponseCapture) Header() http.Header {\n53: \theader := make(http.Header)\n54: \tfor k, v := range r.header {\n55: \t\theader.Set(k, v)\n56: \t}\n57: \treturn header\n58: }\n59: \n60: func (r ResponseCapture) Write(i []byte) (int, error) {\n61: \treturn r.body.Write(i)\n62: }\n63: \n64: func (r ResponseCapture) WriteHeader(statusCode int) {\n65: \tr.header[\"statusCode\"] = strconv.Itoa(statusCode)\n66: }\n67: \n68: func NewResponseCapture() *ResponseCapture {\n69: \treturn &ResponseCapture{\n70: \t\theader:      make(map[string]string),\n71: \t\tbody:        new(bytes.Buffer),\n72: \t\twroteHeader: false,\n73: \t}\n74: }\n75: \n76: func NewDebugGen(s *DiscoveryServer, systemNamespace string, debugMux *http.ServeMux) *DebugGen {\n77: \treturn &DebugGen{\n78: \t\tServer:          s,\n79: \t\tSystemNamespace: systemNamespace,\n80: \t\tDebugMux:        debugMux,\n81: \t}\n82: }\n83: \n84: // Generate XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n85: func (dg *DebugGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n86: \tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n87: \t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n88: \t}\n89: \n90: \tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n91: \tif err != nil {\n92: \t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, err\n93: \t}\n94: \n95: \tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n96: \n97: \tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n98: \t\tName: resourceName,\n99: \t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n100: \t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n101: \t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n102: \t\t},\n103: \t}}\n104: \treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n105: }\n106: \n107: // GenerateDeltas XDS debug responses according to the incoming debug request\n108: func (dg *DebugGen) GenerateDeltas(\n109: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n110: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n111: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n112: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n113: \tif err := validateProxyAuthentication(proxy, w); err != nil {\n114: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n115: \t}\n116: \n117: \tresourceName, err := parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy, w, dg)\n118: \tif err != nil {\n119: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, err\n120: \t}\n121: \n122: \tbuffer := processDebugRequest(dg, resourceName)\n123: \n124: \tres := model.Resources{&discovery.Resource{\n125: \t\tName: resourceName,\n126: \t\tResource: &anypb.Any{\n127: \t\t\tTypeUrl: v3.DebugType,\n128: \t\t\tValue:   buffer.Bytes(),\n129: \t\t},\n130: \t}}\n131: \treturn res, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, true, nil\n132: }\n133: \n134: func validateProxyAuthentication(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource) error {\n135: \tif proxy.VerifiedIdentity == nil {\n136: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"proxy %s is not authorized to receive debug. Ensure you are connecting over TLS port and are authenticated.\", proxy.ID)\n137: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, \"authentication required\")\n138: \t}\n139: \tif w.ResourceNames == nil || len(w.ResourceNames) != 1 {\n140: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, \"exactly one debug request is required\")\n141: \t}\n142: \treturn nil\n143: }\n144: \n145: func parseAndValidateDebugRequest(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, dg *DebugGen) (string, error) {\n146: \tresourceName := w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0]\n147: \tu, _ := url.Parse(resourceName)\n148: \tdebugType := u.Path\n149: \tidentity := proxy.VerifiedIdentity\n150: \tif identity.Namespace != dg.SystemNamespace {\n151: \t\tif _, ok := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugType]; !ok {\n152: \t\t\treturn \"\", status.Errorf(codes.PermissionDenied, \"the debug info is not available for current identity: %q\", identity)\n153: \t\t}\n154: \t}\n155: \treturn resourceName, nil\n156: }\n157: \n158: func processDebugRequest(dg *DebugGen, resourceName string) bytes.Buffer {\n159: \tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n160: \tdebugURL := \"/debug/\" + resourceName\n161: \threq, _ := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, debugURL, nil)\n162: \thandler, _ := dg.DebugMux.Handler(hreq)\n163: \tresponse := NewResponseCapture()\n164: \thandler.ServeHTTP(response, hreq)\n165: \tif response.wroteHeader && len(response.header) >= 1 {\n166: \t\theader, _ := json.Marshal(response.header)\n167: \t\tbuffer.Write(header)\n168: \t}\n169: \tbuffer.Write(response.body.Bytes())\n170: \treturn buffer\n171: }\n172: ",

  }
