Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n21: \\n22: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n28: )\\n29: \\n30: // SvcUpdate is a callback from service discovery when service info changes.\\n31: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SvcUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event model.Event) {\\n32: \\t// When a service deleted, we should cleanup the endpoint shards and also remove keys from EndpointIndex to\\n33: \\t// prevent memory leaks.\\n34: \\tif event == model.EventDelete {\\n35: \\t\\tinboundServiceDeletes.Increment()\\n36: \\t\\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteServiceShard(shard, hostname, namespace, false)\\n37: \\t} else {\\n38: \\t\\tinboundServiceUpdates.Increment()\\n39: \\t}\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: // EDSUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\\n43: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\\n44: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\\n45: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\\n46: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\\n47: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\\n48: \\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\\n49: ) {\\n50: \\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\\n51: \\t// Update the endpoint shards\\n52: \\tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\\n53: \\tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\\n54: \\t\\t// Trigger a push\\n55: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\\n56: \\t\\t\\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\\n57: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\\n58: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\\n59: \\t\\t})\\n60: \\t}\\n61: }\\n62: \\n63: // EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\\n64: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\\n65: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\\n66: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\\n67: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\\n68: //\\n69: // Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\\n70: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\\n71: \\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\\n72: ) {\\n73: \\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\\n74: \\t// Update the endpoint shards\\n75: \\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\\n76: }\\n77: \\n78: func (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\\n79: \\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\\n80: }\\n81: \\n82: // EdsGenerator implements the new Generate method for EDS, using the in-memory, optimized endpoint\\n83: // storage in DiscoveryServer.\\n84: type EdsGenerator struct {\\n85: \\tCache         model.XdsCache\\n86: \\tEndpointIndex *model.EndpointIndex\\n87: }\\n88: \\n89: var _ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &EdsGenerator{}\\n90: \\n91: // Map of all configs that do not impact EDS\\n92: var skippedEdsConfigs = sets.New(\\n93: \\tkind.Gateway,\\n94: \\tkind.VirtualService,\\n95: \\tkind.WorkloadGroup,\\n96: \\tkind.AuthorizationPolicy,\\n97: \\tkind.RequestAuthentication,\\n98: \\tkind.Secret,\\n99: \\tkind.Telemetry,\\n100: \\tkind.WasmPlugin,\\n101: \\tkind.ProxyConfig,\\n102: \\tkind.DNSName,\\n103: )\\n104: \\n105: func edsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) bool {\\n106: \\tif res, ok := xdsNeedsPush(req, proxy); ok {\\n107: \\t\\treturn res\\n108: \\t}\\n109: \\tfor config := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\\n110: \\t\\tif !skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(config.Kind) {\\n111: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n112: \\t\\t}\\n113: \\t}\\n114: \\treturn false\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (eds *EdsGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n118: \\tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\\n119: \\t\\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\\n120: \\t}\\n121: \\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\\n122: \\treturn resources, logDetails, nil\\n123: }\\n124: \\n125: func (eds *EdsGenerator) GenerateDeltas(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest,\\n126: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n127: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n128: \\tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\\n129: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\\n130: \\t}\\n131: \\tif !shouldUseDeltaEds(req) {\\n132: \\t\\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\\n133: \\t\\treturn resources, nil, logDetails, false, nil\\n134: \\t}\\n135: \\n136: \\tresources, removed, logs := eds.buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\\n137: \\treturn resources, removed, logs, true, nil\\n138: }\\n139: \\n140: func shouldUseDeltaEds(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\\n141: \\tif !req.Full {\\n142: \\t\\treturn false\\n143: \\t}\\n144: \\treturn canSendPartialFullPushes(req)\\n145: }\\n146: \\n147: // canSendPartialFullPushes checks if a request contains *only* endpoints updates except `skippedEdsConfigs`.\\n148: // This allows us to perform more efficient pushes where we only update the endpoints that did change.\\n149: func canSendPartialFullPushes(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\\n150: \\t// If we don't know what configs are updated, just send a full push\\n151: \\tif req.Forced {\\n152: \\t\\treturn false\\n153: \\t}\\n154: \\tfor cfg := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\\n155: \\t\\tif skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(cfg.Kind) {\\n156: \\t\\t\\t// the updated config does not impact EDS, skip it\\n157: \\t\\t\\t// this happens when push requests are merged due to debounce\\n158: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n159: \\t\\t}\\n160: \\t\\tif cfg.Kind != kind.ServiceEntry {\\n161: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n162: \\t\\t}\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\treturn true\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: func (eds *EdsGenerator) buildEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\\n168: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n169: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n170: ) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails) {\\n171: \\tvar edsUpdatedServices map[string]struct{}\\n172: \\t// canSendPartialFullPushes determines if we can send a partial push (ie a subset of known CLAs).\\n173: \\t// This is safe when only Services has changed, as this implies that only the CLAs for the\\n174: \\t// associated Service changed. Note when a multi-network Service changes it triggers a push with\\n175: \\t// ConfigsUpdated=ALL, so in this case we would not enable a partial push.\\n176: \\t// Despite this code existing on the SotW code path, sending these partial pushes is still allowed;\\n177: \\t// see https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#grouping-resources-into-responses\\n178: \\tif !req.Full || canSendPartialFullPushes(req) {\\n179: \\t\\tedsUpdatedServices = model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\\n180: \\t}\\n181: \\tvar resources model.Resources\\n182: \\tempty := 0\\n183: \\tcached := 0\\n184: \\tregenerated := 0\\n185: \\tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\\n186: \\t\\tif edsUpdatedServices != nil {\\n187: \\t\\t\\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\\n188: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Cluster was not updated, skip recomputing. This happens when we get an incremental update for a\\n189: \\t\\t\\t\\t// specific Hostname. On connect or for full push edsUpdatedServices will be empty.\\n190: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n191: \\t\\t\\t}\\n192: \\t\\t}\\n193: \\t\\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\\n194: \\n195: \\t\\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\\n196: \\t\\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n197: \\t\\t\\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\\n198: \\t\\t\\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\\n200: \\t\\t\\t\\tcached++\\n201: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n202: \\t\\t\\t}\\n203: \\t\\t}\\n204: \\n205: \\t\\t// generate eds from beginning\\n206: \\t\\t{\\n207: \\t\\t\\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\\n208: \\t\\t\\tif l == nil {\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n210: \\t\\t\\t}\\n211: \\t\\t\\tregenerated++\\n212: \\n213: \\t\\t\\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\tempty++\\n215: \\t\\t\\t}\\n216: \\t\\t\\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\\n217: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     l.ClusterName,\\n218: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\\n219: \\t\\t\\t}\\n220: \\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, resource)\\n221: \\t\\t\\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\\n222: \\t\\t}\\n223: \\t}\\n224: \\treturn resources, model.XdsLogDetails{\\n225: \\t\\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\\n226: \\t\\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\\\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\\\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\\n227: \\t}\\n228: }\\n229: \\n230: // TODO(@hzxuzhonghu): merge with buildEndpoints\\n231: func (eds *EdsGenerator) buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\\n232: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n233: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n234: ) (model.Resources, []string, model.XdsLogDetails) {\\n235: \\tedsUpdatedServices := model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\\n236: \\tvar resources model.Resources\\n237: \\tvar removed []string\\n238: \\tempty := 0\\n239: \\tcached := 0\\n240: \\tregenerated := 0\\n241: \\n242: \\tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\\n243: \\t\\t// filter out eds that are not updated for clusters\\n244: \\t\\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\\n245: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n246: \\t\\t}\\n247: \\n248: \\t\\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\\n249: \\t\\t// if a service is not found, it means the cluster is removed\\n250: \\t\\tif !builder.ServiceFound() {\\n251: \\t\\t\\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\\n252: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n253: \\t\\t}\\n254: \\n255: \\t\\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\\n256: \\t\\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\\n258: \\t\\t\\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\\n259: \\t\\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\tcached++\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n262: \\t\\t\\t}\\n263: \\t\\t}\\n264: \\t\\t// generate new eds cache\\n265: \\t\\t{\\n266: \\t\\t\\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\\n267: \\t\\t\\tif l == nil {\\n268: \\t\\t\\t\\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\\n269: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n270: \\t\\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t\\tregenerated++\\n272: \\t\\t\\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\\n273: \\t\\t\\t\\tempty++\\n274: \\t\\t\\t}\\n275: \\t\\t\\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     l.ClusterName,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, resource)\\n280: \\t\\t\\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\\n281: \\t\\t}\\n282: \\t}\\n283: \\treturn resources, removed, model.XdsLogDetails{\\n284: \\t\\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\\n285: \\t\\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\\\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\\\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\\n286: \\t}\\n287: }\\n288: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/eds.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"fmt\"\n19: \n20: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n21: \n22: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n23: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n25: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\"\n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n28: )\n29: \n30: // SvcUpdate is a callback from service discovery when service info changes.\n31: func (s *DiscoveryServer) SvcUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, hostname string, namespace string, event model.Event) {\n32: \t// When a service deleted, we should cleanup the endpoint shards and also remove keys from EndpointIndex to\n33: \t// prevent memory leaks.\n34: \tif event == model.EventDelete {\n35: \t\tinboundServiceDeletes.Increment()\n36: \t\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteServiceShard(shard, hostname, namespace, false)\n37: \t} else {\n38: \t\tinboundServiceUpdates.Increment()\n39: \t}\n40: }\n41: \n42: // EDSUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n43: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\n44: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n45: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n46: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n47: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n48: \tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n49: ) {\n50: \tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n51: \t// Update the endpoint shards\n52: \tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\n53: \tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\n54: \t\t// Trigger a push\n55: \t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n56: \t\t\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\n57: \t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\n58: \t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\n59: \t\t})\n60: \t}\n61: }\n62: \n63: // EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n64: // This is the main method implementing EDS.\n65: // It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n66: // the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n67: // on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n68: //\n69: // Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\n70: func (s *DiscoveryServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n71: \tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n72: ) {\n73: \tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n74: \t// Update the endpoint shards\n75: \ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\n76: }\n77: \n78: func (s *DiscoveryServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\n79: \ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\n80: }\n81: \n82: // EdsGenerator implements the new Generate method for EDS, using the in-memory, optimized endpoint\n83: // storage in DiscoveryServer.\n84: type EdsGenerator struct {\n85: \tCache         model.XdsCache\n86: \tEndpointIndex *model.EndpointIndex\n87: }\n88: \n89: var _ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &EdsGenerator{}\n90: \n91: // Map of all configs that do not impact EDS\n92: var skippedEdsConfigs = sets.New(\n93: \tkind.Gateway,\n94: \tkind.VirtualService,\n95: \tkind.WorkloadGroup,\n96: \tkind.AuthorizationPolicy,\n97: \tkind.RequestAuthentication,\n98: \tkind.Secret,\n99: \tkind.Telemetry,\n100: \tkind.WasmPlugin,\n101: \tkind.ProxyConfig,\n102: \tkind.DNSName,\n103: )\n104: \n105: func edsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) bool {\n106: \tif res, ok := xdsNeedsPush(req, proxy); ok {\n107: \t\treturn res\n108: \t}\n109: \tfor config := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n110: \t\tif !skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(config.Kind) {\n111: \t\t\treturn true\n112: \t\t}\n113: \t}\n114: \treturn false\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (eds *EdsGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n118: \tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\n119: \t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n120: \t}\n121: \tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n122: \treturn resources, logDetails, nil\n123: }\n124: \n125: func (eds *EdsGenerator) GenerateDeltas(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest,\n126: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n127: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n128: \tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\n129: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\n130: \t}\n131: \tif !shouldUseDeltaEds(req) {\n132: \t\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n133: \t\treturn resources, nil, logDetails, false, nil\n134: \t}\n135: \n136: \tresources, removed, logs := eds.buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n137: \treturn resources, removed, logs, true, nil\n138: }\n139: \n140: func shouldUseDeltaEds(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n141: \tif !req.Full {\n142: \t\treturn false\n143: \t}\n144: \treturn canSendPartialFullPushes(req)\n145: }\n146: \n147: // canSendPartialFullPushes checks if a request contains *only* endpoints updates except `skippedEdsConfigs`.\n148: // This allows us to perform more efficient pushes where we only update the endpoints that did change.\n149: func canSendPartialFullPushes(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n150: \t// If we don't know what configs are updated, just send a full push\n151: \tif req.Forced {\n152: \t\treturn false\n153: \t}\n154: \tfor cfg := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n155: \t\tif skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(cfg.Kind) {\n156: \t\t\t// the updated config does not impact EDS, skip it\n157: \t\t\t// this happens when push requests are merged due to debounce\n158: \t\t\tcontinue\n159: \t\t}\n160: \t\tif cfg.Kind != kind.ServiceEntry {\n161: \t\t\treturn false\n162: \t\t}\n163: \t}\n164: \treturn true\n165: }\n166: \n167: func (eds *EdsGenerator) buildEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\n168: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n169: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n170: ) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails) {\n171: \tvar edsUpdatedServices map[string]struct{}\n172: \t// canSendPartialFullPushes determines if we can send a partial push (ie a subset of known CLAs).\n173: \t// This is safe when only Services has changed, as this implies that only the CLAs for the\n174: \t// associated Service changed. Note when a multi-network Service changes it triggers a push with\n175: \t// ConfigsUpdated=ALL, so in this case we would not enable a partial push.\n176: \t// Despite this code existing on the SotW code path, sending these partial pushes is still allowed;\n177: \t// see https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#grouping-resources-into-responses\n178: \tif !req.Full || canSendPartialFullPushes(req) {\n179: \t\tedsUpdatedServices = model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\n180: \t}\n181: \tvar resources model.Resources\n182: \tempty := 0\n183: \tcached := 0\n184: \tregenerated := 0\n185: \tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\n186: \t\tif edsUpdatedServices != nil {\n187: \t\t\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\n188: \t\t\t\t// Cluster was not updated, skip recomputing. This happens when we get an incremental update for a\n189: \t\t\t\t// specific Hostname. On connect or for full push edsUpdatedServices will be empty.\n190: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n191: \t\t\t}\n192: \t\t}\n193: \t\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\n194: \n195: \t\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\n196: \t\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n197: \t\t\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\n198: \t\t\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\n199: \t\t\t\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\n200: \t\t\t\tcached++\n201: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n202: \t\t\t}\n203: \t\t}\n204: \n205: \t\t// generate eds from beginning\n206: \t\t{\n207: \t\t\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\n208: \t\t\tif l == nil {\n209: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n210: \t\t\t}\n211: \t\t\tregenerated++\n212: \n213: \t\t\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\n214: \t\t\t\tempty++\n215: \t\t\t}\n216: \t\t\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\n217: \t\t\t\tName:     l.ClusterName,\n218: \t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\n219: \t\t\t}\n220: \t\t\tresources = append(resources, resource)\n221: \t\t\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\n222: \t\t}\n223: \t}\n224: \treturn resources, model.XdsLogDetails{\n225: \t\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\n226: \t\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\n227: \t}\n228: }\n229: \n230: // TODO(@hzxuzhonghu): merge with buildEndpoints\n231: func (eds *EdsGenerator) buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\n232: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n233: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n234: ) (model.Resources, []string, model.XdsLogDetails) {\n235: \tedsUpdatedServices := model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\n236: \tvar resources model.Resources\n237: \tvar removed []string\n238: \tempty := 0\n239: \tcached := 0\n240: \tregenerated := 0\n241: \n242: \tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\n243: \t\t// filter out eds that are not updated for clusters\n244: \t\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\n245: \t\t\tcontinue\n246: \t\t}\n247: \n248: \t\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\n249: \t\t// if a service is not found, it means the cluster is removed\n250: \t\tif !builder.ServiceFound() {\n251: \t\t\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\n252: \t\t\tcontinue\n253: \t\t}\n254: \n255: \t\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\n256: \t\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n257: \t\t\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\n258: \t\t\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\n259: \t\t\t\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\n260: \t\t\t\tcached++\n261: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n262: \t\t\t}\n263: \t\t}\n264: \t\t// generate new eds cache\n265: \t\t{\n266: \t\t\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\n267: \t\t\tif l == nil {\n268: \t\t\t\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\n269: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n270: \t\t\t}\n271: \t\t\tregenerated++\n272: \t\t\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\n273: \t\t\t\tempty++\n274: \t\t\t}\n275: \t\t\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\n276: \t\t\t\tName:     l.ClusterName,\n277: \t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\n278: \t\t\t}\n279: \t\t\tresources = append(resources, resource)\n280: \t\t\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\n281: \t\t}\n282: \t}\n283: \treturn resources, removed, model.XdsLogDetails{\n284: \t\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\n285: \t\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\n286: \t}\n287: }\n288: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/eds.go"
  }
}
41. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pilot/pkg/xds/statusgen.go",
