Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\tadmin \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/admin/v3\\\"\\n21: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n22: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n23: \\tstatus \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/status/v3\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n28: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n30: )\\n31: \\n32: const (\\n33: \\tTypeDebugPrefix = v3.DebugType + \\\"/\\\"\\n34: \\n35: \\t// TypeDebugSyncronization requests Envoy CSDS for proxy sync status\\n36: \\tTypeDebugSyncronization = v3.DebugType + \\\"/syncz\\\"\\n37: \\n38: \\t// TypeDebugConfigDump requests Envoy configuration for a proxy without creating one\\n39: \\tTypeDebugConfigDump = v3.DebugType + \\\"/config_dump\\\"\\n40: \\n41: \\t// TODO: TypeURLReady - readiness events for endpoints, agent can propagate\\n42: )\\n43: \\n44: // StatusGen is a Generator for XDS status: connections, syncz, configdump\\n45: type StatusGen struct {\\n46: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n47: \\n48: \\t// TODO: track last N Nacks and connection events, with 'version' based on timestamp.\\n49: \\t// On new connect, use version to send recent events since last update.\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: func NewStatusGen(s *DiscoveryServer) *StatusGen {\\n53: \\treturn &StatusGen{\\n54: \\t\\tServer: s,\\n55: \\t}\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: // Generate XDS responses about internal events:\\n59: // - connection status\\n60: // - NACKs\\n61: // We can also expose ACKS.\\n62: func (sg *StatusGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n63: \\treturn sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\\n64: }\\n65: \\n66: // Generate delta XDS responses about internal events:\\n67: // - connection status\\n68: // - NACKs\\n69: // We can also expose ACKS.\\n70: func (sg *StatusGen) GenerateDeltas(\\n71: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n72: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n73: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n74: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n75: \\tres, detail, err := sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\\n76: \\treturn res, nil, detail, true, err\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: func (sg *StatusGen) handleInternalRequest(_ *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, _ *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n80: \\tres := model.Resources{}\\n81: \\n82: \\tswitch w.TypeUrl {\\n83: \\tcase TypeDebugSyncronization:\\n84: \\t\\tres = sg.debugSyncz()\\n85: \\tcase TypeDebugConfigDump:\\n86: \\t\\tif len(w.ResourceNames) == 0 || len(w.ResourceNames) > 1 {\\n87: \\t\\t\\t// Malformed request from client\\n88: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"%s with %d ResourceNames\\\", TypeDebugConfigDump, len(w.ResourceNames))\\n89: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n90: \\t\\t}\\n91: \\t\\tvar err error\\n92: \\t\\tdumpRes, err := sg.debugConfigDump(w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0])\\n93: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"%s failed: %v\\\", TypeDebugConfigDump, err)\\n95: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n96: \\t\\t}\\n97: \\t\\tres = dumpRes\\n98: \\t}\\n99: \\treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\\n100: }\\n101: \\n102: // isSidecar ad-hoc method to see if connection represents a sidecar\\n103: func isProxy(con *Connection) bool {\\n104: \\treturn con != nil &&\\n105: \\t\\tcon.proxy != nil &&\\n106: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\\n107: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata.ProxyConfig != nil\\n108: }\\n109: \\n110: func isZtunnel(con *Connection) bool {\\n111: \\treturn con != nil &&\\n112: \\t\\tcon.proxy != nil &&\\n113: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\\n114: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: func (sg *StatusGen) debugSyncz() model.Resources {\\n118: \\tres := model.Resources{}\\n119: \\n120: \\tfor _, con := range sg.Server.Clients() {\\n121: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RLock()\\n122: \\t\\t// Skip \\\"nodes\\\" without metadata (they are probably istioctl queries!)\\n123: \\t\\tif isProxy(con) || isZtunnel(con) {\\n124: \\t\\t\\txdsConfigs := make([]*status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig, 0)\\n125: \\t\\t\\twrs := con.proxy.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\\n126: \\t\\t\\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\\n127: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc := &status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig{}\\n128: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.ConfigStatus = debugSyncStatus(wr)\\n129: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.LastUpdated = timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime)\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.TypeUrl = wr.TypeUrl\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\tif wr.LastError != \\\"\\\" {\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpxc.ErrorState = &admin.UpdateFailureState{\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tLastUpdateAttempt: timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime),\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetails:           wr.LastError,\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\t\\t\\t\\txdsConfigs = append(xdsConfigs, pxc)\\n139: \\t\\t\\t}\\n140: \\t\\t\\tslices.SortBy(xdsConfigs, func(a *status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig) string {\\n141: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn a.TypeUrl\\n142: \\t\\t\\t})\\n143: \\t\\t\\tclientConfig := &status.ClientConfig{\\n144: \\t\\t\\t\\tNode: &core.Node{\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tId: con.proxy.ID,\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tMetadata: model.NodeMetadata{\\n147: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tClusterID:    con.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\\n148: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tNamespace:    con.proxy.Metadata.Namespace,\\n149: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tIstioVersion: con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion,\\n150: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}.ToStruct(),\\n151: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\tGenericXdsConfigs: xdsConfigs,\\n153: \\t\\t\\t}\\n154: \\t\\t\\tres = append(res, &discovery.Resource{\\n155: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     clientConfig.Node.Id,\\n156: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(clientConfig),\\n157: \\t\\t\\t})\\n158: \\t\\t}\\n159: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\\n160: \\t}\\n161: \\n162: \\treturn res\\n163: }\\n164: \\n165: func debugSyncStatus(wr model.WatchedResource) status.ConfigStatus {\\n166: \\tif wr.LastError != \\\"\\\" {\\n167: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_ERROR\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tif wr.NonceSent == \\\"\\\" {\\n170: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_NOT_SENT\\n171: \\t}\\n172: \\tif wr.NonceAcked == wr.NonceSent {\\n173: \\t\\treturn status.ConfigStatus_SYNCED\\n174: \\t}\\n175: \\treturn status.ConfigStatus_STALE\\n176: }\\n177: \\n178: func (sg *StatusGen) debugConfigDump(proxyID string) (model.Resources, error) {\\n179: \\tconn := sg.Server.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\\n180: \\tif conn == nil {\\n181: \\t\\t// This is \\\"like\\\" a 404.  The error is the client's.  However, this endpoint\\n182: \\t\\t// only tracks a single \\\"shard\\\" of connections.  The client may try another instance.\\n183: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"config dump could not find connection for proxyID %q\\\", proxyID)\\n184: \\t}\\n185: \\n186: \\tdump, err := sg.Server.connectionConfigDump(conn, false)\\n187: \\tif err != nil {\\n188: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n189: \\t}\\n190: \\n191: \\treturn model.AnyToUnnamedResources(dump.Configs), nil\\n192: }\\n193: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/statusgen.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"fmt\"\n19: \n20: \tadmin \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/admin/v3\"\n21: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n22: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n23: \tstatus \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/status/v3\"\n24: \t\"google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/timestamppb\"\n25: \n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n28: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n29: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n30: )\n31: \n32: const (\n33: \tTypeDebugPrefix = v3.DebugType + \"/\"\n34: \n35: \t// TypeDebugSyncronization requests Envoy CSDS for proxy sync status\n36: \tTypeDebugSyncronization = v3.DebugType + \"/syncz\"\n37: \n38: \t// TypeDebugConfigDump requests Envoy configuration for a proxy without creating one\n39: \tTypeDebugConfigDump = v3.DebugType + \"/config_dump\"\n40: \n41: \t// TODO: TypeURLReady - readiness events for endpoints, agent can propagate\n42: )\n43: \n44: // StatusGen is a Generator for XDS status: connections, syncz, configdump\n45: type StatusGen struct {\n46: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n47: \n48: \t// TODO: track last N Nacks and connection events, with 'version' based on timestamp.\n49: \t// On new connect, use version to send recent events since last update.\n50: }\n51: \n52: func NewStatusGen(s *DiscoveryServer) *StatusGen {\n53: \treturn &StatusGen{\n54: \t\tServer: s,\n55: \t}\n56: }\n57: \n58: // Generate XDS responses about internal events:\n59: // - connection status\n60: // - NACKs\n61: // We can also expose ACKS.\n62: func (sg *StatusGen) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n63: \treturn sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\n64: }\n65: \n66: // Generate delta XDS responses about internal events:\n67: // - connection status\n68: // - NACKs\n69: // We can also expose ACKS.\n70: func (sg *StatusGen) GenerateDeltas(\n71: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n72: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n73: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n74: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n75: \tres, detail, err := sg.handleInternalRequest(proxy, w, req)\n76: \treturn res, nil, detail, true, err\n77: }\n78: \n79: func (sg *StatusGen) handleInternalRequest(_ *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, _ *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n80: \tres := model.Resources{}\n81: \n82: \tswitch w.TypeUrl {\n83: \tcase TypeDebugSyncronization:\n84: \t\tres = sg.debugSyncz()\n85: \tcase TypeDebugConfigDump:\n86: \t\tif len(w.ResourceNames) == 0 || len(w.ResourceNames) > 1 {\n87: \t\t\t// Malformed request from client\n88: \t\t\tlog.Infof(\"%s with %d ResourceNames\", TypeDebugConfigDump, len(w.ResourceNames))\n89: \t\t\tbreak\n90: \t\t}\n91: \t\tvar err error\n92: \t\tdumpRes, err := sg.debugConfigDump(w.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()[0])\n93: \t\tif err != nil {\n94: \t\t\tlog.Infof(\"%s failed: %v\", TypeDebugConfigDump, err)\n95: \t\t\tbreak\n96: \t\t}\n97: \t\tres = dumpRes\n98: \t}\n99: \treturn res, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n100: }\n101: \n102: // isSidecar ad-hoc method to see if connection represents a sidecar\n103: func isProxy(con *Connection) bool {\n104: \treturn con != nil &&\n105: \t\tcon.proxy != nil &&\n106: \t\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\n107: \t\tcon.proxy.Metadata.ProxyConfig != nil\n108: }\n109: \n110: func isZtunnel(con *Connection) bool {\n111: \treturn con != nil &&\n112: \t\tcon.proxy != nil &&\n113: \t\tcon.proxy.Metadata != nil &&\n114: \t\tcon.proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel\n115: }\n116: \n117: func (sg *StatusGen) debugSyncz() model.Resources {\n118: \tres := model.Resources{}\n119: \n120: \tfor _, con := range sg.Server.Clients() {\n121: \t\tcon.proxy.RLock()\n122: \t\t// Skip \"nodes\" without metadata (they are probably istioctl queries!)\n123: \t\tif isProxy(con) || isZtunnel(con) {\n124: \t\t\txdsConfigs := make([]*status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig, 0)\n125: \t\t\twrs := con.proxy.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\n126: \t\t\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\n127: \t\t\t\tpxc := &status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig{}\n128: \t\t\t\tpxc.ConfigStatus = debugSyncStatus(wr)\n129: \t\t\t\tpxc.LastUpdated = timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime)\n130: \t\t\t\tpxc.TypeUrl = wr.TypeUrl\n131: \t\t\t\tif wr.LastError != \"\" {\n132: \t\t\t\t\tpxc.ErrorState = &admin.UpdateFailureState{\n133: \t\t\t\t\t\tLastUpdateAttempt: timestamppb.New(wr.LastSendTime),\n134: \t\t\t\t\t\tDetails:           wr.LastError,\n135: \t\t\t\t\t}\n136: \t\t\t\t}\n137: \n138: \t\t\t\txdsConfigs = append(xdsConfigs, pxc)\n139: \t\t\t}\n140: \t\t\tslices.SortBy(xdsConfigs, func(a *status.ClientConfig_GenericXdsConfig) string {\n141: \t\t\t\treturn a.TypeUrl\n142: \t\t\t})\n143: \t\t\tclientConfig := &status.ClientConfig{\n144: \t\t\t\tNode: &core.Node{\n145: \t\t\t\t\tId: con.proxy.ID,\n146: \t\t\t\t\tMetadata: model.NodeMetadata{\n147: \t\t\t\t\t\tClusterID:    con.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n148: \t\t\t\t\t\tNamespace:    con.proxy.Metadata.Namespace,\n149: \t\t\t\t\t\tIstioVersion: con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion,\n150: \t\t\t\t\t}.ToStruct(),\n151: \t\t\t\t},\n152: \t\t\t\tGenericXdsConfigs: xdsConfigs,\n153: \t\t\t}\n154: \t\t\tres = append(res, &discovery.Resource{\n155: \t\t\t\tName:     clientConfig.Node.Id,\n156: \t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(clientConfig),\n157: \t\t\t})\n158: \t\t}\n159: \t\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\n160: \t}\n161: \n162: \treturn res\n163: }\n164: \n165: func debugSyncStatus(wr model.WatchedResource) status.ConfigStatus {\n166: \tif wr.LastError != \"\" {\n167: \t\treturn status.ConfigStatus_ERROR\n168: \t}\n169: \tif wr.NonceSent == \"\" {\n170: \t\treturn status.ConfigStatus_NOT_SENT\n171: \t}\n172: \tif wr.NonceAcked == wr.NonceSent {\n173: \t\treturn status.ConfigStatus_SYNCED\n174: \t}\n175: \treturn status.ConfigStatus_STALE\n176: }\n177: \n178: func (sg *StatusGen) debugConfigDump(proxyID string) (model.Resources, error) {\n179: \tconn := sg.Server.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\n180: \tif conn == nil {\n181: \t\t// This is \"like\" a 404.  The error is the client's.  However, this endpoint\n182: \t\t// only tracks a single \"shard\" of connections.  The client may try another instance.\n183: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"config dump could not find connection for proxyID %q\", proxyID)\n184: \t}\n185: \n186: \tdump, err := sg.Server.connectionConfigDump(conn, false)\n187: \tif err != nil {\n188: \t\treturn nil, err\n189: \t}\n190: \n191: \treturn model.AnyToUnnamedResources(dump.Configs), nil\n192: }\n193: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/statusgen.go"
  }
}
42. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n19: \\n20: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\\\"\\n22: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n25: )\\n26: \\n27: type WorkloadGenerator struct {\\n28: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n29: }\\n30: \\n31: var (\\n32: \\t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}\\n33: \\t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}\\n34: )\\n35: \\n36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\\n39: //\\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n44: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n45: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n46: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n48: \\taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\\n49: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n50: \\tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\\n51: \\t\\t// Nothing changed...\\n52: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\n55: \\tif !w.Wildcard {\\n56: \\t\\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\treqAddresses := addresses\\n60: \\tif isReq {\\n61: \\t\\treqAddresses = nil\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\\n64: \\t// Note: while \\\"removed\\\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\\n65: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\\n66: \\thave := sets.New[string]()\\n67: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\\n68: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n69: \\t\\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\\n70: \\t}\\n71: \\n72: \\tif isReq {\\n73: \\t\\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\\n74: \\t\\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\\n75: \\t\\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\\n76: \\t\\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\\n77: \\t\\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\n80: \\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\\n81: \\t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\\n82: \\t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\\n83: \\t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\\n84: \\n85: \\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n86: }\\n87: \\n88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\\n89: \\tn := addr.ResourceName()\\n90: \\thave.Insert(n)\\n91: \\tswitch requestedType {\\n92: \\tcase v3.WorkloadType:\\n93: \\t\\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n97: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\\n98: \\t\\t\\t})\\n99: \\t\\t}\\n100: \\tcase v3.AddressType:\\n101: \\t\\tproto := addr.Marshaled\\n102: \\t\\tif proto == nil {\\n103: \\t\\t\\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\n106: \\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n107: \\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n109: \\t\\t\\tResource: proto,\\n110: \\t\\t})\\n111: \\t}\\n112: \\treturn resources\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n116: \\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\\n117: \\treturn resources, details, err\\n118: }\\n119: \\n120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\\n121: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n122: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n123: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n125: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n126: \\tsubs := w.ResourceNames\\n127: \\n128: \\tvar addresses sets.String\\n129: \\tif isReq {\\n130: \\t\\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\\n131: \\t\\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\\n132: \\t\\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\\n133: \\t\\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\\n134: \\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\\n136: \\t\\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\\n137: \\t\\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\t// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they\\n141: \\t// didn't explicitly request.\\n142: \\t// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.\\n143: \\tadditional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)\\n144: \\tif addresses == nil {\\n145: \\t\\taddresses = additional\\n146: \\t} else {\\n147: \\t\\taddresses.Merge(additional)\\n148: \\t}\\n149: \\n150: \\tif len(addresses) == 0 {\\n151: \\t\\tif isReq {\\n152: \\t\\t\\t// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with\\n153: \\t\\t\\treturn make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n154: \\t\\t}\\n155: \\t\\t// For NOP pushes, no need\\n156: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)\\n159: \\t// Note: while \\\"removed\\\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\\n160: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\\n161: \\thave := sets.New[string]()\\n162: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\\n163: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n164: \\t\\taliases := addr.Aliases()\\n165: \\t\\tremoved.DeleteAll(aliases...)\\n166: \\t\\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n170: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n171: \\t// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update\\n172: \\t// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.\\n173: \\tw.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)\\n174: \\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n175: }\\n176: \\n177: type WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {\\n178: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n182: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n183: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n184: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n185: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n186: \\tvar updatedPolicies sets.Set[model.ConfigKey]\\n187: \\texpected := sets.New[string]()\\n188: \\tif req.Forced {\\n189: \\t\\t// Full update, expect everything\\n190: \\t\\texpected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)\\n191: \\t} else {\\n192: \\t\\t// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,\\n193: \\t\\t// so we can only fetch these ones.\\n194: \\t\\tupdatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)\\n195: \\n196: \\t\\tif len(updatedPolicies) == 0 {\\n197: \\t\\t\\t// This was a incremental push for a resource we don't watch... skip\\n198: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\\n199: \\t\\t}\\n200: \\n201: \\t\\tfor k := range updatedPolicies {\\n202: \\t\\t\\texpected.Insert(k.Namespace + \\\"/\\\" + k.Name)\\n203: \\t\\t}\\n204: \\t}\\n205: \\n206: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0)\\n207: \\tpolicies := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(updatedPolicies)\\n208: \\tfor _, p := range policies {\\n209: \\t\\tn := p.ResourceName()\\n210: \\t\\texpected.Delete(n) // delete the generated policy name, left the removed ones\\n211: \\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n212: \\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n213: \\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(p.Authorization),\\n214: \\t\\t})\\n215: \\t}\\n216: \\n217: \\treturn resources, sets.SortedList(expected), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n218: }\\n219: \\n220: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n221: \\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\\n222: \\treturn resources, details, err\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: var (\\n226: \\t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\\n227: \\t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\\n228: )\\n229: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n19: \n20: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n21: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n22: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n23: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n25: )\n26: \n27: type WorkloadGenerator struct {\n28: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n29: }\n30: \n31: var (\n32: \t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n33: \t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n34: )\n35: \n36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\n39: //\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n44: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n45: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n46: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n48: \taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\n49: \tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n50: \tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\n51: \t\t// Nothing changed...\n52: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n53: \t}\n54: \n55: \tif !w.Wildcard {\n56: \t\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\n57: \t}\n58: \n59: \treqAddresses := addresses\n60: \tif isReq {\n61: \t\treqAddresses = nil\n62: \t}\n63: \taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\n64: \t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n65: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n66: \thave := sets.New[string]()\n67: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n68: \tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n69: \t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\n70: \t}\n71: \n72: \tif isReq {\n73: \t\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\n74: \t\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\n75: \t\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\n76: \t\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\n77: \t\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\n78: \t}\n79: \n80: \t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\n81: \t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\n82: \t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\n83: \t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\n84: \n85: \treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n86: }\n87: \n88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\n89: \tn := addr.ResourceName()\n90: \thave.Insert(n)\n91: \tswitch requestedType {\n92: \tcase v3.WorkloadType:\n93: \t\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\n94: \t\t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n95: \t\t\t\tName:     n,\n96: \t\t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n97: \t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\n98: \t\t\t})\n99: \t\t}\n100: \tcase v3.AddressType:\n101: \t\tproto := addr.Marshaled\n102: \t\tif proto == nil {\n103: \t\t\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\n104: \t\t}\n105: \n106: \t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n107: \t\t\tName:     n,\n108: \t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n109: \t\t\tResource: proto,\n110: \t\t})\n111: \t}\n112: \treturn resources\n113: }\n114: \n115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n116: \tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n117: \treturn resources, details, err\n118: }\n119: \n120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\n121: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n122: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n123: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n125: \tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n126: \tsubs := w.ResourceNames\n127: \n128: \tvar addresses sets.String\n129: \tif isReq {\n130: \t\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\n131: \t\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\n132: \t\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\n133: \t\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\n134: \t} else {\n135: \t\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\n136: \t\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\n137: \t\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\n138: \t}\n139: \n140: \t// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they\n141: \t// didn't explicitly request.\n142: \t// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.\n143: \tadditional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)\n144: \tif addresses == nil {\n145: \t\taddresses = additional\n146: \t} else {\n147: \t\taddresses.Merge(additional)\n148: \t}\n149: \n150: \tif len(addresses) == 0 {\n151: \t\tif isReq {\n152: \t\t\t// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with\n153: \t\t\treturn make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n154: \t\t}\n155: \t\t// For NOP pushes, no need\n156: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n157: \t}\n158: \taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)\n159: \t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n160: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n161: \thave := sets.New[string]()\n162: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n163: \tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n164: \t\taliases := addr.Aliases()\n165: \t\tremoved.DeleteAll(aliases...)\n166: \t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)\n167: \t}\n168: \n169: \tproxy.Lock()\n170: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n171: \t// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update\n172: \t// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.\n173: \tw.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)\n174: \treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n175: }\n176: \n177: type WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {\n178: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n179: }\n180: \n181: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n182: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n183: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n184: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n185: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n186: \tvar updatedPolicies sets.Set[model.ConfigKey]\n187: \texpected := sets.New[string]()\n188: \tif req.Forced {\n189: \t\t// Full update, expect everything\n190: \t\texpected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)\n191: \t} else {\n192: \t\t// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,\n193: \t\t// so we can only fetch these ones.\n194: \t\tupdatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)\n195: \n196: \t\tif len(updatedPolicies) == 0 {\n197: \t\t\t// This was a incremental push for a resource we don't watch... skip\n198: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\n199: \t\t}\n200: \n201: \t\tfor k := range updatedPolicies {\n202: \t\t\texpected.Insert(k.Namespace + \"/\" + k.Name)\n203: \t\t}\n204: \t}\n205: \n206: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0)\n207: \tpolicies := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(updatedPolicies)\n208: \tfor _, p := range policies {\n209: \t\tn := p.ResourceName()\n210: \t\texpected.Delete(n) // delete the generated policy name, left the removed ones\n211: \t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n212: \t\t\tName:     n,\n213: \t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(p.Authorization),\n214: \t\t})\n215: \t}\n216: \n217: \treturn resources, sets.SortedList(expected), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n218: }\n219: \n220: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n221: \tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n222: \treturn resources, details, err\n223: }\n224: \n225: var (\n226: \t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\n227: \t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\n228: )\n229: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go"
  }
}
43. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "pilot/pkg/xds/xdsgen.go",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\tcore \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\\\"\\n24: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n28: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/lazy\\\"\\n32: \\tistioversion \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/version\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n34: )\\n35: \\n36: // IstioControlPlaneInstance defines the format Istio uses for when creating Envoy config.core.v3.ControlPlane.identifier\\n37: type IstioControlPlaneInstance struct {\\n38: \\t// The Istio component type (e.g. \\\"istiod\\\")\\n39: \\tComponent string\\n40: \\t// The ID of the component instance\\n41: \\tID string\\n42: \\t// The Istio version\\n43: \\tInfo istioversion.BuildInfo\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // Evaluate the controlPlane lazily in order to allow \\\"POD_NAME\\\" env var setting after running the process.\\n47: var controlPlane = lazy.New(func() (*core.ControlPlane, error) {\\n48: \\t// The Pod Name (instance identity) is in PilotArgs, but not reachable globally nor from DiscoveryServer\\n49: \\tpodName := env.Register(\\\"POD_NAME\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\").Get()\\n50: \\tbyVersion, err := json.Marshal(IstioControlPlaneInstance{\\n51: \\t\\tComponent: \\\"istiod\\\",\\n52: \\t\\tID:        podName,\\n53: \\t\\tInfo:      istioversion.Info,\\n54: \\t})\\n55: \\tif err != nil {\\n56: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"XDS: Could not serialize control plane id: %v\\\", err)\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\treturn &core.ControlPlane{Identifier: string(byVersion)}, nil\\n59: })\\n60: \\n61: // ControlPlane identifies the instance and Istio version, based on the requested type URL\\n62: func ControlPlane(typ string) *core.ControlPlane {\\n63: \\tif typ != TypeDebugSyncronization {\\n64: \\t\\t// Currently only TypeDebugSyncronization utilizes this so don't both sending otherwise\\n65: \\t\\treturn nil\\n66: \\t}\\n67: \\t// Error will never happen because the getter of lazy does not return error.\\n68: \\tcp, _ := controlPlane.Get()\\n69: \\treturn cp\\n70: }\\n71: \\n72: func (s *DiscoveryServer) findGenerator(typeURL string, con *Connection) model.XdsResourceGenerator {\\n73: \\tif g, f := s.Generators[con.proxy.Metadata.Generator+\\\"/\\\"+typeURL]; f {\\n74: \\t\\treturn g\\n75: \\t}\\n76: \\tif g, f := s.Generators[string(con.proxy.Type)+\\\"/\\\"+typeURL]; f {\\n77: \\t\\treturn g\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\n80: \\tif g, f := s.Generators[typeURL]; f {\\n81: \\t\\treturn g\\n82: \\t}\\n83: \\n84: \\t// XdsResourceGenerator is the default generator for this connection. We want to allow\\n85: \\t// some types to use custom generators - for example EDS.\\n86: \\tg := con.proxy.XdsResourceGenerator\\n87: \\tif g == nil {\\n88: \\t\\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeURL, TypeDebugPrefix) {\\n89: \\t\\t\\tg = s.Generators[\\\"event\\\"]\\n90: \\t\\t} else {\\n91: \\t\\t\\t// TODO move this to just directly using the resource TypeUrl\\n92: \\t\\t\\tg = s.Generators[\\\"api\\\"] // default to \\\"MCP\\\" generators - any type supported by store\\n93: \\t\\t}\\n94: \\t}\\n95: \\treturn g\\n96: }\\n97: \\n98: // Push an XDS resource for the given connection. Configuration will be generated\\n99: // based on the passed in generator. Based on the updates field, generators may\\n100: // choose to send partial or even no response if there are no changes.\\n101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\\n102: \\tif w == nil {\\n103: \\t\\treturn nil\\n104: \\t}\\n105: \\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\\n106: \\tif gen == nil {\\n107: \\t\\treturn nil\\n108: \\t}\\n109: \\n110: \\tt0 := time.Now()\\n111: \\n112: \\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\\n113: \\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\\n114: \\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\\n115: \\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\\n116: \\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\\n117: \\tvar logFiltered string\\n118: \\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !con.proxy.IsProxylessGrpc() {\\n119: \\t\\tlogFiltered = \\\" filtered:\\\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\\n120: \\t\\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\\n121: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\\n122: \\t\\t\\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\\n123: \\t\\t}\\n124: \\t}\\n125: \\tres, logdata, err := gen.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\\n126: \\tinfo := \\\"\\\"\\n127: \\tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\\n128: \\t\\tinfo = \\\" \\\" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\\n129: \\t}\\n130: \\tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\\n131: \\t\\tinfo += logFiltered\\n132: \\t}\\n133: \\tif err != nil || res == nil {\\n134: \\t\\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n135: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"%s: SKIP%s for node:%s%s\\\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, info)\\n136: \\t\\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\t\\treturn err\\n139: \\t}\\n140: \\tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\\n141: \\n142: \\tresp := &discovery.DiscoveryResponse{\\n143: \\t\\tControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\\n144: \\t\\tTypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,\\n145: \\t\\t// TODO: send different version for incremental eds\\n146: \\t\\tVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\\n147: \\t\\tNonce:       nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\\n148: \\t\\tResources:   xds.ResourcesToAny(res),\\n149: \\t}\\n150: \\n151: \\tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\\n152: \\tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\\n153: \\n154: \\tptype := \\\"PUSH\\\"\\n155: \\tif logdata.Incremental {\\n156: \\t\\tptype = \\\"PUSH INC\\\"\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\tif err := xds.Send(con, resp); err != nil {\\n160: \\t\\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\\n161: \\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\\\",\\n162: \\t\\t\\t\\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\\n163: \\t\\t}\\n164: \\t\\treturn err\\n165: \\t}\\n166: \\n167: \\tswitch {\\n168: \\tcase !req.Full:\\n169: \\t\\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n170: \\t\\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\\\",\\n171: \\t\\t\\t\\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\\n172: \\t\\t}\\n173: \\tdefault:\\n174: \\t\\tdebug := \\\"\\\"\\n175: \\t\\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\\n176: \\t\\t\\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\\n177: \\t\\t\\tdebug = \\\" nonce:\\\" + resp.Nonce + \\\" version:\\\" + resp.VersionInfo\\n178: \\t\\t}\\n179: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%v%s%s\\\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res),\\n180: \\t\\t\\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\\n181: \\t}\\n182: \\n183: \\treturn nil\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: func ResourceSize(r model.Resources) int {\\n187: \\t// Approximate size by looking at the Any marshaled size. This avoids high cost\\n188: \\t// proto.Size, at the expense of slightly under counting.\\n189: \\tsize := 0\\n190: \\tfor _, r := range r {\\n191: \\t\\tsize += len(r.Resource.Value)\\n192: \\t}\\n193: \\treturn size\\n194: }\\n195: \\n196: // xdsNeedsPush checks for the common cases whether we need to push or not.\\n197: func xdsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) (needsPush, definitive bool) {\\n198: \\tif proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel {\\n199: \\t\\t// Not supported for ztunnel\\n200: \\t\\treturn false, true\\n201: \\t}\\n202: \\tif req == nil {\\n203: \\t\\treturn true, true\\n204: \\t}\\n205: \\t// If none set, we will always push\\n206: \\tif req.Forced {\\n207: \\t\\treturn true, true\\n208: \\t}\\n209: \\n210: \\t// We can not definitively say if we need to push or not based on generic checks, so we will push based\\n211: \\t// on the specific typeURL checks.\\n212: \\treturn false, false\\n213: }\\n214: \\n215: // waypointNeedsPush checks if an incremental push is needed for CDS and LDS which normally do a full push.\\n216: func waypointNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\\n217: \\t// Waypoint proxies needs to be pushed for LDS and CDS on kind.Address changes.\\n218: \\t// Waypoint proxies have a matcher against pod IPs in them. Historically, any LDS change would do a full\\n219: \\t// push, recomputing push context. Doing that on every IP change doesn't scale, so we need these to remain\\n220: \\t// incremental pushes.\\n221: \\t// This allows waypoints only to push LDS on incremental pushes to Address type which would otherwise be skipped.\\n222: \\n223: \\t// Waypoints need CDS updates on kind.Address changes\\n224: \\t// after implementing use-waypoint which decouples waypoint creation, wl pod creation\\n225: \\t// user specifying waypoint use. Without this we're not getting correct waypoint config\\n226: \\t// in a timely manner\\n227: \\treturn model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address)\\n228: }\\n229: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/xdsgen.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"encoding/json\"\n19: \t\"strconv\"\n20: \t\"strings\"\n21: \t\"time\"\n22: \n23: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n24: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n25: \n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n28: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n29: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n30: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n31: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/lazy\"\n32: \tistioversion \"istio.io/istio/pkg/version\"\n33: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n34: )\n35: \n36: // IstioControlPlaneInstance defines the format Istio uses for when creating Envoy config.core.v3.ControlPlane.identifier\n37: type IstioControlPlaneInstance struct {\n38: \t// The Istio component type (e.g. \"istiod\")\n39: \tComponent string\n40: \t// The ID of the component instance\n41: \tID string\n42: \t// The Istio version\n43: \tInfo istioversion.BuildInfo\n44: }\n45: \n46: // Evaluate the controlPlane lazily in order to allow \"POD_NAME\" env var setting after running the process.\n47: var controlPlane = lazy.New(func() (*core.ControlPlane, error) {\n48: \t// The Pod Name (instance identity) is in PilotArgs, but not reachable globally nor from DiscoveryServer\n49: \tpodName := env.Register(\"POD_NAME\", \"\", \"\").Get()\n50: \tbyVersion, err := json.Marshal(IstioControlPlaneInstance{\n51: \t\tComponent: \"istiod\",\n52: \t\tID:        podName,\n53: \t\tInfo:      istioversion.Info,\n54: \t})\n55: \tif err != nil {\n56: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"XDS: Could not serialize control plane id: %v\", err)\n57: \t}\n58: \treturn &core.ControlPlane{Identifier: string(byVersion)}, nil\n59: })\n60: \n61: // ControlPlane identifies the instance and Istio version, based on the requested type URL\n62: func ControlPlane(typ string) *core.ControlPlane {\n63: \tif typ != TypeDebugSyncronization {\n64: \t\t// Currently only TypeDebugSyncronization utilizes this so don't both sending otherwise\n65: \t\treturn nil\n66: \t}\n67: \t// Error will never happen because the getter of lazy does not return error.\n68: \tcp, _ := controlPlane.Get()\n69: \treturn cp\n70: }\n71: \n72: func (s *DiscoveryServer) findGenerator(typeURL string, con *Connection) model.XdsResourceGenerator {\n73: \tif g, f := s.Generators[con.proxy.Metadata.Generator+\"/\"+typeURL]; f {\n74: \t\treturn g\n75: \t}\n76: \tif g, f := s.Generators[string(con.proxy.Type)+\"/\"+typeURL]; f {\n77: \t\treturn g\n78: \t}\n79: \n80: \tif g, f := s.Generators[typeURL]; f {\n81: \t\treturn g\n82: \t}\n83: \n84: \t// XdsResourceGenerator is the default generator for this connection. We want to allow\n85: \t// some types to use custom generators - for example EDS.\n86: \tg := con.proxy.XdsResourceGenerator\n87: \tif g == nil {\n88: \t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeURL, TypeDebugPrefix) {\n89: \t\t\tg = s.Generators[\"event\"]\n90: \t\t} else {\n91: \t\t\t// TODO move this to just directly using the resource TypeUrl\n92: \t\t\tg = s.Generators[\"api\"] // default to \"MCP\" generators - any type supported by store\n93: \t\t}\n94: \t}\n95: \treturn g\n96: }\n97: \n98: // Push an XDS resource for the given connection. Configuration will be generated\n99: // based on the passed in generator. Based on the updates field, generators may\n100: // choose to send partial or even no response if there are no changes.\n101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n102: \tif w == nil {\n103: \t\treturn nil\n104: \t}\n105: \tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n106: \tif gen == nil {\n107: \t\treturn nil\n108: \t}\n109: \n110: \tt0 := time.Now()\n111: \n112: \t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\n113: \t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\n114: \t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\n115: \t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n116: \t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n117: \tvar logFiltered string\n118: \tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !con.proxy.IsProxylessGrpc() {\n119: \t\tlogFiltered = \" filtered:\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\n120: \t\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\n121: \t\t\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\n122: \t\t\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\n123: \t\t}\n124: \t}\n125: \tres, logdata, err := gen.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\n126: \tinfo := \"\"\n127: \tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\n128: \t\tinfo = \" \" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\n129: \t}\n130: \tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\n131: \t\tinfo += logFiltered\n132: \t}\n133: \tif err != nil || res == nil {\n134: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n135: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: SKIP%s for node:%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, info)\n136: \t\t}\n137: \n138: \t\treturn err\n139: \t}\n140: \tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\n141: \n142: \tresp := &discovery.DiscoveryResponse{\n143: \t\tControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\n144: \t\tTypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,\n145: \t\t// TODO: send different version for incremental eds\n146: \t\tVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\n147: \t\tNonce:       nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\n148: \t\tResources:   xds.ResourcesToAny(res),\n149: \t}\n150: \n151: \tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\n152: \tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\n153: \n154: \tptype := \"PUSH\"\n155: \tif logdata.Incremental {\n156: \t\tptype = \"PUSH INC\"\n157: \t}\n158: \n159: \tif err := xds.Send(con, resp); err != nil {\n160: \t\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\n161: \t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\",\n162: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t\treturn err\n165: \t}\n166: \n167: \tswitch {\n168: \tcase !req.Full:\n169: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n170: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n171: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n172: \t\t}\n173: \tdefault:\n174: \t\tdebug := \"\"\n175: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n176: \t\t\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\n177: \t\t\tdebug = \" nonce:\" + resp.Nonce + \" version:\" + resp.VersionInfo\n178: \t\t}\n179: \t\tlog.Infof(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%v%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res),\n180: \t\t\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\n181: \t}\n182: \n183: \treturn nil\n184: }\n185: \n186: func ResourceSize(r model.Resources) int {\n187: \t// Approximate size by looking at the Any marshaled size. This avoids high cost\n188: \t// proto.Size, at the expense of slightly under counting.\n189: \tsize := 0\n190: \tfor _, r := range r {\n191: \t\tsize += len(r.Resource.Value)\n192: \t}\n193: \treturn size\n194: }\n195: \n196: // xdsNeedsPush checks for the common cases whether we need to push or not.\n197: func xdsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) (needsPush, definitive bool) {\n198: \tif proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel {\n199: \t\t// Not supported for ztunnel\n200: \t\treturn false, true\n201: \t}\n202: \tif req == nil {\n203: \t\treturn true, true\n204: \t}\n205: \t// If none set, we will always push\n206: \tif req.Forced {\n207: \t\treturn true, true\n208: \t}\n209: \n210: \t// We can not definitively say if we need to push or not based on generic checks, so we will push based\n211: \t// on the specific typeURL checks.\n212: \treturn false, false\n213: }\n214: \n215: // waypointNeedsPush checks if an incremental push is needed for CDS and LDS which normally do a full push.\n216: func waypointNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n217: \t// Waypoint proxies needs to be pushed for LDS and CDS on kind.Address changes.\n218: \t// Waypoint proxies have a matcher against pod IPs in them. Historically, any LDS change would do a full\n219: \t// push, recomputing push context. Doing that on every IP change doesn't scale, so we need these to remain\n220: \t// incremental pushes.\n221: \t// This allows waypoints only to push LDS on incremental pushes to Address type which would otherwise be skipped.\n222: \n223: \t// Waypoints need CDS updates on kind.Address changes\n224: \t// after implementing use-waypoint which decouples waypoint creation, wl pod creation\n225: \t// user specifying waypoint use. Without this we're not getting correct waypoint config\n226: \t// in a timely manner\n227: \treturn model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address)\n228: }\n229: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/xdsgen.go"
  }
}

&xds.NdsGenerator{ConfigGenerator: cg}\\n50: \\tgenerators[v3.ProxyConfigType] = &xds.PcdsGenerator{TrustBundle: env.TrustBundle}\\n51: \\n52: \\tworkloadGen := &xds.WorkloadGenerator{Server: s}\\n53: InitGenerators(\n30: \ts *xds.DiscoveryServer,\n31: \tcg core.ConfigGenerator,\n32: \tsystemNameSpace string,\n33: \tclusterID cluster.ID,\n34: \tinternalDebugMux *http.ServeMux,\n35: ) {\n36: \tenv := s.Env\n37: \tgenerators := map[string]model.XdsResourceGenerator{}\n38: \tedsGen := &xds.EdsGenerator{Cache: s.Cache, EndpointIndex: env.EndpointIndex}\n39: \tgenerators[v3.ClusterType] = xds.NewSecretGen(env.CredentialsController, s.Cache, clusterID, env.Mesh())\n46: \t\tecdsGen.SetCredController(env.CredentialsController)\n47: \t}\n48: \tgenerators[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType] = ecdsGen\n49: \tgenerators[v3.NameTableType] = &xds.NdsGenerator{ConfigGenerator: cg}\n50: \tgenerators[v3.ProxyConfigType] = &xds.PcdsGenerator{TrustBundle: env.TrustBundle}\n51: \n52: \tworkloadGen template\\\", s.injectTemplateHandler(webhook))\\n224: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/mesh\\\", \\\"Active mesh config\\\", s.meshHandler)\\n225: \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, \\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \\\"/debug/list\\\", \\\"List all supported debug commands in json\\\", s.list)\\n230: }\\n231: \\n232: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\\n233: \\tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\\n234: ) {\\n235: \\ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\\n236: \\t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\\n237: \\tif internalMux != nil {\\n238: \\t\\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\\n239: \\t}\\n240: \\t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\\n241: \\tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\\n245: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n246: \\t\\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\\n247: \\t\\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\\n248: \\t\\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\\n249: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n250: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n251: \\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\\n253: \\t\\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\\n254: \\t\\tvar ids []string\\n255: \\t\\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\\n256: \\t\\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t// If one authenticator passes, return\\n259: \\t\\t\\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\tids = u.Identities\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n262: \\t\\t\\t}\\n263: \\t\\t\\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Authenticator %s: %v\\\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\\n264: \\t\\t}\\n265: \\t\\tif ids == nil {\\n266: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\\\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \\\"authentication failure).\\n268: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\\n269: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\\n272: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\\n274: \\t\\t\\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\\\", ids, req.URL.Path)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\\n280: \\t\\t\\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\\n281: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\\n282: \\t\\t} else {\\n283: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t}\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\\n289: \\tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\\n290: \\tif err != nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn false\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\\n295: \\treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\\n300: \\tfor _, id := range identities `json:\"totalClients\"`\n122: \tConnected []AdsClient `json:\"clients,omitempty\"`\n123: }\n124: \n125: // SyncStatus is the synchronization status between Pilot and a given Envoy\n126: type SyncStatus struct {\n127: \tClusterID            string                    `json:\"cluster_id,omitempty\"`\n128: \tProxyID              string                    `json:\"proxy,omitempty\"`\n129: string    `json:\"sent,omitempty\"`\n147: \tAcked     string    `json:\"acked,omitempty\"`\n148: \tSentTime  time.Time `json:\"sentTime,omitempty\"`\n149: \tLastError string    `json:\"lastError,omitempty\"`\n150: }\n151: \n152: // SyncedVersions shows what resourceVersion of a given resource has been acked by Envoy.\n153: type SyncedVersions struct {\n154: \tProxyID         string `json:\"proxy,omitempty\"`\n155: \tClusterVersion  string `json:\"cluster_acked,omitempty\"`\n156: \tListenerVersion string `json:\"listener_acked,omitempty\"`\n157: \tRouteVersion    string `json:\"route_acked,omitempty\"`\n158: \tEndpointVersion string `json:\"endpoint_acked,omitempty\"`\n159: }\n160: \n161: // InitDebug initializes the debug handlers and adds a debug in-memory registry.\n162: func (s *DiscoveryServer) InitDebug(\n163: \tmux *http.ServeMux,\n164: \tenableProfiling bool,\n165: \tfetchWebhook func() map[string]string,\n166: ) *http.ServeMux {\n167: \tinternalMux := http.NewServeMux()\n168: \ts.AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux, enableProfiling, fetchWebhook)\n169: \treturn internalMux\n170: }\n171: \n172: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux *http.ServeMux, enableProfiling bool, webhook func() map[string]string) {\n173: \t// Debug handlers on HTTP ports are added for backward compatibility.\n174: \t// They will be exposed on XDS-over-TLS in future releases.\n175: \tif !features.EnableDebugOnHTTP {\n176: \t\treturn\n177: \t}\n178: \n179: \tif enableProfiling {\n180: \t\truntime.SetMutexProfileFraction(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n181: \t\truntime.SetBlockProfileRate(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n182: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/\", \"Displays pprof index\", pprof.Index)\n183: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/cmdline\", \"The command line invocation of the current program\", pprof.Cmdline)\n184: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/profile\", \"CPU profile\", pprof.Profile)\n185: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/symbol\", \"Symbol looks up the program counters listed in the request\", pprof.Symbol)\n186: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/trace\", \"A trace of execution of the current program.\", pprof.Trace)\n187: \t}\n188: \n189: \tmux.HandleFunc(\"/debug\", s.Debug)\n190: \n191: \tif features.EnableUnsafeAdminEndpoints {\n192: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/force_disconnect\", \"Disconnects a proxy from this Pilot\", s.forceDisconnect)\n193: \t}\n194: \n195: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ecdsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ECDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"ecds\"))\n196: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/edsz\", \"Status and debug interface for EDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"eds\"))\n197: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ndsz\", \"Status and debug interface for NDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"nds\"))\n198: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ADS\", s.adsz)\n199: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz?push=true\", \"Initiates push of the current state to all connected endpoints\", s.adsz)\n200: \n201: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/syncz\", \"Synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\", s.Syncz)\n202: \n203: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/registryz\", \"Debug support for registry\", s.registryz)\n204: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointz\", \"Obsolete, use endpointShardz\", s.endpointShardz)\n205: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointShardz\", \"Info about the endpoint shards\", s.endpointShardz)\n206: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez\", \"Info about the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n207: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?sizes=true\", \"Info about the size of the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n208: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?clear=true\", \"Clear the XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n209: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/configz\", \"Debug support for config\", s.configz)\n210: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/sidecarz\", \"Debug sidecar scope for a proxy\", s.sidecarz)\n211: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/resourcesz\", \"Debug support for watched resources\", s.resourcez)\n212: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/instancesz\", \"Debug support for service instances\", s.instancesz)\n213: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ambientz\", \"Debug support for ambient\", s.ambientz)\n214: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/krtz\", \"Debug support for krt (internal state)\", s.krtz)\n215: \n216: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/authorizationz\", \"Internal authorization policies\", s.authorizationz)\n217: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/telemetryz\", \"Debug Telemetry configuration\", s.telemetryz)\n218: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/config_dump\", \"ConfigDump in the form of the Envoy admin config dump API for passed in proxyID\", s.ConfigDump)\n219: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/push_status\", \"Last PushContext Details\", s.pushStatusHandler)\n220: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pushcontext\", \"Debug support for current push context\", s.pushContextHandler)\n221: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/connections\", \"Info about the connected XDS clients\", s.connectionsHandler)\n222: \n223: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/inject\", \"Active inject template\", s.injectTemplateHandler(webhook))\n224: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mesh\", \"Active mesh config\", s.meshHandler)\n225: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/clusterz\", \"List remote clusters where istiod reads endpoints\", s.clusterz)\n226: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/networkz\", \"List cross-network gateways\", s.networkz)\n227: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mcsz\", \"List information about Kubernetes MCS services\", s.mcsz)\n228: \n229: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/list\", \"List all supported debug commands in json\", s.list)\n230: }\n231: \n232: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\n233: \tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\n234: ) {\n235: \ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\n236: \t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\n237: \tif internalMux != nil {\n238: \t\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\n239: \t}\n240: \t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\n241: \tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\n242: }\n243: \n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\n245: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n246: \t\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\n247: \t\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\n248: \t\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\n249: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n250: \t\t\treturn\n251: \t\t}\n252: \t\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\n253: \t\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\n254: \t\tvar ids []string\n255: \t\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\n256: \t\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\n257: \t\t\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\n258: \t\t\t// If one authenticator passes, return\n259: \t\t\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\n260: \t\t\t\tids = u.Identities\n261: \t\t\t\tbreak\n262: \t\t\t}\n263: \t\t\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\"Authenticator %s: %v\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\n264: \t\t}\n265: \t\tif ids == nil {\n266: \t\t\tistiolog.Errorf(\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\n267: \t\t\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \"authentication failure).\n268: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\n269: \t\t\treturn\n270: \t\t}\n271: \t\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\n272: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n273: \t\t\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\n274: \t\t\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\n275: \t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\", ids, req.URL.Path)\n276: \t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\n277: \t\t\t\treturn\n278: \t\t\t}\n279: \t\t\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\n280: \t\t\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\n281: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\n282: \t\t} else {\n283: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n284: \t\t}\n285: \t}\n286: }\n287: \n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\n289: \tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\n290: \tif err != nil {\n291: \t\treturn false\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\n295: \treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\n296: }\n297: \n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\n300: \tfor _, id := range identities {\n301: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go%3FL51-300"
  }
}

  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "overlay / overlay rw,relatime,lowerdir=/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/5ZHQERATFB3TLQOKAIDHVOKUF7:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/YGIEGNIH4E6UEBWBISWTPABJMO:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/WERLUMBNR74HHNILAFXPK3LXWB:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/XSF7MLODBID6FOX33DGRBMNSZO:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/MTSYJF522VFXLL2J5E7XPAFGZC:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/GJTROT4WOQU4IBMFFDK5EAEU6K:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/53SM6SYBXMDSRYD7ZPP33RV2M2:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/Q4DU57NJQY6ESXVBJISOJLANYU:/var/lib/docker/overlay2/l/S763DLWTIDKVIYY327LNTX2QU2,upperdir=/var/lib/docker/overlay2/f191654416813a300ea8dff48a2b405512e665dcd77cf4b6373b9fd896043299/diff,workdir=/var/lib/docker/overlay2/f191654416813a300ea8dff48a2b405512e665dcd77cf4b6373b9fd896043299/work,nouserxattr 0 0\nmqueue /dev/mqueue mqueue rw,nosuid,nodev,noexec,relatime 0 0\n/dev/md1 /etc/resolv.conf xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0\n/dev/md1 /etc/hostname xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0\n/dev/md1 /etc/hosts xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0\n/dev/md0 /usr/local/bin/daytona ext4 ro,relatime,idmapped 0 0\n/dev/md0 /usr/local/lib/daytona-computer-use ext4 ro,relatime,idmapped 0 0\n/dev/md1 /var/lib/rancher/rke2 xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0\n/dev/md1 /var/lib/kubelet xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0\n/dev/md1 /var/lib/k0s xfs rw,relatime,idmapped,attr2,inode64,logbufs=8,logbsize=32k,prjquota 0 0"
}
