    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"encoding/json\"\n19: \t\"strconv\"\n20: \t\"strings\"\n21: \t\"time\"\n22: \n23: \tcore \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/core/v3\"\n24: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n25: \n26: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n27: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n28: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n29: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n30: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n31: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/lazy\"\n32: \tistioversion \"istio.io/istio/pkg/version\"\n33: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n34: )\n35: \n36: // IstioControlPlaneInstance defines the format Istio uses for when creating Envoy config.core.v3.ControlPlane.identifier\n37: type IstioControlPlaneInstance struct {\n38: \t// The Istio component type (e.g. \"istiod\")\n39: \tComponent string\n40: \t// The ID of the component instance\n41: \tID string\n42: \t// The Istio version\n43: \tInfo istioversion.BuildInfo\n44: }\n45: \n46: // Evaluate the controlPlane lazily in order to allow \"POD_NAME\" env var setting after running the process.\n47: var controlPlane = lazy.New(func() (*core.ControlPlane, error) {\n48: \t// The Pod Name (instance identity) is in PilotArgs, but not reachable globally nor from DiscoveryServer\n49: \tpodName := env.Register(\"POD_NAME\", \"\", \"\").Get()\n50: \tbyVersion, err := json.Marshal(IstioControlPlaneInstance{\n51: \t\tComponent: \"istiod\",\n52: \t\tID:        podName,\n53: \t\tInfo:      istioversion.Info,\n54: \t})\n55: \tif err != nil {\n56: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"XDS: Could not serialize control plane id: %v\", err)\n57: \t}\n58: \treturn &core.ControlPlane{Identifier: string(byVersion)}, nil\n59: })\n60: \n61: // ControlPlane identifies the instance and Istio version, based on the requested type URL\n62: func ControlPlane(typ string) *core.ControlPlane {\n63: \tif typ != TypeDebugSyncronization {\n64: \t\t// Currently only TypeDebugSyncronization utilizes this so don't both sending otherwise\n65: \t\treturn nil\n66: \t}\n67: \t// Error will never happen because the getter of lazy does not return error.\n68: \tcp, _ := controlPlane.Get()\n69: \treturn cp\n70: }\n71: \n72: func (s *DiscoveryServer) findGenerator(typeURL string, con *Connection) model.XdsResourceGenerator {\n73: \tif g, f := s.Generators[con.proxy.Metadata.Generator+\"/\"+typeURL]; f {\n74: \t\treturn g\n75: \t}\n76: \tif g, f := s.Generators[string(con.proxy.Type)+\"/\"+typeURL]; f {\n77: \t\treturn g\n78: \t}\n79: \n80: \tif g, f := s.Generators[typeURL]; f {\n81: \t\treturn g\n82: \t}\n83: \n84: \t// XdsResourceGenerator is the default generator for this connection. We want to allow\n85: \t// some types to use custom generators - for example EDS.\n86: \tg := con.proxy.XdsResourceGenerator\n87: \tif g == nil {\n88: \t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeURL, TypeDebugPrefix) {\n89: \t\t\tg = s.Generators[\"event\"]\n90: \t\t} else {\n91: \t\t\t// TODO move this to just directly using the resource TypeUrl\n92: \t\t\tg = s.Generators[\"api\"] // default to \"MCP\" generators - any type supported by store\n93: \t\t}\n94: \t}\n95: \treturn g\n96: }\n97: \n98: // Push an XDS resource for the given connection. Configuration will be generated\n99: // based on the passed in generator. Based on the updates field, generators may\n100: // choose to send partial or even no response if there are no changes.\n101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n102: \tif w == nil {\n103: \t\treturn nil\n104: \t}\n105: \tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n106: \tif gen == nil {\n107: \t\treturn nil\n108: \t}\n109: \n110: \tt0 := time.Now()\n111: \n112: \t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\n113: \t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\n114: \t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\n115: \t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n116: \t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n117: \tvar logFiltered string\n118: \tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !con.proxy.IsProxylessGrpc() {\n119: \t\tlogFiltered = \" filtered:\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\n120: \t\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\n121: \t\t\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\n122: \t\t\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\n123: \t\t}\n124: \t}\n125: \tres, logdata, err := gen.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\n126: \tinfo := \"\"\n127: \tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\n128: \t\tinfo = \" \" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\n129: \t}\n130: \tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\n131: \t\tinfo += logFiltered\n132: \t}\n133: \tif err != nil || res == nil {\n134: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n135: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: SKIP%s for node:%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, info)\n136: \t\t}\n137: \n138: \t\treturn err\n139: \t}\n140: \tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\n141: \n142: \tresp := &discovery.DiscoveryResponse{\n143: \t\tControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\n144: \t\tTypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,\n145: \t\t// TODO: send different version for incremental eds\n146: \t\tVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\n147: \t\tNonce:       nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\n148: \t\tResources:   xds.ResourcesToAny(res),\n149: \t}\n150: \n151: \tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\n152: \tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\n153: \n154: \tptype := \"PUSH\"\n155: \tif logdata.Incremental {\n156: \t\tptype = \"PUSH INC\"\n157: \t}\n158: \n159: \tif err := xds.Send(con, resp); err != nil {\n160: \t\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\n161: \t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\",\n162: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n163: \t\t}\n164: \t\treturn err\n165: \t}\n166: \n167: \tswitch {\n168: \tcase !req.Full:\n169: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n170: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n171: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n172: \t\t}\n173: \tdefault:\n174: \t\tdebug := \"\"\n175: \t\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n176: \t\t\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\n177: \t\t\tdebug = \" nonce:\" + resp.Nonce + \" version:\" + resp.VersionInfo\n178: \t\t}\n179: \t\tlog.Infof(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%v%s%s\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res),\n180: \t\t\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\n181: \t}\n182: \n183: \treturn nil\n184: }\n185: \n186: func ResourceSize(r model.Resources) int {\n187: \t// Approximate size by looking at the Any marshaled size. This avoids high cost\n188: \t// proto.Size, at the expense of slightly under counting.\n189: \tsize := 0\n190: \tfor _, r := range r {\n191: \t\tsize += len(r.Resource.Value)\n192: \t}\n193: \treturn size\n194: }\n195: \n196: // xdsNeedsPush checks for the common cases whether we need to push or not.\n197: func xdsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) (needsPush, definitive bool) {\n198: \tif proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel {\n199: \t\t// Not supported for ztunnel\n200: \t\treturn false, true\n201: \t}\n202: \tif req == nil {\n203: \t\treturn true, true\n204: \t}\n205: \t// If none set, we will always push\n206: \tif req.Forced {\n207: \t\treturn true, true\n208: \t}\n209: \n210: \t// We can not definitively say if we need to push or not based on generic checks, so we will push based\n211: \t// on the specific typeURL checks.\n212: \treturn false, false\n213: }\n214: \n215: // waypointNeedsPush checks if an incremental push is needed for CDS and LDS which normally do a full push.\n216: func waypointNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n217: \t// Waypoint proxies needs to be pushed for LDS and CDS on kind.Address changes.\n218: \t// Waypoint proxies have a matcher against pod IPs in them. Historically, any LDS change would do a full\n219: \t// push, recomputing push context. Doing that on every IP change doesn't scale, so we need these to remain\n220: \t// incremental pushes.\n221: \t// This allows waypoints only to push LDS on incremental pushes to Address type which would otherwise be skipped.\n222: \n223: \t// Waypoints need CDS updates on kind.Address changes\n224: \t// after implementing use-waypoint which decouples waypoint creation, wl pod creation\n225: \t// user specifying waypoint use. Without this we're not getting correct waypoint config\n226: \t// in a timely manner\n227: \treturn model.HasConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.Address)\n228: }\n229: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/xdsgen.go"
  }
}

  "path": "pilot/test/xds/fake.go",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: // Copyright Istio Authors\\n2: //\\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n6: //\\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n8: //\\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n13: // limitations under the License.\\n14: \\n15: package xds\\n16: \\n17: import (\\n18: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n19: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n20: \\t\\\"net\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\tendpoint \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/endpoint/v3\\\"\\n26: \\tdiscovery \\\"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"google.golang.org/grpc/test/bufconn\\\"\\n30: \\tauthorizationv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/authorization/v1\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake\\\"\\n34: \\tk8stesting \\\"k8s.io/client-go/testing\\\"\\n35: \\n36: \\tmeshconfig \\\"istio.io/api/mesh/v1alpha1\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/bootstrap\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway\\\"\\n40: \\tingress \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/ingress\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/memory\\\"\\n42: \\tkubesecrets \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/credentials/kube\\\"\\n43: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/core\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry\\\"\\n47: \\tkube \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller\\\"\\n48: \\tmemregistry \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/memory\\\"\\n49: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/xdsfake\\\"\\n50: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds\\\"\\n51: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\\\"\\n52: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n53: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/test/xdstest\\\"\\n54: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/adsc\\\"\\n55: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\\\"\\n56: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config\\\"\\n57: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/constants\\\"\\n58: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh\\\"\\n59: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh/meshwatcher\\\"\\n60: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/collections\\\"\\n61: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvk\\\"\\n62: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvr\\\"\\n63: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\\\"\\n64: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/keepalive\\\"\\n65: \\tkubelib \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube\\\"\\n66: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/krt\\\"\\n67: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/multicluster\\\"\\n68: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test\\\"\\n69: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test/util/retry\\\"\\n70: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n71: )\\n72: \\n73: type FakeOptions struct {\\n74: \\t// If provided, sets the name of the \\\"default\\\" or local cluster to the similaed pilots. (Defaults to opts.DefaultClusterName)\\n75: \\tDefaultClusterName cluster.ID\\n76: \\t// If provided, the minor version will be overridden for calls to GetKubernetesVersion to 1.minor\\n77: \\tKubernetesVersion string\\n78: \\t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\\n79: \\tKubernetesObjectsByCluster map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object\\n80: \\t// If provided, these objects will be used directly for the default cluster (\\\"Kubernetes\\\" or DefaultClusterName)\\n81: \\tKubernetesObjects []runtime.Object\\n82: \\t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\\n83: \\tKubernetesObjectStringByCluster map[cluster.ID]string\\n84: \\t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as objects for the default cluster (\\\"Kubernetes\\\" or DefaultClusterName)\\n85: \\tKubernetesObjectString string\\n86: \\t// If provided, these configs will be used directly\\n87: \\tConfigs []config.Config\\n88: \\t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as configs\\n89: \\tConfigString string\\n90: \\t// If provided, the ConfigString will be treated as a go template, with this as input params\\n91: \\tConfigTemplateInput any\\n92: \\t// If provided, this mesh config will be used\\n93: \\tMeshConfig      *meshconfig.MeshConfig\\n94: \\tNetworksWatcher mesh.NetworksWatcher\\n95: \\n96: \\t// Callback to modify the kube client before it is started\\n97: \\tKubeClientModifier func(c kubelib.Client)\\n98: \\n99: \\t// Override the default kube client constructor\\n100: \\tKubeClientBuilder func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client\\n101: \\n102: \\t// ListenerBuilder, if specified, allows making the server use the given\\n103: \\t// listener instead of a buffered conn.\\n104: \\tListenerBuilder func() (net.Listener, error)\\n105: \\n106: \\t// Time to debounce\\n107: \\t// By default, set to 0s to speed up tests\\n108: \\tDebounceTime time.Duration\\n109: \\n110: \\t// EnableFakeXDSUpdater will use a XDSUpdater that can be used to watch events\\n111: \\tEnableFakeXDSUpdater       bool\\n112: \\tDisableSecretAuthorization bool\\n113: \\tServices                   []*model.Service\\n114: \\tGateways                   []model.NetworkGateway\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: type FakeDiscoveryServer struct {\\n118: \\t*core.ConfigGenTest\\n119: \\tt              test.Failer\\n120: \\tDiscovery      *xds.DiscoveryServer\\n121: \\tDiscoveryDebug *http.ServeMux\\n122: \\tListener       net.Listener\\n123: \\tBufListener    *bufconn.Listener\\n124: \\tkubeClient     kubelib.Client\\n125: \\tKubeRegistry   *kube.FakeController\\n126: \\tXdsUpdater     model.XDSUpdater\\n127: \\tMemRegistry    *memregistry.ServiceDiscovery\\n128: }\\n129: \\n130: func NewFakeDiscoveryServer(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) *FakeDiscoveryServer {\\n131: \\tm := opts.MeshConfig\\n132: \\tif m == nil {\\n133: \\t\\tm = mesh.DefaultMeshConfig()\\n134: \\t}\\n135: \\n136: \\t// Init with a dummy environment, since we have a circular dependency with the env creation.\\n137: \\ts := xds.NewDiscoveryServer(model.NewEnvironment(), map[string]string{}, krt.GlobalDebugHandler)\\n138: \\t// Disable debounce to reduce test times\\n139: \\ts.DebounceOptions.DebounceAfter = opts.DebounceTime\\n140: \\t// Setup time to Now instead of process start to make logs not misleading\\n141: \\ts.DiscoveryStartTime = time.Now()\\n142: \\tt.Cleanup(s.Shutdown)\\n143: \\n144: \\tserviceHandler := func(_, curr *model.Service, _ model.Event) {\\n145: \\t\\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\\n146: \\t\\t\\tFull:           true,\\n147: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: string(curr.Hostname), Namespace: curr.Attributes.Namespace}),\\n148: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ServiceUpdate),\\n149: \\t\\t}\\n150: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\\n151: \\t}\\n152: \\n153: \\tif opts.DefaultClusterName == \\\"\\\" {\\n154: \\t\\topts.DefaultClusterName = constants.DefaultClusterName\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\tk8sObjects := getKubernetesObjects(t, opts)\\n157: \\tvar defaultKubeClient kubelib.Client\\n158: \\tvar defaultKubeController *kube.FakeController\\n159: \\tvar registries []serviceregistry.Instance\\n160: \\tif opts.NetworksWatcher != nil {\\n161: \\t\\topts.NetworksWatcher.AddNetworksHandler(func() {\\n162: \\t\\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\\n163: \\t\\t\\t\\tFull:   true,\\n164: \\t\\t\\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.NetworksTrigger),\\n165: \\t\\t\\t\\tForced: true,\\n166: \\t\\t\\t})\\n167: \\t\\t})\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\tvar xdsUpdater model.XDSUpdater = s\\n170: \\tif opts.EnableFakeXDSUpdater {\\n171: \\t\\txdsUpdater = xdsfake.NewWithDelegate(s)\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\tmc := multicluster.NewFakeController()\\n174: \\tcreds := kubesecrets.NewMulticluster(opts.DefaultClusterName, mc)\\n175: \\n176: \\tconfigController := memory.NewController(memory.MakeSkipValidation(collections.PilotGatewayAPI()))\\n177: \\tclientBuilder := opts.KubeClientBuilder\\n178: \\tif clientBuilder == nil {\\n179: \\t\\tclientBuilder = func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client {\\n180: \\t\\t\\treturn kubelib.NewFakeClientWithVersion(opts.KubernetesVersion, objects...)\\n181: \\t\\t}\\n182: \\t}\\n183: \\tfor k8sCluster, objs := range k8sObjects {\\n184: \\t\\tclient := clientBuilder(objs...)\\n185: \\t\\tif opts.KubeClientModifier != nil {\\n186: \\t\\t\\topts.KubeClientModifier(client)\\n187: \\t\\t}\\n188: \\t\\tk8s, _ := kube.NewFakeControllerWithOptions(t, kube.FakeControllerOptions{\\n189: \\t\\t\\tServiceHandler:  serviceHandler,\\n190: \\t\\t\\tClient:          client,\\n191: \\t\\t\\tClusterID:       k8sCluster,\\n192: \\t\\t\\tDomainSuffix:    \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n193: \\t\\t\\tXDSUpdater:      xdsUpdater,\\n194: \\t\\t\\tNetworksWatcher: opts.NetworksWatcher,\\n195: \\t\\t\\tSkipRun:         true,\\n196: \\t\\t\\tConfigCluster:   k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName,\\n197: \\t\\t\\tMeshWatcher:     meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m),\\n198: \\t\\t\\tCRDs: []schema.GroupVersionResource{\\n199: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Install all CRDs used (mostly in Ambient)\\n200: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.AuthorizationPolicy,\\n201: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.PeerAuthentication,\\n202: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.WorkloadEntry,\\n203: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.GatewayClass,\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.KubernetesGateway,\\n205: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.HTTPRoute,\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.GRPCRoute,\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.TCPRoute,\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.TLSRoute,\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.ReferenceGrant,\\n210: \\t\\t\\t\\tgvr.ServiceEntry,\\n211: \\t\\t\\t},\\n212: \\t\\t})\\n213: \\t\\tstop := test.NewStop(t)\\n214: \\t\\t// start default client informers after creating ingress/secret controllers\\n215: \\t\\tif defaultKubeClient == nil || k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName {\\n216: \\t\\t\\tdefaultKubeClient = client\\n217: \\t\\t\\tif opts.DisableSecretAuthorization {\\n218: \\t\\t\\t\\tDisableAuthorizationForSecret(defaultKubeClient.Kube().(*fake.Clientset))\\n219: \\t\\t\\t}\\n220: \\t\\t\\tdefaultKubeController = k8s\\n221: \\t\\t} else {\\n222: \\t\\t\\tclient.RunAndWait(stop)\\n223: \\t\\t}\\n224: \\t\\tregistries = append(registries, k8s)\\n225: \\t\\tmc.Add(k8sCluster, client, stop)\\n226: \\t}\\n227: \\n228: \\tstop := test.NewStop(t)\\n229: \\tingr := ingress.NewController(defaultKubeClient, meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m), kube.Options{\\n230: \\t\\tDomainSuffix: \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n231: \\t}, xdsUpdater)\\n232: \\tdefaultKubeClient.RunAndWait(stop)\\n233: \\n234: \\tvar gwc *gateway.Controller\\n235: \\tcg := core.NewConfigGenTest(t, core.TestOptions{\\n236: \\t\\tConfigs:             opts.Configs,\\n237: \\t\\tConfigString:        opts.ConfigString,\\n238: \\t\\tConfigTemplateInput: opts.ConfigTemplateInput,\\n239: \\t\\tConfigController:    configController,\\n240: \\t\\tMeshConfig:          m,\\n241: \\t\\tXDSUpdater:          xdsUpdater,\\n242: \\t\\tNetworksWatcher:     opts.NetworksWatcher,\\n243: \\t\\tServiceRegistries:   registries,\\n244: \\t\\tConfigStoreCaches:   []model.ConfigStoreController{ingr},\\n245: \\t\\tCreateConfigStore: func(c model.ConfigStoreController) model.ConfigStoreController {\\n246: \\t\\t\\tg := gateway.NewController(defaultKubeClient, func(class schema.GroupVersionResource, stop <-chan struct{}) bool {\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n248: \\t\\t\\t}, kube.Options{\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\tDomainSuffix: \\\"cluster.local\\\",\\n250: \\t\\t\\t}, xdsUpdater)\\n251: \\t\\t\\tgwc = g\\n252: \\t\\t\\treturn gwc\\n253: \\t\\t},\\n254: \\t\\tSkipRun:   true,\\n255: \\t\\tClusterID: opts.DefaultClusterName,\\n256: \\t\\tServices:  opts.Services,\\n257: \\t\\tGateways:  opts.Gateways,\\n258: \\t})\\n259: \\tcg.Registry.AppendServiceHandler(serviceHandler)\\n260: \\ts.Env = cg.Env()\\n261: \\ts.Env.GatewayAPIController = gwc\\n262: \\tif err := s.Env.InitNetworksManager(s); err != nil {\\n263: \\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n264: \\t}\\n265: \\n266: \\tbootstrap.InitGenerators(s, core.NewConfigGenerator(s.Cache), \\\"istio-system\\\", \\\"\\\", nil)\\n267: \\ts.Generators[v3.SecretType] = xds.NewSecretGen(creds, s.Cache, opts.DefaultClusterName, nil)\\n268: \\ts.Generators[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType].(*xds.EcdsGenerator).SetCredController(creds)\\n269: \\n270: \\tdebugMux := s.InitDebug(http.NewServeMux(), false, func() map[string]string {\\n271: \\t\\treturn nil\\n272: \\t})\\n273: \\n274: \\tmemRegistry := cg.MemRegistry\\n275: \\tmemRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\\n276: \\n277: \\t// Setup config handlers\\n278: \\t// TODO code re-use from server.go\\n279: \\tconfigHandler := func(_, curr config.Config, event model.Event) {\\n280: \\t\\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\\n281: \\t\\t\\tFull:           true,\\n282: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: gvk.MustToKind(curr.GroupVersionKind), Name: curr.Name, Namespace: curr.Namespace}),\\n283: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ConfigUpdate),\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t\\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\\n286: \\t}\\n287: \\tschemas := collections.Pilot.All()\\n288: \\tif features.EnableGatewayAPI {\\n289: \\t\\tschemas = collections.PilotGatewayAPI().All()\\n290: \\t}\\n291: \\tfor _, schema := range schemas {\\n292: \\t\\t// This resource type was handled in external/servicediscovery.go, no need to rehandle here.\\n293: \\t\\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.ServiceEntry {\\n294: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n295: \\t\\t}\\n296: \\t\\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.WorkloadEntry {\\n297: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n298: \\t\\t}\\n299: \\n300: \\t\\tcg.Store().RegisterEventHandler(schema.GroupVersionKind(), configHandler)\\n301: \\t}\\n302: \\tfor _, registry := range registries {\\n303: \\t\\tk8s, ok := registry.(*kube.FakeController)\\n304: \\t\\t// this closely matches what we do in serviceregistry/kube/controller/multicluster.go\\n305: \\t\\tif !ok || k8s.Cluster() != cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.Cluster() {\\n306: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n307: \\t\\t}\\n308: \\t\\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.AppendWorkloadHandler(k8s.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\\n309: \\t\\tk8s.AppendWorkloadHandler(cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\ts.WorkloadEntryController = autoregistration.NewController(cg.Store(), \\\"test\\\", keepalive.Infinity)\\n312: \\n313: \\tvar listener net.Listener\\n314: \\tif opts.ListenerBuilder != nil {\\n315: \\t\\tvar err error\\n316: \\t\\tif listener, err = opts.ListenerBuilder(); err != nil {\\n317: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n318: \\t\\t}\\n319: \\t} else {\\n320: \\t\\t// Start in memory gRPC listener\\n321: \\t\\tbuffer := 1024 * 1024\\n322: \\t\\tlistener = bufconn.Listen(buffer)\\n323: \\t}\\n324: \\n325: \\tgrpcServer := grpc.NewServer()\\n326: \\ts.Register(grpcServer)\\n327: \\tgo func() {\\n328: \\t\\tif err := grpcServer.Serve(listener); err != nil && !(err == grpc.ErrServerStopped || err.Error() == \\\"closed\\\") {\\n329: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatal(err)\\n330: \\t\\t}\\n331: \\t}()\\n332: \\tt.Cleanup(func() {\\n333: \\t\\tgrpcServer.Stop()\\n334: \\t\\t_ = listener.Close()\\n335: \\t})\\n336: \\t// Start the discovery server\\n337: \\ts.Start(stop)\\n338: \\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\\n339: \\t// Now that handlers are added, get everything started\\n340: \\tcg.Run()\\n341: \\tkubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\\\"fake\\\", stop,\\n342: \\t\\tcg.Registry.HasSynced,\\n343: \\t\\tcg.Store().HasSynced)\\n344: \\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.ResyncEDS()\\n345: \\n346: \\t// Send an update. This ensures that even if there are no configs provided, the push context is\\n347: \\t// initialized.\\n348: \\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Forced: true})\\n349: \\n350: \\t// Wait until initial updates are committed\\n351: \\tc := s.InboundUpdates.Load()\\n352: \\tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\\n353: \\t\\treturn s.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\\n354: \\t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\\n355: \\n356: \\t// Mark ourselves ready\\n357: \\ts.CachesSynced()\\n358: \\n359: \\tbufListener, _ := listener.(*bufconn.Listener)\\n360: \\tfake := &FakeDiscoveryServer{\\n361: \\t\\tt:              t,\\n362: \\t\\tDiscovery:      s,\\n363: \\t\\tDiscoveryDebug: debugMux,\\n364: \\t\\tListener:       listener,\\n365: \\t\\tBufListener:    bufListener,\\n366: \\t\\tConfigGenTest:  cg,\\n367: \\t\\tkubeClient:     defaultKubeClient,\\n368: \\t\\tKubeRegistry:   defaultKubeController,\\n369: \\t\\tXdsUpdater:     xdsUpdater,\\n370: \\t\\tMemRegistry:    memRegistry,\\n371: \\t}\\n372: \\n373: \\treturn fake\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) KubeClient() kubelib.Client {\\n377: \\treturn f.kubeClient\\n378: }\\n379: \\n380: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) PushContext() *model.PushContext {\\n381: \\treturn f.Env().PushContext()\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // ConnectADS starts an ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n385: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectADS() *xds.AdsTest {\\n386: \\tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\\\"buffcon\\\",\\n387: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n388: \\t\\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\\n389: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n390: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n391: \\t\\t}))\\n392: \\tif err != nil {\\n393: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"failed to connect: %v\\\", err)\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\treturn xds.NewAdsTest(f.t, conn)\\n396: }\\n397: \\n398: // ConnectDeltaADS starts a Delta ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n399: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectDeltaADS() *xds.DeltaAdsTest {\\n400: \\tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\\\"buffcon\\\",\\n401: \\t\\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\\n402: \\t\\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\\n403: \\t\\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n404: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n405: \\t\\t}))\\n406: \\tif err != nil {\\n407: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"failed to connect: %v\\\", err)\\n408: \\t}\\n409: \\treturn xds.NewDeltaAdsTest(f.t, conn)\\n410: }\\n411: \\n412: func APIWatches() []string {\\n413: \\twatches := []string{gvk.MeshConfig.String()}\\n414: \\tfor _, sch := range collections.Pilot.All() {\\n415: \\t\\twatches = append(watches, sch.GroupVersionKind().String())\\n416: \\t}\\n417: \\treturn watches\\n418: }\\n419: \\n420: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectUnstarted(p *model.Proxy, watch []string) *adsc.ADSC {\\n421: \\tf.t.Helper()\\n422: \\tp = f.SetupProxy(p)\\n423: \\tinitialWatch := []*discovery.DiscoveryRequest{}\\n424: \\tfor _, typeURL := range watch {\\n425: \\t\\tinitialWatch = append(initialWatch, &discovery.DiscoveryRequest{TypeUrl: typeURL})\\n426: \\t}\\n427: \\topts := []grpc.DialOption{grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials())}\\n428: \\tif f.BufListener != nil {\\n429: \\t\\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\\n430: \\t\\t\\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\\n431: \\t\\t}))\\n432: \\t}\\n433: \\tadscConn, err := adsc.New(f.Listener.Addr().String(), &adsc.ADSConfig{\\n434: \\t\\tConfig: adsc.Config{\\n435: \\t\\t\\tIP:        p.IPAddresses[0],\\n436: \\t\\t\\tNodeType:  p.Type,\\n437: \\t\\t\\tMeta:      p.Metadata.ToStruct(),\\n438: \\t\\t\\tLocality:  p.Locality,\\n439: \\t\\t\\tNamespace: p.ConfigNamespace,\\n440: \\t\\t\\tGrpcOpts:  opts,\\n441: \\t\\t},\\n442: \\t\\tInitialDiscoveryRequests: initialWatch,\\n443: \\t})\\n444: \\tif err != nil {\\n445: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"Error connecting: %v\\\", err)\\n446: \\t}\\n447: \\tf.t.Cleanup(func() {\\n448: \\t\\tadscConn.Close()\\n449: \\t})\\n450: \\treturn adscConn\\n451: }\\n452: \\n453: // Connect starts an ADS connection to the server using adsc. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\\n454: // watch can be configured to determine the resources to watch initially, and wait can be configured to determine what\\n455: // resources we should initially wait for.\\n456: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Connect(p *model.Proxy, watch []string, wait []string) *adsc.ADSC {\\n457: \\tf.t.Helper()\\n458: \\tif watch == nil {\\n459: \\t\\twatch = []string{v3.ClusterType}\\n460: \\t}\\n461: \\tadscConn := f.ConnectUnstarted(p, watch)\\n462: \\tif err := adscConn.Run(); err != nil {\\n463: \\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"ADSC: failed running: %v\\\", err)\\n464: \\t}\\n465: \\tif len(wait) > 0 {\\n466: \\t\\t_, err := adscConn.Wait(10*time.Second, wait...)\\n467: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n468: \\t\\t\\tf.t.Fatalf(\\\"Error getting initial for %v config: %v\\\", wait, err)\\n469: \\t\\t}\\n470: \\t}\\n471: \\treturn adscConn\\n472: }\\n473: \\n474: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Endpoints(p *model.Proxy) []*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment {\\n475: \\tloadAssignments := make([]*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment, 0)\\n476: \\tfor _, c := range xdstest.ExtractEdsClusterNames(f.Clusters(p)) {\\n477: \\t\\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(c, p, f.PushContext())\\n478: \\t\\tloadAssignments = append(loadAssignments, builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(f.Discovery.Env.EndpointIndex))\\n479: \\t}\\n480: \\treturn loadAssignments\\n481: }\\n482: \\n483: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) T() test.Failer {\\n484: \\treturn f.t\\n485: }\\n486: \\n487: // EnsureSynced checks that all ConfigUpdates sent have been established\\n488: // This does NOT ensure that the change has been sent to all proxies; only that PushContext is updated\\n489: // Typically, if trying to ensure changes are sent, its better to wait for the push event.\\n490: \\n491: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) EnsureSynced(t test.Failer) {\\n492: \\tc := f.Discovery.InboundUpdates.Load()\\n493: \\tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\\n494: \\t\\treturn f.Discovery.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\\n495: \\t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\\n496: }\\n497: \\n498: func getKubernetesObjects(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object {\\n499: \\tobjects := map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{}\\n500: \\n501: \\tif len(opts.KubernetesObjects) > 0 {\\n502: \\t\\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], opts.KubernetesObjects...)\\n503: \\t}\\n504: \\tif len(opts.KubernetesObjectString) > 0 {\\n505: \\t\\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(opts.KubernetesObjectString)\\n506: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n507: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectString: %v\\\", err)\\n508: \\t\\t}\\n509: \\t\\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], parsed...)\\n510: \\t}\\n511: \\tfor k8sCluster, objectStr := range opts.KubernetesObjectStringByCluster {\\n512: \\t\\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(objectStr)\\n513: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n514: \\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectStringByCluster for %s: %v\\\", k8sCluster, err)\\n515: \\t\\t}\\n516: \\t\\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], parsed...)\\n517: \\t}\\n518: \\tfor k8sCluster, clusterObjs := range opts.KubernetesObjectsByCluster {\\n519: \\t\\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], clusterObjs...)\\n520: \\t}\\n521: \\n522: \\tif len(objects) == 0 {\\n523: \\t\\treturn map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{opts.DefaultClusterName: {}}\\n524: \\t}\\n525: \\n526: \\treturn objects\\n527: }\\n528: \\n529: func kubernetesObjectsFromString(s string) ([]runtime.Object, error) {\\n530: \\tvar objects []runtime.Object\\n531: \\tdecode := kubelib.IstioCodec.UniversalDeserializer().Decode\\n532: \\tobjectStrs := strings.Split(s, \\\"\\\\n---\\\\n\\\")\\n533: \\tfor _, s := range objectStrs {\\n534: \\t\\tif len(strings.TrimSpace(s)) == 0 {\\n535: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n536: \\t\\t}\\n537: \\t\\to, _, err := decode([]byte(s), nil, nil)\\n538: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n539: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed deserializing kubernetes object: %v (%v)\\\", err, s)\\n540: \\t\\t}\\n541: \\t\\tobjects = append(objects, o)\\n542: \\t}\\n543: \\treturn objects, nil\\n544: }\\n545: \\n546: // DisableAuthorizationForSecret makes the authorization check always pass. Should be used only for tests.\\n547: func DisableAuthorizationForSecret(fake *fake.Clientset) {\\n548: \\tfake.Fake.PrependReactor(\\\"create\\\", \\\"subjectaccessreviews\\\", func(action k8stesting.Action) (bool, runtime.Object, error) {\\n549: \\t\\treturn true, &authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReview{\\n550: \\t\\t\\tStatus: authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReviewStatus{\\n551: \\t\\t\\t\\tAllowed: true,\\n552: \\t\\t\\t},\\n553: \\t\\t}, nil\\n554: \\t})\\n555: }\\n556: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/test/xds/fake.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // Copyright Istio Authors\n2: //\n3: // Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n4: // you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n5: // You may obtain a copy of the License at\n6: //\n7: //     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n8: //\n9: // Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n10: // distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n11: // WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n12: // See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \t\"context\"\n19: \t\"fmt\"\n20: \t\"net\"\n21: \t\"net/http\"\n22: \t\"strings\"\n23: \t\"time\"\n24: \n25: \tendpoint \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/config/endpoint/v3\"\n26: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n27: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n28: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\"\n29: \t\"google.golang.org/grpc/test/bufconn\"\n30: \tauthorizationv1 \"k8s.io/api/authorization/v1\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake\"\n34: \tk8stesting \"k8s.io/client-go/testing\"\n35: \n36: \tmeshconfig \"istio.io/api/mesh/v1alpha1\"\n37: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/autoregistration\"\n38: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/bootstrap\"\n39: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/gateway\"\n40: \tingress \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/kube/ingress\"\n41: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/config/memory\"\n42: \tkubesecrets \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/credentials/kube\"\n43: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n44: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n45: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/core\"\n46: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry\"\n47: \tkube \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/kube/controller\"\n48: \tmemregistry \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/memory\"\n49: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/serviceregistry/util/xdsfake\"\n50: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds\"\n51: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/endpoints\"\n52: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n53: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/test/xdstest\"\n54: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/adsc\"\n55: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n56: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config\"\n57: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/constants\"\n58: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh\"\n59: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/mesh/meshwatcher\"\n60: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/collections\"\n61: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvk\"\n62: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/gvr\"\n63: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n64: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/keepalive\"\n65: \tkubelib \"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube\"\n66: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/krt\"\n67: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/kube/multicluster\"\n68: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test\"\n69: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/test/util/retry\"\n70: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n71: )\n72: \n73: type FakeOptions struct {\n74: \t// If provided, sets the name of the \"default\" or local cluster to the similaed pilots. (Defaults to opts.DefaultClusterName)\n75: \tDefaultClusterName cluster.ID\n76: \t// If provided, the minor version will be overridden for calls to GetKubernetesVersion to 1.minor\n77: \tKubernetesVersion string\n78: \t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\n79: \tKubernetesObjectsByCluster map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object\n80: \t// If provided, these objects will be used directly for the default cluster (\"Kubernetes\" or DefaultClusterName)\n81: \tKubernetesObjects []runtime.Object\n82: \t// If provided, a service registry with the name of each map key will be created with the given objects.\n83: \tKubernetesObjectStringByCluster map[cluster.ID]string\n84: \t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as objects for the default cluster (\"Kubernetes\" or DefaultClusterName)\n85: \tKubernetesObjectString string\n86: \t// If provided, these configs will be used directly\n87: \tConfigs []config.Config\n88: \t// If provided, the yaml string will be parsed and used as configs\n89: \tConfigString string\n90: \t// If provided, the ConfigString will be treated as a go template, with this as input params\n91: \tConfigTemplateInput any\n92: \t// If provided, this mesh config will be used\n93: \tMeshConfig      *meshconfig.MeshConfig\n94: \tNetworksWatcher mesh.NetworksWatcher\n95: \n96: \t// Callback to modify the kube client before it is started\n97: \tKubeClientModifier func(c kubelib.Client)\n98: \n99: \t// Override the default kube client constructor\n100: \tKubeClientBuilder func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client\n101: \n102: \t// ListenerBuilder, if specified, allows making the server use the given\n103: \t// listener instead of a buffered conn.\n104: \tListenerBuilder func() (net.Listener, error)\n105: \n106: \t// Time to debounce\n107: \t// By default, set to 0s to speed up tests\n108: \tDebounceTime time.Duration\n109: \n110: \t// EnableFakeXDSUpdater will use a XDSUpdater that can be used to watch events\n111: \tEnableFakeXDSUpdater       bool\n112: \tDisableSecretAuthorization bool\n113: \tServices                   []*model.Service\n114: \tGateways                   []model.NetworkGateway\n115: }\n116: \n117: type FakeDiscoveryServer struct {\n118: \t*core.ConfigGenTest\n119: \tt              test.Failer\n120: \tDiscovery      *xds.DiscoveryServer\n121: \tDiscoveryDebug *http.ServeMux\n122: \tListener       net.Listener\n123: \tBufListener    *bufconn.Listener\n124: \tkubeClient     kubelib.Client\n125: \tKubeRegistry   *kube.FakeController\n126: \tXdsUpdater     model.XDSUpdater\n127: \tMemRegistry    *memregistry.ServiceDiscovery\n128: }\n129: \n130: func NewFakeDiscoveryServer(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) *FakeDiscoveryServer {\n131: \tm := opts.MeshConfig\n132: \tif m == nil {\n133: \t\tm = mesh.DefaultMeshConfig()\n134: \t}\n135: \n136: \t// Init with a dummy environment, since we have a circular dependency with the env creation.\n137: \ts := xds.NewDiscoveryServer(model.NewEnvironment(), map[string]string{}, krt.GlobalDebugHandler)\n138: \t// Disable debounce to reduce test times\n139: \ts.DebounceOptions.DebounceAfter = opts.DebounceTime\n140: \t// Setup time to Now instead of process start to make logs not misleading\n141: \ts.DiscoveryStartTime = time.Now()\n142: \tt.Cleanup(s.Shutdown)\n143: \n144: \tserviceHandler := func(_, curr *model.Service, _ model.Event) {\n145: \t\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\n146: \t\t\tFull:           true,\n147: \t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: string(curr.Hostname), Namespace: curr.Attributes.Namespace}),\n148: \t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ServiceUpdate),\n149: \t\t}\n150: \t\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\n151: \t}\n152: \n153: \tif opts.DefaultClusterName == \"\" {\n154: \t\topts.DefaultClusterName = constants.DefaultClusterName\n155: \t}\n156: \tk8sObjects := getKubernetesObjects(t, opts)\n157: \tvar defaultKubeClient kubelib.Client\n158: \tvar defaultKubeController *kube.FakeController\n159: \tvar registries []serviceregistry.Instance\n160: \tif opts.NetworksWatcher != nil {\n161: \t\topts.NetworksWatcher.AddNetworksHandler(func() {\n162: \t\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n163: \t\t\t\tFull:   true,\n164: \t\t\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.NetworksTrigger),\n165: \t\t\t\tForced: true,\n166: \t\t\t})\n167: \t\t})\n168: \t}\n169: \tvar xdsUpdater model.XDSUpdater = s\n170: \tif opts.EnableFakeXDSUpdater {\n171: \t\txdsUpdater = xdsfake.NewWithDelegate(s)\n172: \t}\n173: \tmc := multicluster.NewFakeController()\n174: \tcreds := kubesecrets.NewMulticluster(opts.DefaultClusterName, mc)\n175: \n176: \tconfigController := memory.NewController(memory.MakeSkipValidation(collections.PilotGatewayAPI()))\n177: \tclientBuilder := opts.KubeClientBuilder\n178: \tif clientBuilder == nil {\n179: \t\tclientBuilder = func(objects ...runtime.Object) kubelib.Client {\n180: \t\t\treturn kubelib.NewFakeClientWithVersion(opts.KubernetesVersion, objects...)\n181: \t\t}\n182: \t}\n183: \tfor k8sCluster, objs := range k8sObjects {\n184: \t\tclient := clientBuilder(objs...)\n185: \t\tif opts.KubeClientModifier != nil {\n186: \t\t\topts.KubeClientModifier(client)\n187: \t\t}\n188: \t\tk8s, _ := kube.NewFakeControllerWithOptions(t, kube.FakeControllerOptions{\n189: \t\t\tServiceHandler:  serviceHandler,\n190: \t\t\tClient:          client,\n191: \t\t\tClusterID:       k8sCluster,\n192: \t\t\tDomainSuffix:    \"cluster.local\",\n193: \t\t\tXDSUpdater:      xdsUpdater,\n194: \t\t\tNetworksWatcher: opts.NetworksWatcher,\n195: \t\t\tSkipRun:         true,\n196: \t\t\tConfigCluster:   k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName,\n197: \t\t\tMeshWatcher:     meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m),\n198: \t\t\tCRDs: []schema.GroupVersionResource{\n199: \t\t\t\t// Install all CRDs used (mostly in Ambient)\n200: \t\t\t\tgvr.AuthorizationPolicy,\n201: \t\t\t\tgvr.PeerAuthentication,\n202: \t\t\t\tgvr.WorkloadEntry,\n203: \t\t\t\tgvr.GatewayClass,\n204: \t\t\t\tgvr.KubernetesGateway,\n205: \t\t\t\tgvr.HTTPRoute,\n206: \t\t\t\tgvr.GRPCRoute,\n207: \t\t\t\tgvr.TCPRoute,\n208: \t\t\t\tgvr.TLSRoute,\n209: \t\t\t\tgvr.ReferenceGrant,\n210: \t\t\t\tgvr.ServiceEntry,\n211: \t\t\t},\n212: \t\t})\n213: \t\tstop := test.NewStop(t)\n214: \t\t// start default client informers after creating ingress/secret controllers\n215: \t\tif defaultKubeClient == nil || k8sCluster == opts.DefaultClusterName {\n216: \t\t\tdefaultKubeClient = client\n217: \t\t\tif opts.DisableSecretAuthorization {\n218: \t\t\t\tDisableAuthorizationForSecret(defaultKubeClient.Kube().(*fake.Clientset))\n219: \t\t\t}\n220: \t\t\tdefaultKubeController = k8s\n221: \t\t} else {\n222: \t\t\tclient.RunAndWait(stop)\n223: \t\t}\n224: \t\tregistries = append(registries, k8s)\n225: \t\tmc.Add(k8sCluster, client, stop)\n226: \t}\n227: \n228: \tstop := test.NewStop(t)\n229: \tingr := ingress.NewController(defaultKubeClient, meshwatcher.NewTestWatcher(m), kube.Options{\n230: \t\tDomainSuffix: \"cluster.local\",\n231: \t}, xdsUpdater)\n232: \tdefaultKubeClient.RunAndWait(stop)\n233: \n234: \tvar gwc *gateway.Controller\n235: \tcg := core.NewConfigGenTest(t, core.TestOptions{\n236: \t\tConfigs:             opts.Configs,\n237: \t\tConfigString:        opts.ConfigString,\n238: \t\tConfigTemplateInput: opts.ConfigTemplateInput,\n239: \t\tConfigController:    configController,\n240: \t\tMeshConfig:          m,\n241: \t\tXDSUpdater:          xdsUpdater,\n242: \t\tNetworksWatcher:     opts.NetworksWatcher,\n243: \t\tServiceRegistries:   registries,\n244: \t\tConfigStoreCaches:   []model.ConfigStoreController{ingr},\n245: \t\tCreateConfigStore: func(c model.ConfigStoreController) model.ConfigStoreController {\n246: \t\t\tg := gateway.NewController(defaultKubeClient, func(class schema.GroupVersionResource, stop <-chan struct{}) bool {\n247: \t\t\t\treturn true\n248: \t\t\t}, kube.Options{\n249: \t\t\t\tDomainSuffix: \"cluster.local\",\n250: \t\t\t}, xdsUpdater)\n251: \t\t\tgwc = g\n252: \t\t\treturn gwc\n253: \t\t},\n254: \t\tSkipRun:   true,\n255: \t\tClusterID: opts.DefaultClusterName,\n256: \t\tServices:  opts.Services,\n257: \t\tGateways:  opts.Gateways,\n258: \t})\n259: \tcg.Registry.AppendServiceHandler(serviceHandler)\n260: \ts.Env = cg.Env()\n261: \ts.Env.GatewayAPIController = gwc\n262: \tif err := s.Env.InitNetworksManager(s); err != nil {\n263: \t\tt.Fatal(err)\n264: \t}\n265: \n266: \tbootstrap.InitGenerators(s, core.NewConfigGenerator(s.Cache), \"istio-system\", \"\", nil)\n267: \ts.Generators[v3.SecretType] = xds.NewSecretGen(creds, s.Cache, opts.DefaultClusterName, nil)\n268: \ts.Generators[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType].(*xds.EcdsGenerator).SetCredController(creds)\n269: \n270: \tdebugMux := s.InitDebug(http.NewServeMux(), false, func() map[string]string {\n271: \t\treturn nil\n272: \t})\n273: \n274: \tmemRegistry := cg.MemRegistry\n275: \tmemRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\n276: \n277: \t// Setup config handlers\n278: \t// TODO code re-use from server.go\n279: \tconfigHandler := func(_, curr config.Config, event model.Event) {\n280: \t\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\n281: \t\t\tFull:           true,\n282: \t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: gvk.MustToKind(curr.GroupVersionKind), Name: curr.Name, Namespace: curr.Namespace}),\n283: \t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ConfigUpdate),\n284: \t\t}\n285: \t\ts.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\n286: \t}\n287: \tschemas := collections.Pilot.All()\n288: \tif features.EnableGatewayAPI {\n289: \t\tschemas = collections.PilotGatewayAPI().All()\n290: \t}\n291: \tfor _, schema := range schemas {\n292: \t\t// This resource type was handled in external/servicediscovery.go, no need to rehandle here.\n293: \t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.ServiceEntry {\n294: \t\t\tcontinue\n295: \t\t}\n296: \t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.WorkloadEntry {\n297: \t\t\tcontinue\n298: \t\t}\n299: \n300: \t\tcg.Store().RegisterEventHandler(schema.GroupVersionKind(), configHandler)\n301: \t}\n302: \tfor _, registry := range registries {\n303: \t\tk8s, ok := registry.(*kube.FakeController)\n304: \t\t// this closely matches what we do in serviceregistry/kube/controller/multicluster.go\n305: \t\tif !ok || k8s.Cluster() != cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.Cluster() {\n306: \t\t\tcontinue\n307: \t\t}\n308: \t\tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.AppendWorkloadHandler(k8s.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\n309: \t\tk8s.AppendWorkloadHandler(cg.ServiceEntryRegistry.WorkloadInstanceHandler)\n310: \t}\n311: \ts.WorkloadEntryController = autoregistration.NewController(cg.Store(), \"test\", keepalive.Infinity)\n312: \n313: \tvar listener net.Listener\n314: \tif opts.ListenerBuilder != nil {\n315: \t\tvar err error\n316: \t\tif listener, err = opts.ListenerBuilder(); err != nil {\n317: \t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n318: \t\t}\n319: \t} else {\n320: \t\t// Start in memory gRPC listener\n321: \t\tbuffer := 1024 * 1024\n322: \t\tlistener = bufconn.Listen(buffer)\n323: \t}\n324: \n325: \tgrpcServer := grpc.NewServer()\n326: \ts.Register(grpcServer)\n327: \tgo func() {\n328: \t\tif err := grpcServer.Serve(listener); err != nil && !(err == grpc.ErrServerStopped || err.Error() == \"closed\") {\n329: \t\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n330: \t\t}\n331: \t}()\n332: \tt.Cleanup(func() {\n333: \t\tgrpcServer.Stop()\n334: \t\t_ = listener.Close()\n335: \t})\n336: \t// Start the discovery server\n337: \ts.Start(stop)\n338: \tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.XdsUpdater = s\n339: \t// Now that handlers are added, get everything started\n340: \tcg.Run()\n341: \tkubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\"fake\", stop,\n342: \t\tcg.Registry.HasSynced,\n343: \t\tcg.Store().HasSynced)\n344: \tcg.ServiceEntryRegistry.ResyncEDS()\n345: \n346: \t// Send an update. This ensures that even if there are no configs provided, the push context is\n347: \t// initialized.\n348: \ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Forced: true})\n349: \n350: \t// Wait until initial updates are committed\n351: \tc := s.InboundUpdates.Load()\n352: \tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\n353: \t\treturn s.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\n354: \t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\n355: \n356: \t// Mark ourselves ready\n357: \ts.CachesSynced()\n358: \n359: \tbufListener, _ := listener.(*bufconn.Listener)\n360: \tfake := &FakeDiscoveryServer{\n361: \t\tt:              t,\n362: \t\tDiscovery:      s,\n363: \t\tDiscoveryDebug: debugMux,\n364: \t\tListener:       listener,\n365: \t\tBufListener:    bufListener,\n366: \t\tConfigGenTest:  cg,\n367: \t\tkubeClient:     defaultKubeClient,\n368: \t\tKubeRegistry:   defaultKubeController,\n369: \t\tXdsUpdater:     xdsUpdater,\n370: \t\tMemRegistry:    memRegistry,\n371: \t}\n372: \n373: \treturn fake\n374: }\n375: \n376: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) KubeClient() kubelib.Client {\n377: \treturn f.kubeClient\n378: }\n379: \n380: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) PushContext() *model.PushContext {\n381: \treturn f.Env().PushContext()\n382: }\n383: \n384: // ConnectADS starts an ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\n385: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectADS() *xds.AdsTest {\n386: \tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\"buffcon\",\n387: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n388: \t\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\n389: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\n390: \t\t\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\n391: \t\t}))\n392: \tif err != nil {\n393: \t\tf.t.Fatalf(\"failed to connect: %v\", err)\n394: \t}\n395: \treturn xds.NewAdsTest(f.t, conn)\n396: }\n397: \n398: // ConnectDeltaADS starts a Delta ADS connection to the server. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\n399: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectDeltaADS() *xds.DeltaAdsTest {\n400: \tconn, err := grpc.Dial(\"buffcon\",\n401: \t\tgrpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()),\n402: \t\tgrpc.WithBlock(),\n403: \t\tgrpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\n404: \t\t\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\n405: \t\t}))\n406: \tif err != nil {\n407: \t\tf.t.Fatalf(\"failed to connect: %v\", err)\n408: \t}\n409: \treturn xds.NewDeltaAdsTest(f.t, conn)\n410: }\n411: \n412: func APIWatches() []string {\n413: \twatches := []string{gvk.MeshConfig.String()}\n414: \tfor _, sch := range collections.Pilot.All() {\n415: \t\twatches = append(watches, sch.GroupVersionKind().String())\n416: \t}\n417: \treturn watches\n418: }\n419: \n420: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) ConnectUnstarted(p *model.Proxy, watch []string) *adsc.ADSC {\n421: \tf.t.Helper()\n422: \tp = f.SetupProxy(p)\n423: \tinitialWatch := []*discovery.DiscoveryRequest{}\n424: \tfor _, typeURL := range watch {\n425: \t\tinitialWatch = append(initialWatch, &discovery.DiscoveryRequest{TypeUrl: typeURL})\n426: \t}\n427: \topts := []grpc.DialOption{grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials())}\n428: \tif f.BufListener != nil {\n429: \t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithContextDialer(func(context.Context, string) (net.Conn, error) {\n430: \t\t\treturn f.BufListener.Dial()\n431: \t\t}))\n432: \t}\n433: \tadscConn, err := adsc.New(f.Listener.Addr().String(), &adsc.ADSConfig{\n434: \t\tConfig: adsc.Config{\n435: \t\t\tIP:        p.IPAddresses[0],\n436: \t\t\tNodeType:  p.Type,\n437: \t\t\tMeta:      p.Metadata.ToStruct(),\n438: \t\t\tLocality:  p.Locality,\n439: \t\t\tNamespace: p.ConfigNamespace,\n440: \t\t\tGrpcOpts:  opts,\n441: \t\t},\n442: \t\tInitialDiscoveryRequests: initialWatch,\n443: \t})\n444: \tif err != nil {\n445: \t\tf.t.Fatalf(\"Error connecting: %v\", err)\n446: \t}\n447: \tf.t.Cleanup(func() {\n448: \t\tadscConn.Close()\n449: \t})\n450: \treturn adscConn\n451: }\n452: \n453: // Connect starts an ADS connection to the server using adsc. It will automatically be cleaned up when the test ends\n454: // watch can be configured to determine the resources to watch initially, and wait can be configured to determine what\n455: // resources we should initially wait for.\n456: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Connect(p *model.Proxy, watch []string, wait []string) *adsc.ADSC {\n457: \tf.t.Helper()\n458: \tif watch == nil {\n459: \t\twatch = []string{v3.ClusterType}\n460: \t}\n461: \tadscConn := f.ConnectUnstarted(p, watch)\n462: \tif err := adscConn.Run(); err != nil {\n463: \t\tf.t.Fatalf(\"ADSC: failed running: %v\", err)\n464: \t}\n465: \tif len(wait) > 0 {\n466: \t\t_, err := adscConn.Wait(10*time.Second, wait...)\n467: \t\tif err != nil {\n468: \t\t\tf.t.Fatalf(\"Error getting initial for %v config: %v\", wait, err)\n469: \t\t}\n470: \t}\n471: \treturn adscConn\n472: }\n473: \n474: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) Endpoints(p *model.Proxy) []*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment {\n475: \tloadAssignments := make([]*endpoint.ClusterLoadAssignment, 0)\n476: \tfor _, c := range xdstest.ExtractEdsClusterNames(f.Clusters(p)) {\n477: \t\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(c, p, f.PushContext())\n478: \t\tloadAssignments = append(loadAssignments, builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(f.Discovery.Env.EndpointIndex))\n479: \t}\n480: \treturn loadAssignments\n481: }\n482: \n483: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) T() test.Failer {\n484: \treturn f.t\n485: }\n486: \n487: // EnsureSynced checks that all ConfigUpdates sent have been established\n488: // This does NOT ensure that the change has been sent to all proxies; only that PushContext is updated\n489: // Typically, if trying to ensure changes are sent, its better to wait for the push event.\n490: \n491: func (f *FakeDiscoveryServer) EnsureSynced(t test.Failer) {\n492: \tc := f.Discovery.InboundUpdates.Load()\n493: \tretry.UntilOrFail(t, func() bool {\n494: \t\treturn f.Discovery.CommittedUpdates.Load() >= c\n495: \t}, retry.Delay(time.Millisecond))\n496: }\n497: \n498: func getKubernetesObjects(t test.Failer, opts FakeOptions) map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object {\n499: \tobjects := map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{}\n500: \n501: \tif len(opts.KubernetesObjects) > 0 {\n502: \t\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], opts.KubernetesObjects...)\n503: \t}\n504: \tif len(opts.KubernetesObjectString) > 0 {\n505: \t\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(opts.KubernetesObjectString)\n506: \t\tif err != nil {\n507: \t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectString: %v\", err)\n508: \t\t}\n509: \t\tobjects[opts.DefaultClusterName] = append(objects[opts.DefaultClusterName], parsed...)\n510: \t}\n511: \tfor k8sCluster, objectStr := range opts.KubernetesObjectStringByCluster {\n512: \t\tparsed, err := kubernetesObjectsFromString(objectStr)\n513: \t\tif err != nil {\n514: \t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed parsing KubernetesObjectStringByCluster for %s: %v\", k8sCluster, err)\n515: \t\t}\n516: \t\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], parsed...)\n517: \t}\n518: \tfor k8sCluster, clusterObjs := range opts.KubernetesObjectsByCluster {\n519: \t\tobjects[k8sCluster] = append(objects[k8sCluster], clusterObjs...)\n520: \t}\n521: \n522: \tif len(objects) == 0 {\n523: \t\treturn map[cluster.ID][]runtime.Object{opts.DefaultClusterName: {}}\n524: \t}\n525: \n526: \treturn objects\n527: }\n528: \n529: func kubernetesObjectsFromString(s string) ([]runtime.Object, error) {\n530: \tvar objects []runtime.Object\n531: \tdecode := kubelib.IstioCodec.UniversalDeserializer().Decode\n532: \tobjectStrs := strings.Split(s, \"\\n---\\n\")\n533: \tfor _, s := range objectStrs {\n534: \t\tif len(strings.TrimSpace(s)) == 0 {\n535: \t\t\tcontinue\n536: \t\t}\n537: \t\to, _, err := decode([]byte(s), nil, nil)\n538: \t\tif err != nil {\n539: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed deserializing kubernetes object: %v (%v)\", err, s)\n540: \t\t}\n541: \t\tobjects = append(objects, o)\n542: \t}\n543: \treturn objects, nil\n544: }\n545: \n546: // DisableAuthorizationForSecret makes the authorization check always pass. Should be used only for tests.\n547: func DisableAuthorizationForSecret(fake *fake.Clientset) {\n548: \tfake.Fake.PrependReactor(\"create\", \"subjectaccessreviews\", func(action k8stesting.Action) (bool, runtime.Object, error) {\n549: \t\treturn true, &authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReview{\n550: \t\t\tStatus: authorizationv1.SubjectAccessReviewStatus{\n551: \t\t\t\tAllowed: true,\n552: \t\t\t},\n553: \t\t}, nil\n554: \t})\n555: }\n556: ",

  }
}

func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\\n233: \\tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\\n234: ) {\\n235: \\ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\\n236: \\t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\\n237: \\tif internalMux != nil {\\n238: \\t\\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\\n239: \\t}\\n240: \\t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\\n241: \\tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\\n242: }\\n243: \\n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\\n245: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n246: \\t\\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\\n247: \\t\\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\\n248: \\t\\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\\n249: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n250: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n251: \\t\\t}\\n252: \\t\\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\\n253: \\t\\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\\n254: \\t\\tvar ids []string\\n255: \\t\\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\\n256: \\t\\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\\n257: \\t\\t\\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\\n258: \\t\\t\\t// If one authenticator passes, return\\n259: \\t\\t\\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\\n260: \\t\\t\\t\\tids = u.Identities\\n261: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n262: \\t\\t\\t}\\n263: \\t\\t\\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Authenticator %s: %v\\\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\\n264: \\t\\t}\\n265: \\t\\tif ids == nil {\\n266: \\t\\t\\tistiolog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\\\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\\n267: \\t\\t\\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \\\"authentication failure).\\n268: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\\n269: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n270: \\t\\t}\\n271: \\t\\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\\n272: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n273: \\t\\t\\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\\n274: \\t\\t\\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\\n275: \\t\\t\\t\\tistiolog.Warnf(\\\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\\\", ids, req.URL.Path)\\n276: \\t\\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\\n277: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n278: \\t\\t\\t}\\n279: \\t\\t\\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\\n280: \\t\\t\\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\\n281: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\\n282: \\t\\t} else {\\n283: \\t\\t\\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n284: \\t\\t}\\n285: \\t}\\n286: }\\n287: \\n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\\n289: \\tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\\n290: \\tif err != nil {\\n291: \\t\\treturn false\\n292: \\t}\\n293: \\n294: \\tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\\n295: \\treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\\n296: }\\n297: \\n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\\n300: \\tfor _, id := range identities }\n160: \n161: // InitDebug initializes the debug handlers and adds a debug in-memory registry.\n162: func (s *DiscoveryServer) InitDebug(\n163: \tmux *http.ServeMux,\n164: \tenableProfiling bool,\n165: \tfetchWebhook func() map[string]string,\n166: ) *http.ServeMux {\n167: \tinternalMux := http.NewServeMux()\n168: \ts.AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux, enableProfiling, fetchWebhook)\n169: \treturn internalMux\n170: }\n171: \n172: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AddDebugHandlers(mux, internalMux *http.ServeMux, enableProfiling bool, webhook func() map[string]string) {\n173: \t// Debug handlers on HTTP ports are added for backward compatibility.\n174: \t// They will be exposed on XDS-over-TLS in future releases.\n175: \tif !features.EnableDebugOnHTTP {\n176: \t\treturn\n177: \t}\n178: \n179: \tif enableProfiling {\n180: \t\truntime.SetMutexProfileFraction(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n181: \t\truntime.SetBlockProfileRate(features.MutexProfileFraction)\n182: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/\", \"Displays pprof index\", pprof.Index)\n183: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/cmdline\", \"The command line invocation of the current program\", pprof.Cmdline)\n184: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/profile\", \"CPU profile\", pprof.Profile)\n185: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/symbol\", \"Symbol looks up the program counters listed in the request\", pprof.Symbol)\n186: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pprof/trace\", \"A trace of execution of the current program.\", pprof.Trace)\n187: \t}\n188: \n189: \tmux.HandleFunc(\"/debug\", s.Debug)\n190: \n191: \tif features.EnableUnsafeAdminEndpoints {\n192: \t\ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/force_disconnect\", \"Disconnects a proxy from this Pilot\", s.forceDisconnect)\n193: \t}\n194: \n195: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ecdsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ECDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"ecds\"))\n196: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/edsz\", \"Status and debug interface for EDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"eds\"))\n197: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ndsz\", \"Status and debug interface for NDS\", s.typedConfigDumpHandler(\"nds\"))\n198: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz\", \"Status and debug interface for ADS\", s.adsz)\n199: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/adsz?push=true\", \"Initiates push of the current state to all connected endpoints\", s.adsz)\n200: \n201: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/syncz\", \"Synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\", s.Syncz)\n202: \n203: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/registryz\", \"Debug support for registry\", s.registryz)\n204: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointz\", \"Obsolete, use endpointShardz\", s.endpointShardz)\n205: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/endpointShardz\", \"Info about the endpoint shards\", s.endpointShardz)\n206: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez\", \"Info about the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n207: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?sizes=true\", \"Info about the size of the internal XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n208: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/cachez?clear=true\", \"Clear the XDS caches\", s.cachez)\n209: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/configz\", \"Debug support for config\", s.configz)\n210: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/sidecarz\", \"Debug sidecar scope for a proxy\", s.sidecarz)\n211: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/resourcesz\", \"Debug support for watched resources\", s.resourcez)\n212: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/instancesz\", \"Debug support for service instances\", s.instancesz)\n213: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/ambientz\", \"Debug support for ambient\", s.ambientz)\n214: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/krtz\", \"Debug support for krt (internal state)\", s.krtz)\n215: \n216: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/authorizationz\", \"Internal authorization policies\", s.authorizationz)\n217: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/telemetryz\", \"Debug Telemetry configuration\", s.telemetryz)\n218: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/config_dump\", \"ConfigDump in the form of the Envoy admin config dump API for passed in proxyID\", s.ConfigDump)\n219: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/push_status\", \"Last PushContext Details\", s.pushStatusHandler)\n220: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/pushcontext\", \"Debug support for current push context\", s.pushContextHandler)\n221: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/connections\", \"Info about the connected XDS clients\", s.connectionsHandler)\n222: \n223: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/inject\", \"Active inject template\", s.injectTemplateHandler(webhook))\n224: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mesh\", \"Active mesh config\", s.meshHandler)\n225: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/clusterz\", \"List remote clusters where istiod reads endpoints\", s.clusterz)\n226: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/networkz\", \"List cross-network gateways\", s.networkz)\n227: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/mcsz\", \"List information about Kubernetes MCS services\", s.mcsz)\n228: \n229: \ts.addDebugHandler(mux, internalMux, \"/debug/list\", \"List all supported debug commands in json\", s.list)\n230: }\n231: \n232: func (s *DiscoveryServer) addDebugHandler(mux *http.ServeMux, internalMux *http.ServeMux,\n233: \tpath string, help string, handler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request),\n234: ) {\n235: \ts.debugHandlers[path] = help\n236: \t// Add handler without auth. This mux is never exposed on an HTTP server and only used internally\n237: \tif internalMux != nil {\n238: \t\tinternalMux.HandleFunc(path, handler)\n239: \t}\n240: \t// Add handler with auth; this is expose on an HTTP server\n241: \tmux.HandleFunc(path, s.allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(http.HandlerFunc(handler)))\n242: }\n243: \n244: func (s *DiscoveryServer) allowAuthenticatedOrLocalhost(next http.Handler) http.HandlerFunc {\n245: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n246: \t\t// Localhost gets unrestricted access (istiod talking to itself on 127.0.0.1:8080)\n247: \t\t// No namespace context set, so namespace checks are skipped\n248: \t\tif isRequestFromLocalhost(req) {\n249: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n250: \t\t\treturn\n251: \t\t}\n252: \t\t// Authenticate request with the same method as XDS\n253: \t\tauthFailMsgs := make([]string, 0)\n254: \t\tvar ids []string\n255: \t\tauthRequest := security.AuthContext{Request: req}\n256: \t\tfor _, authn := range s.Authenticators {\n257: \t\t\tu, err := authn.Authenticate(authRequest)\n258: \t\t\t// If one authenticator passes, return\n259: \t\t\tif u != nil && u.Identities != nil && err == nil {\n260: \t\t\t\tids = u.Identities\n261: \t\t\t\tbreak\n262: \t\t\t}\n263: \t\t\tauthFailMsgs = append(authFailMsgs, fmt.Sprintf(\"Authenticator %s: %v\", authn.AuthenticatorType(), err))\n264: \t\t}\n265: \t\tif ids == nil {\n266: \t\t\tistiolog.Errorf(\"Failed to authenticate %s %v\", req.URL, authFailMsgs)\n267: \t\t\t// Not including detailed info in the response, XDS doesn't either (returns a generic \"authentication failure).\n268: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusUnauthorized)\n269: \t\t\treturn\n270: \t\t}\n271: \t\t// Check namespace-based authorization for debug endpoints\n272: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n273: \t\t\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(ids)\n274: \t\t\tif !s.AuthorizeDebugRequest(ids, req) {\n275: \t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"Unauthorized debug request from %v to %s\", ids, req.URL.Path)\n276: \t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\n277: \t\t\t\treturn\n278: \t\t\t}\n279: \t\t\t// Store caller namespace in context for handlers to enforce proxy-level access\n280: \t\t\tctx := context.WithValue(req.Context(), CallerNamespaceKey{}, namespace)\n281: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req.WithContext(ctx))\n282: \t\t} else {\n283: \t\t\tnext.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n284: \t\t}\n285: \t}\n286: }\n287: \n288: func isRequestFromLocalhost(r *http.Request) bool {\n289: \tip, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(r.RemoteAddr)\n290: \tif err != nil {\n291: \t\treturn false\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tuserIP, _ := netip.ParseAddr(ip)\n295: \treturn userIP.IsLoopback()\n296: }\n297: \n298: // extractNamespace extracts namespace from authenticated identities\n299: func (s *DiscoveryServer) extractNamespace(identities []string) string {\n300: \tfor _, id := range identities {\n301: ",

  }

minimize noise in logs\n93: \twatchDebounceDelay = 100 * time.Millisecond\n94: )\n95: \n96: func init() {\n97: \t// Disable gRPC tracing. It has performance impacts (See https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/issues/695)\n98: \tgrpc.EnableTracing = false\n99: }\n100: \n101: // readinessProbe defines a function that will be used indicate whether a server is ready.\n102: type readinessProbe func() bool\n103: \n104: // Server contains the runtime configuration for the Pilot discovery service.\n105: type Server struct {\n106: \tXDSServer *xds.DiscoveryServer\n107: \n108: \tclusterID   cluster.ID\n109: \tenvironment *model.Environment\n110: \n111: \tkubeClient kubelib.Client\n112: \n113: \tmulticlusterController *multicluster.Controller\n114: \n115: \tconfigController       model.ConfigStoreController\n116: \tConfigStores           []model.ConfigStoreController\n117: \tserviceEntryController *serviceentry.Controller\n118: \n119: \thttpServer  *http.Server // debug, monitoring and readiness Server.\n120: \thttpAddr    string\n121: \thttpsServer *http.Server // webhooks HTTPS Server.\n122: \thttpsAddr   string\n123: \n124: \tgrpcServer        *grpc.Server\n125: \tgrpcAddress       string\n126: \tsecureGrpcServer  *grpc.Server\n127: \tsecureGrpcAddress string\n128: \n129: \t// monitoringMux listens on monitoringAddr(:15014).\n130: \t// Currently runs prometheus monitoring and debug (if enabled).\n131: \tmonitoringMux *http.ServeMux\n132: \t// internalDebugMux is a mux for *internal* calls to the debug interface. That is, authentication is disabled.\n133: \tinternalDebugMux *http.ServeMux\n134: \n135: \tmetricsExporter http.Handler\n136: \n137: \t// httpMux listens on the httpAddr (8080).\n138: \t// If a Gateway is used in front and https is off it is also multiplexing\n139: \t// the rest of the features if their port is empty.\n140: \t// Currently runs readiness and debug (if enabled)\n141: \thttpMux *http.ServeMux\n142: \n143: \t// httpsMux listens on the httpsAddr(15017), handling webhooks\n144: \t// If the address os empty, the webhooks will be set on the default httpPort.\n145: \thttpsMux *http.ServeMux // webhooks\n146: \n147: \t// fileWatcher used to watch mesh config, networks and certificates.\n148: \tfileWatcher filewatcher.FileWatcher\n149: \n150: \t// certWatcher watches the certificates for changes and triggers a notification to Istiod.\n151: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go%3FL1-150"
  }
}

Input
{
  "endLine": 600,

  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae",
