Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"300: \\tfor _, id := range identities {\\n301: \\t\\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\\n302: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n303: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n304: \\t\\t}\\n305: \\t\\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\\n306: \\t}\\n307: \\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\\n313: \\tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\\n314: \\n315: \\t// deny if no valid identity found\\n316: \\tif namespace == \\\"\\\" {\\n317: \\t\\treturn false\\n318: \\t}\\n319: \\n320: \\t// get system namespace (istio-system by default, or mesh root namespace)\\n321: \\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\\n322: \\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \\\"\\\" {\\n323: \\t\\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\\n324: \\t}\\n325: \\n326: \\t// allow all if identity is from system namespace\\n327: \\tif namespace == systemNamespace {\\n328: \\t\\treturn true\\n329: \\t}\\n330: \\n331: \\t// non-system namespace: only allow specific endpoints\\n332: \\tdebugPath := strings.TrimPrefix(req.URL.Path, \\\"/debug/\\\")\\n333: \\t_, allowed := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugPath]\\n334: \\treturn allowed\\n335: }\\n336: \\n337: // Syncz dumps the synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n339: \\tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"namespace\\\")\\n340: \\n341: \\tsyncz := make([]SyncStatus, 0)\\n342: \\tfor _, con := range s.SortedClients() {\\n343: \\t\\tnode := con.proxy\\n344: \\t\\tif node != nil && (namespace == \\\"\\\" || node.GetNamespace() == namespace) {\\n345: \\t\\t\\twrs := node.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\\n346: \\t\\t\\tres := make(map[string]ResourceStatus, len(wrs))\\n347: \\t\\t\\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\\n348: \\t\\t\\t\\tres[wr.TypeUrl] = ResourceStatus{\\n349: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSent:      wr.NonceSent,\\n350: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tAcked:     wr.NonceAcked,\\n351: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSentTime:  wr.LastSendTime,\\n352: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tLastError: wr.LastError,\\n353: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n354: \\t\\t\\t}\\n355: \\t\\t\\tsyncz = append(syncz, SyncStatus{\\n356: \\t\\t\\t\\tProxyID:              node.ID,\\n357: \\t\\t\\t\\tProxyType:            node.Type,\\n358: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterID:            node.GetClusterID().String(),\\n359: \\t\\t\\t\\tIstioVersion:         node.GetIstioVersion(),\\n360: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterSent:          node.NonceSent(v3.ClusterType),\\n361: \\t\\t\\t\\tClusterAcked:         node.NonceAcked(v3.ClusterType),\\n362: \\t\\t\\t\\tListenerSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.ListenerType),\\n363: \\t\\t\\t\\tListenerAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.ListenerType),\\n364: \\t\\t\\t\\tRouteSent:            node.NonceSent(v3.RouteType),\\n365: \\t\\t\\t\\tRouteAcked:           node.NonceAcked(v3.RouteType),\\n366: \\t\\t\\t\\tEndpointSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.EndpointType),\\n367: \\t\\t\\t\\tEndpointAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.EndpointType),\\n368: \\t\\t\\t\\tExtensionConfigSent:  node.NonceSent(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\\n369: \\t\\t\\t\\tExtensionConfigAcked: node.NonceAcked(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\tResources:            res,\\n371: \\t\\t\\t})\\n372: \\t\\t}\\n373: \\t}\\n374: \\twriteJSON(w, syncz, req)\\n375: }\\n376: \\n377: // registryz providees debug support for registry - adding and listing model items.\\n378: // Can be combined with the push debug interface to reproduce changes.\\n379: func (s *DiscoveryServer) registryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n380: \\tall := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Services()\\n381: \\twriteJSON(w, all, req)\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // Dumps info about the endpoint shards, tracked using the new direct interface.\\n385: // Legacy registry provides are synced to the new data structure as well, during\\n386: // the full push.\\n387: func (s *DiscoveryServer) endpointShardz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n388: \\twriteJSON(w, s.Env.EndpointIndex.Shardz(), req)\\n389: }\\n390: \\n391: func (s *DiscoveryServer) cachez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n392: \\tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\\n393: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n394: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Failed to parse request\\\\n\\\"))\\n395: \\t\\treturn\\n396: \\t}\\n397: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"clear\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n398: \\t\\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\\n399: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Cache cleared\\\\n\\\"))\\n400: \\t\\treturn\\n401: \\t}\\n402: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"sizes\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n403: \\t\\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n404: \\t\\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\\n405: \\t\\ttotalSize := 0\\n406: \\t\\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n407: \\t\\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n408: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n409: \\t\\t\\t}\\n410: \\t\\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n411: \\t\\t\\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\\n412: \\t\\t\\traw[resourceType] += sz\\n413: \\t\\t\\ttotalSize += sz\\n414: \\t\\t}\\n415: \\t\\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\\n416: \\t\\tfor k, v := range raw {\\n417: \\t\\t\\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\\n418: \\t\\t}\\n419: \\t\\tres[\\\"total\\\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\\n420: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\\n421: \\t\\treturn\\n422: \\t}\\n423: \\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\\n424: \\tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\\n425: \\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\\n426: \\t\\tif resource == nil {\\n427: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n428: \\t\\t}\\n429: \\t\\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\\n430: \\t\\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\\n431: \\t}\\n432: \\twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\\n433: }\\n434: \\n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\\n436: // object structure.\\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\\n438: \\tconfig.Config\\n439: }\\n440: \\n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\\n442: \\tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\\n443: \\tif err != nil {\\n444: \\t\\treturn nil, err\\n445: \\t}\\n446: \\treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\\n447: }\\n448: \\n449: // Config debugging.\\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n451: \\tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\\n452: \\tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\\n453: \\t\\treturn\\n454: \\t}\\n455: \\ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n456: \\t\\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \\\"\\\")\\n457: \\t\\tfor _, c := range cfg {\\n458: \\t\\t\\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\\n459: \\t\\t}\\n460: \\t\\treturn false\\n461: \\t})\\n462: \\twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\\n463: }\\n464: \\n465: // SidecarScope debugging\\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n467: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n468: \\tif con == nil {\\n469: \\t\\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\\n470: \\t\\treturn\\n471: \\t}\\n472: \\twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\\n473: }\\n474: \\n475: // Resource debugging.\\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n477: \\tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\\n478: \\n479: \\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\\n480: \\t\\ts.Env.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\\n481: \\t\\t\\tschemas = append(schemas, schema.GroupVersionKind())\\n482: \\t\\t\\treturn false\\n483: \\t\\t})\\n484: \\t}\\n485: \\n486: \\twriteJSON(w, schemas, req)\\n487: }\\n488: \\n489: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n490: type AuthorizationDebug struct {\\n491: \\tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies `json:\\\"authorization_policies\\\"`\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // authorizationz dumps the internal authorization policies.\\n495: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorizationz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n496: \\tinfo := AuthorizationDebug{\\n497: \\t\\tAuthorizationPolicies: s.globalPushContext().AuthzPolicies,\\n498: \\t}\\n499: \\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\\n503: type TelemetryDebug struct {\\n504: \\tTelemetries *model.Telemetries `json:\\\"telemetries\\\"`\\n505: }\\n506: \\n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n508: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n509: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n510: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n511: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n512: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n513: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n514: \\t\\treturn\\n515: \\t}\\n516: \\tif con == nil {\\n517: \\t\\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\\n518: \\t\\t\\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\\n519: \\t\\t}\\n520: \\t\\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\\n521: \\t\\treturn\\n522: \\t}\\n523: \\twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\\n524: }\\n525: \\n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n529: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n530: \\tconnections := s.SortedClients()\\n531: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n532: \\n533: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n534: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n535: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n536: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n537: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n538: \\t\\t}\\n539: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n540: \\t}\\n541: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n542: }\\n543: \\n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n547: \\tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\\n548: \\t\\treturn\\n549: \\t}\\n550: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n551: \\tif proxyID != \\\"\\\" && con == nil {\\n552: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n553: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n554: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n555: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\\\n\\\"))\\n556: \\t\\treturn\\n557: \\t}\\n558: \\tvar connections []*Connection\\n559: \\tif con != nil {\\n560: \\t\\tconnections = []*Connection{con}\\n561: \\t} else {\\n562: \\t\\tconnections = s.SortedClients()\\n563: \\t}\\n564: \\n565: \\tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\\n566: \\tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\\n567: \\tfor _, c := range connections {\\n568: \\t\\tadsClient := AdsClient{\\n569: \\t\\t\\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\\n570: \\t\\t\\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\\n571: \\t\\t\\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\\n572: \\t\\t\\tLabels:       c.proxy.Labels,\\n573: \\t\\t\\tMetadata:     c.proxy.Metadata,\\n574: \\t\\t\\tLocality:     c.proxy.Locality,\\n575: \\t\\t\\tWatches:      map[string][]string{},\\n576: \\t\\t}\\n577: \\t\\tc.proxy.RLock()\\n578: \\t\\tfor k, wr := range c.proxy.WatchedResources {\\n579: \\t\\t\\tadsClient.Watches[k] = wr.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()\\n580: \\t\\t}\\n581: \\t\\tc.proxy.RUnlock()\\n582: \\t\\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\\n583: \\t}\\n584: \\twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\\n585: }\\n586: \\n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\\n588: \\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n589: \\t\\tq := r.URL.Query()\\n590: \\t\\tq.Set(\\\"types\\\", typ)\\n591: \\t\\tr.URL.RawQuery = q.Encode()\\n592: \\t\\ts.ConfigDump(w, r)\\n593: \\t}\\n594: }\\n595: \\n596: // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n600: \\tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\\n601: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go%3FL300-600\"}",

    "content": "300: \tfor _, id := range identities {\n301: \t\tspiffeID, err := spiffe.ParseIdentity(id)\n302: \t\tif err != nil {\n303: \t\t\tcontinue\n304: \t\t}\n305: \t\treturn spiffeID.Namespace\n306: \t}\n307: \treturn \"\"\n308: }\n309: \n310: // AuthorizeDebugRequest checks if authenticated identities are authorized to access the requested debug endpoint.\n311: // Note: non-system namespace requests are further verified at connection time to ensure same-namespace proxy access only.\n312: func (s *DiscoveryServer) AuthorizeDebugRequest(identities []string, req *http.Request) bool {\n313: \tnamespace := s.extractNamespace(identities)\n314: \n315: \t// deny if no valid identity found\n316: \tif namespace == \"\" {\n317: \t\treturn false\n318: \t}\n319: \n320: \t// get system namespace (istio-system by default, or mesh root namespace)\n321: \tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\n322: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \"\" {\n323: \t\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\n324: \t}\n325: \n326: \t// allow all if identity is from system namespace\n327: \tif namespace == systemNamespace {\n328: \t\treturn true\n329: \t}\n330: \n331: \t// non-system namespace: only allow specific endpoints\n332: \tdebugPath := strings.TrimPrefix(req.URL.Path, \"/debug/\")\n333: \t_, allowed := activeNamespaceDebuggers[debugPath]\n334: \treturn allowed\n335: }\n336: \n337: // Syncz dumps the synchronization status of all Envoys connected to this Pilot instance\n338: func (s *DiscoveryServer) Syncz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n339: \tnamespace := req.URL.Query().Get(\"namespace\")\n340: \n341: \tsyncz := make([]SyncStatus, 0)\n342: \tfor _, con := range s.SortedClients() {\n343: \t\tnode := con.proxy\n344: \t\tif node != nil && (namespace == \"\" || node.GetNamespace() == namespace) {\n345: \t\t\twrs := node.DeepCloneWatchedResources()\n346: \t\t\tres := make(map[string]ResourceStatus, len(wrs))\n347: \t\t\tfor _, wr := range wrs {\n348: \t\t\t\tres[wr.TypeUrl] = ResourceStatus{\n349: \t\t\t\t\tSent:      wr.NonceSent,\n350: \t\t\t\t\tAcked:     wr.NonceAcked,\n351: \t\t\t\t\tSentTime:  wr.LastSendTime,\n352: \t\t\t\t\tLastError: wr.LastError,\n353: \t\t\t\t}\n354: \t\t\t}\n355: \t\t\tsyncz = append(syncz, SyncStatus{\n356: \t\t\t\tProxyID:              node.ID,\n357: \t\t\t\tProxyType:            node.Type,\n358: \t\t\t\tClusterID:            node.GetClusterID().String(),\n359: \t\t\t\tIstioVersion:         node.GetIstioVersion(),\n360: \t\t\t\tClusterSent:          node.NonceSent(v3.ClusterType),\n361: \t\t\t\tClusterAcked:         node.NonceAcked(v3.ClusterType),\n362: \t\t\t\tListenerSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.ListenerType),\n363: \t\t\t\tListenerAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.ListenerType),\n364: \t\t\t\tRouteSent:            node.NonceSent(v3.RouteType),\n365: \t\t\t\tRouteAcked:           node.NonceAcked(v3.RouteType),\n366: \t\t\t\tEndpointSent:         node.NonceSent(v3.EndpointType),\n367: \t\t\t\tEndpointAcked:        node.NonceAcked(v3.EndpointType),\n368: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigSent:  node.NonceSent(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n369: \t\t\t\tExtensionConfigAcked: node.NonceAcked(v3.ExtensionConfigurationType),\n370: \t\t\t\tResources:            res,\n371: \t\t\t})\n372: \t\t}\n373: \t}\n374: \twriteJSON(w, syncz, req)\n375: }\n376: \n377: // registryz providees debug support for registry - adding and listing model items.\n378: // Can be combined with the push debug interface to reproduce changes.\n379: func (s *DiscoveryServer) registryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n380: \tall := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Services()\n381: \twriteJSON(w, all, req)\n382: }\n383: \n384: // Dumps info about the endpoint shards, tracked using the new direct interface.\n385: // Legacy registry provides are synced to the new data structure as well, during\n386: // the full push.\n387: func (s *DiscoveryServer) endpointShardz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n388: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.EndpointIndex.Shardz(), req)\n389: }\n390: \n391: func (s *DiscoveryServer) cachez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n392: \tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n393: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n394: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Failed to parse request\\n\"))\n395: \t\treturn\n396: \t}\n397: \tif req.Form.Get(\"clear\") != \"\" {\n398: \t\ts.Cache.ClearAll()\n399: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Cache cleared\\n\"))\n400: \t\treturn\n401: \t}\n402: \tif req.Form.Get(\"sizes\") != \"\" {\n403: \t\tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n404: \t\traw := make(map[string]int, len(snapshot))\n405: \t\ttotalSize := 0\n406: \t\tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n407: \t\t\tif resource == nil {\n408: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n409: \t\t\t}\n410: \t\t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n411: \t\t\tsz := len(resource.Resource.GetValue())\n412: \t\t\traw[resourceType] += sz\n413: \t\t\ttotalSize += sz\n414: \t\t}\n415: \t\tres := make(map[string]string, len(raw))\n416: \t\tfor k, v := range raw {\n417: \t\t\tres[k] = util.ByteCount(v)\n418: \t\t}\n419: \t\tres[\"total\"] = util.ByteCount(totalSize)\n420: \t\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\n421: \t\treturn\n422: \t}\n423: \tsnapshot := s.Cache.Snapshot()\n424: \tresources := make(map[string][]string, len(snapshot)) // Key is typeUrl and value is resource names.\n425: \tfor _, resource := range snapshot {\n426: \t\tif resource == nil {\n427: \t\t\tcontinue\n428: \t\t}\n429: \t\tresourceType := resource.Resource.TypeUrl\n430: \t\tresources[resourceType] = append(resources[resourceType], resource.Name)\n431: \t}\n432: \twriteJSON(w, resources, req)\n433: }\n434: \n435: // kubernetesConfig wraps a config.Config with a custom marshaling method that matches a Kubernetes\n436: // object structure.\n437: type kubernetesConfig struct {\n438: \tconfig.Config\n439: }\n440: \n441: func (k kubernetesConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n442: \tcfg, err := crd.ConvertConfig(k.Config)\n443: \tif err != nil {\n444: \t\treturn nil, err\n445: \t}\n446: \treturn json.Marshal(cfg)\n447: }\n448: \n449: // Config debugging.\n450: func (s *DiscoveryServer) configz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n451: \tconfigs := make([]kubernetesConfig, 0)\n452: \tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.ConfigStore == nil {\n453: \t\treturn\n454: \t}\n455: \ts.Env.ConfigStore.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\n456: \t\tcfg := s.Env.ConfigStore.List(schema.GroupVersionKind(), \"\")\n457: \t\tfor _, c := range cfg {\n458: \t\t\tconfigs = append(configs, kubernetesConfig{c})\n459: \t\t}\n460: \t\treturn false\n461: \t})\n462: \twriteJSON(w, configs, req)\n463: }\n464: \n465: // SidecarScope debugging\n466: func (s *DiscoveryServer) sidecarz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n467: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n468: \tif con == nil {\n469: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n470: \t\treturn\n471: \t}\n472: \twriteJSON(w, con.proxy.SidecarScope, req)\n473: }\n474: \n475: // Resource debugging.\n476: func (s *DiscoveryServer) resourcez(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n477: \tschemas := make([]config.GroupVersionKind, 0)\n478: \n479: \tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.ConfigStore != nil {\n480: \t\ts.Env.Schemas().ForEach(func(schema resource.Schema) bool {\n481: \t\t\tschemas = append(schemas, schema.GroupVersionKind())\n482: \t\t\treturn false\n483: \t\t})\n484: \t}\n485: \n486: \twriteJSON(w, schemas, req)\n487: }\n488: \n489: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\n490: type AuthorizationDebug struct {\n491: \tAuthorizationPolicies *model.AuthorizationPolicies `json:\"authorization_policies\"`\n492: }\n493: \n494: // authorizationz dumps the internal authorization policies.\n495: func (s *DiscoveryServer) authorizationz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n496: \tinfo := AuthorizationDebug{\n497: \t\tAuthorizationPolicies: s.globalPushContext().AuthzPolicies,\n498: \t}\n499: \twriteJSON(w, info, req)\n500: }\n501: \n502: // AuthorizationDebug holds debug information for authorization policy.\n503: type TelemetryDebug struct {\n504: \tTelemetries *model.Telemetries `json:\"telemetries\"`\n505: }\n506: \n507: func (s *DiscoveryServer) telemetryz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n508: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n509: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n510: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n511: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n512: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n513: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n514: \t\treturn\n515: \t}\n516: \tif con == nil {\n517: \t\tinfo := TelemetryDebug{\n518: \t\t\tTelemetries: s.globalPushContext().Telemetry,\n519: \t\t}\n520: \t\twriteJSON(w, info, req)\n521: \t\treturn\n522: \t}\n523: \twriteJSON(w, s.globalPushContext().Telemetry.Debug(con.proxy), req)\n524: }\n525: \n526: // connectionsHandler implements interface for displaying current connections.\n527: // It is mapped to /debug/connections.\n528: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionsHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n529: \tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\n530: \tconnections := s.SortedClients()\n531: \tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\n532: \n533: \tfor _, c := range connections {\n534: \t\tadsClient := AdsClient{\n535: \t\t\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\n536: \t\t\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\n537: \t\t\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\n538: \t\t}\n539: \t\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\n540: \t}\n541: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n542: }\n543: \n544: // adsz implements a status and debug interface for ADS.\n545: // It is mapped to /debug/adsz\n546: func (s *DiscoveryServer) adsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n547: \tif s.handlePushRequest(w, req) {\n548: \t\treturn\n549: \t}\n550: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n551: \tif proxyID != \"\" && con == nil {\n552: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n553: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n554: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n555: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n556: \t\treturn\n557: \t}\n558: \tvar connections []*Connection\n559: \tif con != nil {\n560: \t\tconnections = []*Connection{con}\n561: \t} else {\n562: \t\tconnections = s.SortedClients()\n563: \t}\n564: \n565: \tadsClients := &AdsClients{}\n566: \tadsClients.Total = len(connections)\n567: \tfor _, c := range connections {\n568: \t\tadsClient := AdsClient{\n569: \t\t\tConnectionID: c.ID(),\n570: \t\t\tConnectedAt:  c.ConnectedAt(),\n571: \t\t\tPeerAddress:  c.Peer(),\n572: \t\t\tLabels:       c.proxy.Labels,\n573: \t\t\tMetadata:     c.proxy.Metadata,\n574: \t\t\tLocality:     c.proxy.Locality,\n575: \t\t\tWatches:      map[string][]string{},\n576: \t\t}\n577: \t\tc.proxy.RLock()\n578: \t\tfor k, wr := range c.proxy.WatchedResources {\n579: \t\t\tadsClient.Watches[k] = wr.ResourceNames.UnsortedList()\n580: \t\t}\n581: \t\tc.proxy.RUnlock()\n582: \t\tadsClients.Connected = append(adsClients.Connected, adsClient)\n583: \t}\n584: \twriteJSON(w, adsClients, req)\n585: }\n586: \n587: func (s *DiscoveryServer) typedConfigDumpHandler(typ string) http.HandlerFunc {\n588: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n589: \t\tq := r.URL.Query()\n590: \t\tq.Set(\"types\", typ)\n591: \t\tr.URL.RawQuery = q.Encode()\n592: \t\ts.ConfigDump(w, r)\n593: \t}\n594: }\n595: \n596: // ConfigDump returns information in the form of the Envoy admin API config dump for the specified proxy\n597: // The dump will only contain dynamic listeners/clusters/routes and can be used to compare what an Envoy instance\n598: // should look like according to Pilot vs what it currently does look like.\n599: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ConfigDump(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n600: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n601: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/debug.go%3FL300-600"
  }
}

modify the cached item\n687: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Make a clone\n688: \t\t\t\t\t\trr = protomarshal.Clone(rr)\n689: \t\t\t\t\t\tsecret.GetTlsCertificate().PrivateKey = &core.DataSource{\n690: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tSpecifier: &core.DataSource_InlineBytes{\n691: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tInlineBytes: []byte(\"[redacted]\"),\n692: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n693: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n694: \t\t\t\t\t}\n695: \t\t\t\t\trr.Resource = protoconv.MessageToAny(secret)\n696: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n697: \t\t\t\tcase v3.ExtensionConfigurationType:\n698: \t\t\t\t\ttce := &core.TypedExtensionConfig{}\n699: \t\t\t\t\tif err := rr.GetResource().UnmarshalTo(tce); err != nil {\n700: \t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal extension: %v\", err)\n701: \t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n702: \t\t\t\t\t}\n703: \n704: \t\t\t\t\tswitch tce.TypedConfig.TypeUrl {\n705: \t\t\t\t\tcase xds.WasmHTTPFilterType:\n706: \t\t\t\t\t\tw := &wasm.Wasm{}\n707: \t\t\t\t\t\tif err := tce.TypedConfig.UnmarshalTo(w); err != nil {\n708: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"failed to unmarshal wasm filter: %v\", err)\n709: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n710: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n711: \t\t\t\t\t\t// Redact Wasm secret env variable.\n712: \t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs := w.GetConfig().GetVmConfig().EnvironmentVariables\n713: \t\t\t\t\t\tif vmenvs != nil {\n714: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tif _, found := vmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv]; found {\n715: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tvmenvs.KeyValues[model.WasmSecretEnv] = \"<Redacted>\"\n716: \t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n717: \t\t\t\t\t\t}\n718: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], &discoveryv3.Resource{\n719: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tName:     w.Config.Name,\n720: \t\t\t\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(w),\n721: \t\t\t\t\t\t})\n722: \t\t\t\t\tdefault:\n723: \t\t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n724: \t\t\t\t\t}\n725: \t\t\t\tdefault:\n726: \t\t\t\t\tdumps[resourceType] = append(dumps[resourceType], rr)\n727: \t\t\t\t}\n728: \t\t\t}\n729: \t\t} else {\n730: \t\t\tistiolog.Warnf(\"generate failed for request resource type (%v): %v\", resourceType, err)\n731: \t\t\tcontinue\n732: \t\t}\n733: \t}\n734: \n735: \treturn dumps\n736: }\n737: \n738: // connectionConfigDump converts the connection internal state into an Envoy Admin API config dump proto\n739: // It is used in debugging to create a consistent object for comparison between Envoy and Pilot outputs\n740: func (s *DiscoveryServer) connectionConfigDump(conn *Connection, includeEds bool) (*admin.ConfigDump, error) {\n741: \treq := &model.PushRequest{Push: conn.proxy.LastPushContext, Start: time.Now(), Full: true, Forced: true}\n742: \tversion := req.Push.PushVersion\n743: \n744: \tdump := s.getConfigDumpByResourceType(conn, req, []string{\n745: \t\tv3.ClusterType,\n746: \t\tv3.ListenerType,\n747: \t\tv3.RouteType,\n748: \t\tv3.SecretType,\n749: \t\tv3.EndpointType,\n750: \t\tv3.ExtensionConfigurationType,\n751: \t})\n752: \n753: \tdynamicActiveClusters := make([]*admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster, 0)\n754: \tfor _, cluster := range dump[v3.ClusterType] {\n755: \t\tdynamicActiveClusters = append(dynamicActiveClusters, &admin.ClustersConfigDump_DynamicCluster{\n756: \t\t\tCluster: cluster.Resource,\n757: \t\t})\n758: \t}\n759: \tclustersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ClustersConfigDump{\n760: \t\tVersionInfo:           version,\n761: \t\tDynamicActiveClusters: dynamicActiveClusters,\n762: \t})\n763: \tif err != nil {\n764: \t\treturn nil, err\n765: \t}\n766: \n767: \tdynamicActiveListeners := make([]*admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener, 0)\n768: \tfor _, listener := range dump[v3.ListenerType] {\n769: \t\tdynamicActiveListeners = append(dynamicActiveListeners, &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListener{\n770: \t\t\tName: listener.Name,\n771: \t\t\tActiveState: &admin.ListenersConfigDump_DynamicListenerState{\n772: \t\t\t\tListener:    listener.Resource,\n773: \t\t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n774: \t\t\t},\n775: \t\t})\n776: \t}\n777: \tlistenersAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.ListenersConfigDump{\n778: \t\tVersionInfo:      version,\n779: \t\tDynamicListeners: dynamicActiveListeners,\n780: \t})\n781: \tif err != nil {\n782: \t\treturn nil, err\n783: \t}\n784: \n785: \tdynamicRouteConfig := make([]*admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig, 0)\n786: \tfor _, route := range dump[v3.RouteType] {\n787: \t\tdynamicRouteConfig = append(dynamicRouteConfig, &admin.RoutesConfigDump_DynamicRouteConfig{\n788: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n789: \t\t\tRouteConfig: route.Resource,\n790: \t\t})\n791: \t}\n792: \troutesAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.RoutesConfigDump{\n793: \t\tDynamicRouteConfigs: dynamicRouteConfig,\n794: \t})\n795: \tif err != nil {\n796: \t\treturn nil, err\n797: \t}\n798: \n799: \tdynamicSecretsConfig := make([]*admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret, 0)\n800: \tfor _, secret := range dump[v3.SecretType] {\n801: \t\tdynamicSecretsConfig = append(dynamicSecretsConfig, &admin.SecretsConfigDump_DynamicSecret{\n802: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n803: \t\t\tSecret:      secret.Resource,\n804: \t\t})\n805: \t}\n806: \tsecretsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.SecretsConfigDump{\n807: \t\tDynamicActiveSecrets: dynamicSecretsConfig,\n808: \t})\n809: \tif err != nil {\n810: \t\treturn nil, err\n811: \t}\n812: \n813: \textensionsConfig := make([]*admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig, 0)\n814: \tfor _, ext := range dump[v3.ExtensionConfigurationType] {\n815: \t\textensionsConfig = append(extensionsConfig, &admin.EcdsConfigDump_EcdsFilterConfig{\n816: \t\t\tVersionInfo: version,\n817: \t\t\tEcdsFilter:  ext.Resource,\n818: \t\t})\n819: \t}\n820: \textensionsAny, err := protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EcdsConfigDump{\n821: \t\tEcdsFilters: extensionsConfig,\n822: \t})\n823: \tif err != nil {\n824: \t\treturn nil, err\n825: \t}\n826: \n827: \tvar endpointsAny *anypb.Any\n828: \t// EDS is disabled by default for compatibility with Envoy config_dump interface\n829: \tif includeEds {\n830: \t\tendpointConfig := make([]*admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig, 0)\n831: \t\tfor _, endpoint := range dump[v3.EndpointType] {\n832: \t\t\tendpointConfig = append(endpointConfig, &admin.EndpointsConfigDump_DynamicEndpointConfig{\n833: \t\t\t\tVersionInfo:    version,\n834: \t\t\t\tEndpointConfig: endpoint.Resource,\n835: \t\t\t})\n836: \t\t}\n837: \t\tendpointsAny, err = protoconv.MessageToAnyWithError(&admin.EndpointsConfigDump{\n838: \t\t\tDynamicEndpointConfigs: endpointConfig,\n839: \t\t})\n840: \t\tif err != nil {\n841: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n842: \t\t}\n843: \t}\n844: \n845: \tbootstrapAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.BootstrapConfigDump{})\n846: \tscopedRoutesAny := protoconv.MessageToAny(&admin.ScopedRoutesConfigDump{})\n847: \t// The config dump must have all configs with connections specified in\n848: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-v2/admin/v2alpha/config_dump.proto\n849: \tconfigs := []*anypb.Any{\n850: \t\tbootstrapAny,\n851: \t\tclustersAny,\n852: \t}\n853: \tif includeEds {\n854: \t\tconfigs = append(configs, endpointsAny)\n855: \t}\n856: \tconfigs = append(configs,\n857: \t\tlistenersAny,\n858: \t\tscopedRoutesAny,\n859: \t\troutesAny,\n860: \t\tsecretsAny,\n861: \t\textensionsAny,\n862: \t)\n863: \tconfigDump := &admin.ConfigDump{\n864: \t\tConfigs: configs,\n865: \t}\n866: \treturn configDump, nil\n867: }\n868: \n869: // injectTemplateHandler dumps the injection template\n870: // Replaces dumping the template at startup.\n871: func (s *DiscoveryServer) injectTemplateHandler(webhook func() map[string]string) func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request) {\n872: \treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n873: \t\t// TODO: we should split the inject template into smaller modules (separate one for dump core, etc),\n874: \t\t// and allow pods to select which patches will be selected. When this happen, this should return\n875: \t\t// all inject templates or take a param to select one.\n876: \t\tif webhook == nil {\n877: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n878: \t\t\treturn\n879: \t\t}\n880: \n881: \t\twriteJSON(w, webhook(), req)\n882: \t}\n883: }\n884: \n885: // meshHandler dumps the mesh config\n886: func (s *DiscoveryServer) meshHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n887: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.Mesh(), req)\n888: }\n889: \n890: // pushStatusHandler dumps the last PushContext\n891: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushStatusHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n892: \tmodel.LastPushMutex.Lock()\n893: \tdefer model.LastPushMutex.Unlock()\n894: \tif model.LastPushStatus == nil {\n895: \t\treturn\n896: \t}\n897: \tout, err := model.LastPushStatus.StatusJSON()\n898: \tif err != nil {\n899: \t\thandleHTTPError(w, err)\n900: \t\treturn\n901: ",

\\tfor k := range s.debugHandlers {\\n965: \\t\\tkey := strings.Replace(k, \\\"/debug/\\\", \\\"\\\", -1)\\n966: \\t\\t// exclude current list command\\n967: \\t\\tif key == \\\"list\\\" {\\n968: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n969: \\t\\t}\\n970: \\t\\t// can not support pprof commands\\n971: \\t\\tif strings.Contains(key, \\\"pprof\\\") {\\n972: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n973: \\t\\t}\\n974: \\t\\tcmdNames = \\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"OK\\\"))\\n988: }\\n989: \\n990: func cloneProxy(proxy *model.Proxy) *model.Proxy {\\n991: \\tif proxy == nil {\\n992: \\t\\treturn nil\\n993: \\t}\\n994: \\n995: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n996: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n997: \\t// nolint: govet\\n998: \\tcopied := *proxy\\n999: \\tout := &copied\\n1000: \\tout.RWMutex = sync.RWMutex{}\\n1001: \\t// clone WatchedResources which can be mutated when processing request\\n1002: \\tout.WatchedResources = make(map[string]*model.WatchedResource, len(proxy.WatchedResources))\\n1003: \\tfor k, v := range proxy.WatchedResources {\\n1004: \\t\\t// nolint: govet\\n1005: \\t\\tv := *v\\n1006: \\t\\tv.ResourceNames = v.ResourceNames.Copy()\\n1007: \\t\\tout.WatchedResources[k] = &v\\n1008: \\t}\\n1009: \\treturn out\\n1010: }\\n1011: \\n1012: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getProxyConnection(proxyID string) *Connection {\\n1013: \\tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\\n1014: \\t\\tif strings.Contains(con.ID(), proxyID) {\\n1015: \\t\\t\\tout := *con\\n1016: \\t\\t\\tout.proxy = cloneProxy(con.proxy)\\n1017: \\t\\t\\treturn &out\\n1018: \\t\\t}\\n1019: \\t}\\n1020: \\n1021: \\treturn nil\\n1022: }\\n1023: \\n1024: func (s *DiscoveryServer) instancesz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1025: \\tinstances := map[string][]model.ServiceTarget{}\\n1026: \\tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\\n1027: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RLock()\\n1028: \\t\\tif con.proxy != nil {\\n1029: \\t\\t\\tinstances[con.proxy.ID] = con.proxy.ServiceTargets\\n1030: \\t\\t}\\n1031: \\t\\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\\n1032: \\t}\\n1033: \\twriteJSON(w, instances, req)\\n1034: }\\n1035: \\n1036: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ambientz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1037: \\taddresses, _ := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(nil)\\n1038: \\tres := struct {\\n1039: \\t\\tWorkloads []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"workloads\\\"`\\n1040: \\t\\tServices  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"services\\\"`\\n1041: \\t\\tPolicies  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\\\"policies\\\"`\\n1042: \\t}{}\\n1043: \\t// WDS stores IPs as raw byte form. We want to view them as strings, so convert.\\n1044: \\t// This doesn't quite work ideally, since json marshal will write as base64, but its better than nothing\\n1045: \\trewriteAddress := func(b []byte) []byte {\\n1046: \\t\\tip, ok := netip.AddrFromSlice(b)\\n1047: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n1048: \\t\\t\\treturn b\\n1049: \\t\\t}\\n1050: \\t\\treturn []byte(ip.String())\\n1051: \\t}\\n1052: \\trewriteNetworkAddress := func(b *workloadapi.NetworkAddress) *workloadapi.NetworkAddress {\\n1053: \\t\\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\\n1054: \\t\\tnb.Address = rewriteAddress(nb.Address)\\n1055: \\t\\treturn nb\\n1056: \\t}\\n1057: \\trewriteGatewayAddress := func(b *workloadapi.GatewayAddress) *workloadapi.GatewayAddress {\\n1058: \\t\\tif b == nil {\\n1059: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n1060: \\t\\t}\\n1061: \\t\\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\\n1062: \\t\\tswitch t := nb.Destination.(type) {\\n1063: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.GatewayAddress_Address:\\n1064: \\t\\t\\tt.Address = rewriteNetworkAddress(t.Address)\\n1065: \\t\\t}\\n1066: \\t\\treturn nb\\n1067: \\t}\\n1068: \\tfor _, original := range addresses {\\n1069: \\t\\taddr := protomarshal.Clone(original.Address)\\n1070: \\t\\tswitch addr := addr.Type.(type) {\\n1071: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Workload:\\n1072: \\t\\t\\tw := addr.Workload\\n1073: \\t\\t\\tw.Addresses = slices.Map(w.Addresses, rewriteAddress)\\n1074: \\t\\t\\tw.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.Waypoint)\\n1075: \\t\\t\\tw.NetworkGateway = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.NetworkGateway)\\n1076: \\t\\t\\tres.Workloads = append(res.Workloads, jsonMarshalProto{w})\\n1077: \\t\\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Service:\\n1078: \\t\\t\\ts := addr.Service\\n1079: \\t\\t\\ts.Addresses = slices.Map(s.Addresses, rewriteNetworkAddress)\\n1080: \\t\\t\\ts.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(s.Waypoint)\\n1081: \\t\\t\\tres.Services = append(res.Services, jsonMarshalProto{s})\\n1082: \\t\\t}\\n1083: \\t}\\n1084: \\tfor _, policy := range s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(nil) {\\n1085: \\t\\tres.Policies = append(res.Policies, jsonMarshalProto{policy.Authorization})\\n1086: \\t}\\n1087: \\twriteJSON(w, res, req)\\n1088: }\\n1089: \\n1090: func (s *DiscoveryServer) krtz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1091: \\twriteJSON(w, s.krtDebugger, req)\\n1092: }\\n1093: \\n1094: func (s *DiscoveryServer) networkz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n1095: \\tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.NetworkManager == nil {\\n1096: \\t\\treturn\\n1097: \\t}\\n1098: \\twriteJSON(w, s.Env.NetworkManager.AllGateways(), req)\\n1099: }\\n1100: \\n1101: *http.Request) {\n963: \tvar cmdNames []string\n964: \tfor k := range s.debugHandlers {\n965: \t\tkey := strings.Replace(k, \"/debug/\", \"\", -1)\n966: \t\t// exclude current list command\n967: \t\tif key == \"list\" {\n968: \t\t\tcontinue\n969: \t\t}\n970: \t\t// can not support pprof commands\n971: \t\tif strings.Contains(key, \"pprof\") {\n972: \t\t\tcontinue\n973: \t\t}\n974: \t\tcmdNames = append(cmdNames, key)\n975: \t}\n976: \tsort.Strings(cmdNames)\n977: \twriteJSON(w, cmdNames, req)\n978: }\n979: \n980: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceDisconnect(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n981: \tproxyID, con := s.getDebugConnection(req)\n982: \tif con == nil {\n983: \t\ts.errorHandler(w, proxyID, con)\n984: \t\treturn\n985: \t}\n986: \tcon.Stop()\n987: \t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n988: }\n989: \n990: func cloneProxy(proxy *model.Proxy) *model.Proxy {\n991: \tif proxy == nil {\n992: \t\treturn nil\n993: \t}\n994: \n995: \tproxy.Lock()\n996: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n997: \t// nolint: govet\n998: \tcopied := *proxy\n999: \tout := &copied\n1000: \tout.RWMutex = sync.RWMutex{}\n1001: \t// clone WatchedResources which can be mutated when processing request\n1002: \tout.WatchedResources = make(map[string]*model.WatchedResource, len(proxy.WatchedResources))\n1003: \tfor k, v := range proxy.WatchedResources {\n1004: \t\t// nolint: govet\n1005: \t\tv := *v\n1006: \t\tv.ResourceNames = v.ResourceNames.Copy()\n1007: \t\tout.WatchedResources[k] = &v\n1008: \t}\n1009: \treturn out\n1010: }\n1011: \n1012: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getProxyConnection(proxyID string) *Connection {\n1013: \tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\n1014: \t\tif strings.Contains(con.ID(), proxyID) {\n1015: \t\t\tout := *con\n1016: \t\t\tout.proxy = cloneProxy(con.proxy)\n1017: \t\t\treturn &out\n1018: \t\t}\n1019: \t}\n1020: \n1021: \treturn nil\n1022: }\n1023: \n1024: func (s *DiscoveryServer) instancesz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1025: \tinstances := map[string][]model.ServiceTarget{}\n1026: \tfor _, con := range s.Clients() {\n1027: \t\tcon.proxy.RLock()\n1028: \t\tif con.proxy != nil {\n1029: \t\t\tinstances[con.proxy.ID] = con.proxy.ServiceTargets\n1030: \t\t}\n1031: \t\tcon.proxy.RUnlock()\n1032: \t}\n1033: \twriteJSON(w, instances, req)\n1034: }\n1035: \n1036: func (s *DiscoveryServer) ambientz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1037: \taddresses, _ := s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(nil)\n1038: \tres := struct {\n1039: \t\tWorkloads []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"workloads\"`\n1040: \t\tServices  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"services\"`\n1041: \t\tPolicies  []jsonMarshalProto `json:\"policies\"`\n1042: \t}{}\n1043: \t// WDS stores IPs as raw byte form. We want to view them as strings, so convert.\n1044: \t// This doesn't quite work ideally, since json marshal will write as base64, but its better than nothing\n1045: \trewriteAddress := func(b []byte) []byte {\n1046: \t\tip, ok := netip.AddrFromSlice(b)\n1047: \t\tif !ok {\n1048: \t\t\treturn b\n1049: \t\t}\n1050: \t\treturn []byte(ip.String())\n1051: \t}\n1052: \trewriteNetworkAddress := func(b *workloadapi.NetworkAddress) *workloadapi.NetworkAddress {\n1053: \t\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\n1054: \t\tnb.Address = rewriteAddress(nb.Address)\n1055: \t\treturn nb\n1056: \t}\n1057: \trewriteGatewayAddress := func(b *workloadapi.GatewayAddress) *workloadapi.GatewayAddress {\n1058: \t\tif b == nil {\n1059: \t\t\treturn nil\n1060: \t\t}\n1061: \t\tnb := protomarshal.Clone(b)\n1062: \t\tswitch t := nb.Destination.(type) {\n1063: \t\tcase *workloadapi.GatewayAddress_Address:\n1064: \t\t\tt.Address = rewriteNetworkAddress(t.Address)\n1065: \t\t}\n1066: \t\treturn nb\n1067: \t}\n1068: \tfor _, original := range addresses {\n1069: \t\taddr := protomarshal.Clone(original.Address)\n1070: \t\tswitch addr := addr.Type.(type) {\n1071: \t\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Workload:\n1072: \t\t\tw := addr.Workload\n1073: \t\t\tw.Addresses = slices.Map(w.Addresses, rewriteAddress)\n1074: \t\t\tw.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.Waypoint)\n1075: \t\t\tw.NetworkGateway = rewriteGatewayAddress(w.NetworkGateway)\n1076: \t\t\tres.Workloads = append(res.Workloads, jsonMarshalProto{w})\n1077: \t\tcase *workloadapi.Address_Service:\n1078: \t\t\ts := addr.Service\n1079: \t\t\ts.Addresses = slices.Map(s.Addresses, rewriteNetworkAddress)\n1080: \t\t\ts.Waypoint = rewriteGatewayAddress(s.Waypoint)\n1081: \t\t\tres.Services = append(res.Services, jsonMarshalProto{s})\n1082: \t\t}\n1083: \t}\n1084: \tfor _, policy := range s.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(nil) {\n1085: \t\tres.Policies = append(res.Policies, jsonMarshalProto{policy.Authorization})\n1086: \t}\n1087: \twriteJSON(w, res, req)\n1088: }\n1089: \n1090: func (s *DiscoveryServer) krtz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1091: \twriteJSON(w, s.krtDebugger, req)\n1092: }\n1093: \n1094: func (s *DiscoveryServer) networkz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1095: \tif s.Env == nil || s.Env.NetworkManager == nil {\n1096: \t\treturn\n1097: \t}\n1098: \twriteJSON(w, s.Env.NetworkManager.AllGateways(), req)\n1099: }\n1100: \n1101: ",

  }

err != nil {\\n1131: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n1132: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Failed to parse request\\\\n\\\"))\\n1133: \\t\\treturn true\\n1134: \\t}\\n1135: \\tif req.Form.Get(\\\"push\\\") != \\\"\\\" {\\n1136: \\t\\tAdsPushAll(s)\\n1137: \\t\\t_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, \\\"Pushed to %d servers\\\\n\\\", s.adsClientCount())\\n1138: \\t\\treturn true\\n1139: \\t}\\n1140: \\treturn false\\n1141: }\\n1142: \\n1143: // getDebugConnection fetches the Connection requested by proxyID\\n1144: // For non-system namespaces, restricts access to proxies in the caller's namespace only\\n1145: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getDebugConnection(req *http.Request) (string, *Connection) {\\n1146: \\tif proxyID := req.URL.Query().Get(\\\"proxyID\\\"); proxyID != \\\"\\\" {\\n1147: \\t\\tcon := s.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\\n1148: \\t\\t// Verify namespace if caller is not from system namespace (when auth is enabled)\\n1149: \\t\\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\\n1150: \\t\\t\\tcallerNamespace, _ := req.Context().Value(CallerNamespaceKey{}).(string)\\n1151: \\t\\t\\tif con != nil && callerNamespace != \\\"\\\" {\\n1152: \\t\\t\\t\\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\\n1153: \\t\\t\\t\\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \\\"\\\" {\\n1154: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\\n1155: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1156: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Non-system namespaces can only access proxies in their own namespace\\n1157: \\t\\t\\t\\tif callerNamespace != systemNamespace && con.proxy.ConfigNamespace != callerNamespace {\\n1158: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn proxyID, nil // Return nil connection to deny access\\n1159: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n1160: \\t\\t\\t}\\n1161: \\t\\t}\\n1162: \\t\\treturn proxyID, con\\n1163: \\t}\\n1164: \\treturn \\\"\\\", nil\\n1165: }\\n1166: \\n1167: func (s *DiscoveryServer) errorHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, proxyID string, con *Connection) {\\n1168: \\tif proxyID == \\\"\\\" {\\n1169: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\\n1170: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"You must provide a proxyID in the query string\\\\n\\\"))\\n1171: \\t\\treturn\\n1172: \\t}\\n1173: \\tif con == nil {\\n1174: \\t\\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\\n1175: \\t\\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\\n1176: \\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\\n1177: \\t\\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\\\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance.   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1100: \n1101: func (s *DiscoveryServer) mcsz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1102: \tsvcs := sortMCSServices(s.Env.MCSServices())\n1103: \twriteJSON(w, svcs, req)\n1104: }\n1105: \n1106: func sortMCSServices(svcs []model.MCSServiceInfo) []model.MCSServiceInfo {\n1107: \tsort.Slice(svcs, func(i, j int) bool {\n1108: \t\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Cluster.String(), svcs[j].Cluster.String()) < 0 {\n1109: \t\t\treturn true\n1110: \t\t}\n1111: \t\tif strings.Compare(svcs[i].Namespace, svcs[j].Namespace) < 0 {\n1112: \t\t\treturn true\n1113: \t\t}\n1114: \t\treturn strings.Compare(svcs[i].Name, svcs[j].Name) < 0\n1115: \t})\n1116: \treturn svcs\n1117: }\n1118: \n1119: func (s *DiscoveryServer) clusterz(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n1120: \tif s.ListRemoteClusters == nil {\n1121: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1122: \t\treturn\n1123: \t}\n1124: \twriteJSON(w, s.ListRemoteClusters(), req)\n1125: }\n1126: \n1127: // handlePushRequest handles a ?push=true query param and triggers a push.\n1128: // A boolean response is returned to indicate if the caller should continue\n1129: func (s *DiscoveryServer) handlePushRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) bool {\n1130: \tif err := req.ParseForm(); err != nil {\n1131: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1132: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Failed to parse request\\n\"))\n1133: \t\treturn true\n1134: \t}\n1135: \tif req.Form.Get(\"push\") != \"\" {\n1136: \t\tAdsPushAll(s)\n1137: \t\t_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, \"Pushed to %d servers\\n\", s.adsClientCount())\n1138: \t\treturn true\n1139: \t}\n1140: \treturn false\n1141: }\n1142: \n1143: // getDebugConnection fetches the Connection requested by proxyID\n1144: // For non-system namespaces, restricts access to proxies in the caller's namespace only\n1145: func (s *DiscoveryServer) getDebugConnection(req *http.Request) (string, *Connection) {\n1146: \tif proxyID := req.URL.Query().Get(\"proxyID\"); proxyID != \"\" {\n1147: \t\tcon := s.getProxyConnection(proxyID)\n1148: \t\t// Verify namespace if caller is not from system namespace (when auth is enabled)\n1149: \t\tif features.EnableDebugEndpointAuth {\n1150: \t\t\tcallerNamespace, _ := req.Context().Value(CallerNamespaceKey{}).(string)\n1151: \t\t\tif con != nil && callerNamespace != \"\" {\n1152: \t\t\t\tsystemNamespace := constants.IstioSystemNamespace\n1153: \t\t\t\tif s.Env != nil && s.Env.Mesh() != nil && s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace() != \"\" {\n1154: \t\t\t\t\tsystemNamespace = s.Env.Mesh().GetRootNamespace()\n1155: \t\t\t\t}\n1156: \t\t\t\t// Non-system namespaces can only access proxies in their own namespace\n1157: \t\t\t\tif callerNamespace != systemNamespace && con.proxy.ConfigNamespace != callerNamespace {\n1158: \t\t\t\t\treturn proxyID, nil // Return nil connection to deny access\n1159: \t\t\t\t}\n1160: \t\t\t}\n1161: \t\t}\n1162: \t\treturn proxyID, con\n1163: \t}\n1164: \treturn \"\", nil\n1165: }\n1166: \n1167: func (s *DiscoveryServer) errorHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, proxyID string, con *Connection) {\n1168: \tif proxyID == \"\" {\n1169: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusBadRequest)\n1170: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"You must provide a proxyID in the query string\\n\"))\n1171: \t\treturn\n1172: \t}\n1173: \tif con == nil {\n1174: \t\t// We can't guarantee the Pilot we are connected to has a connection to the proxy we requested\n1175: \t\t// There isn't a great way around this, but for debugging purposes its suitable to have the caller retry.\n1176: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)\n1177: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(\"Proxy not connected to this Pilot instance. It may be connected to another instance.\\n\"))\n1178: \t\treturn\n1179: \t}\n1180: }\n1181: \n1182: // jsonMarshalProto wraps a proto.Message so it can be marshaled with the standard encoding/json library\n1183: type jsonMarshalProto struct {\n1184: \tproto.Message\n1185: }\n1186: \n1187: func (p jsonMarshalProto) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n1188: \treturn protomarshal.Marshal(p.Message)\n1189: }\n1190: \n1191: // writeJSON writes a json payload, handling content type, marshaling, and errors\n1192: func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, obj any, req *http.Request) {\n1193: \tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n1194: \tvar b []byte\n1195: \tvar err error\n1196: \tif req.URL.Query().Has(\"pretty\") {\n1197: \t\tb, err = config.ToPrettyJSON(obj)\n1198: \t} else {\n1199: \t\tb, err = config.ToJSON(obj)\n1200: \t}\n1201: \tif err != nil {\n1202: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1203: \t\t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\n1204: \t\treturn\n1205: \t}\n1206: \t_, err = w.Write(b)\n1207: \tif err != nil {\n1208: \t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1209: \t}\n1210: }\n1211: \n1212: // handleHTTPError writes an error message to the response\n1213: func handleHTTPError(w http.ResponseWriter, err error) {\n1214: \tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n1215: \t_, _ = w.Write([]byte(err.Error()))\n1216: }\n1217: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"350: \\t// Secure gRPC Server must be initialized after CA is created as may use a Citadel generated cert.\\n351: \\tif err := s.initSecureDiscoveryService(args, s.environment.Mesh().GetTrustDomain()); err != nil {\\n352: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error initializing secure gRPC Listener: %v\\\", err)\\n353: \\t}\\n354: \\n355: \\t// common https server for webhooks (e.g. injection, validation)\\n356: \\tif s.kubeClient != nil {\\n357: \\t\\ts.initSecureWebhookServer(args)\\n358: \\t\\twh, err := s.initSidecarInjector(args)\\n359: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n360: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error initializing sidecar injector: %v\\\", err)\\n361: \\t\\t}\\n362: \\t\\ts.readinessFlags.sidecarInjectorReady.Store(true)\\n363: \\t\\ts.webhookInfo.mu.Lock()\\n364: \\t\\ts.webhookInfo.wh = wh\\n365: \\t\\ts.webhookInfo.mu.Unlock()\\n366: \\t\\tif err := s.initConfigValidation(args); err != nil {\\n367: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error initializing config validator: %v\\\", err)\\n368: \\t\\t}\\n369: \\t}\\n370: \\n371: \\t// This should be called only after controllers are initialized.\\n372: \\ts.initRegistryEventHandlers()\\n373: \\n374: \\ts.initDiscoveryService()\\n375: \\n376: \\t// Notice that the order of authenticators matters, since at runtime\\n377: \\t// authenticators are activated sequentially and the first successful attempt\\n378: \\t// is used as the authentication result.\\n379: \\tauthenticators := []security.Authenticator{\\n380: \\t\\t&authenticate.ClientCertAuthenticator{},\\n381: \\t}\\n382: \\tif args.JwtRule != \\\"\\\" {\\n383: \\t\\tjwtAuthn, err := initOIDC(args, s.environment.Watcher)\\n384: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n385: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"error initializing OIDC: %v\\\", err)\\n386: \\t\\t}\\n387: \\t\\tif jwtAuthn == nil {\\n388: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"JWT authenticator is nil\\\")\\n389: \\t\\t}\\n390: \\t\\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, jwtAuthn)\\n391: \\t}\\n392: \\t// The k8s JWT authenticator requires the multicluster registry to be initialized,\\n393: \\t// so we build it later.\\n394: \\tif s.kubeClient != nil {\\n395: \\t\\tauthenticators = append(authenticators,\\n396: \\t\\t\\tkubeauth.NewKubeJWTAuthenticator(\\n397: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.environment.Watcher,\\n398: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.kubeClient.Kube(),\\n399: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.clusterID,\\n400: \\t\\t\\t\\targs.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.ClusterAliases,\\n401: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.multiclusterController))\\n402: \\t}\\n403: \\tif len(features.TrustedGatewayCIDR) > 0 {\\n404: \\t\\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, &authenticate.XfccAuthenticator{})\\n405: \\t}\\n406: \\tif features.XDSAuth {\\n407: \\t\\ts.XDSServer.Authenticators = authenticators\\n408: \\t}\\n409: \\tcaOpts.Authenticators = authenticators\\n410: \\n411: \\t// Start CA or RA server. This should be called after CA and Istiod certs have been created.\\n412: \\ts.startCA(caOpts)\\n413: \\n414: \\t// TODO: don't run this if galley is started, one ctlz is enough\\n415: \\tif args.CtrlZOptions != nil {\\n416: \\t\\t_, _ = ctrlz.Run(args.CtrlZOptions, nil)\\n417: \\t}\\n418: \\n419: \\t// This must be last, otherwise we will not know which informers to register\\n420: \\tif s.kubeClient != nil {\\n421: \\t\\ts.addStartFunc(\\\"kube client\\\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\\n422: \\t\\t\\ts.kubeClient.RunAndWait(stop)\\n423: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n424: \\t\\t})\\n425: \\t}\\n426: \\n427: \\treturn s, nil\\n428: }\\n429: \\n430: func initOIDC(args *PilotArgs, meshWatcher mesh.Watcher) (security.Authenticator, error) {\\n431: \\t// JWTRule is from the JWT_RULE environment variable.\\n432: \\t// An example of json string for JWTRule is:\\n433: \\t// `{\\\"issuer\\\": \\\"foo\\\", \\\"jwks_uri\\\": \\\"baz\\\", \\\"audiences\\\": [\\\"aud1\\\", \\\"aud2\\\"]}`.\\n434: \\tjwtRule := &v1beta1.JWTRule{}\\n435: \\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(args.JwtRule), jwtRule)\\n436: \\tif err != nil {\\n437: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to unmarshal JWT rule: %v\\\", err)\\n438: \\t}\\n439: \\tlog.Infof(\\\"Istiod authenticating using JWTRule: %v\\\", jwtRule)\\n440: \\tjwtAuthn, err := authenticate.NewJwtAuthenticator(jwtRule, meshWatcher)\\n441: \\tif err != nil {\\n442: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create the JWT authenticator: %v\\\", err)\\n443: \\t}\\n444: \\treturn jwtAuthn, nil\\n445: }\\n446: \\n447: func getClusterID(args *PilotArgs) cluster.ID {\\n448: \\tclusterID := args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.ClusterID\\n449: \\tif clusterID == \\\"\\\" {\\n450: \\t\\tif hasKubeRegistry(args.RegistryOptions.Registries) {\\n451: \\t\\t\\tclusterID = cluster.ID(provider.Kubernetes)\\n452: \\t\\t}\\n453: \\t}\\n454: \\treturn clusterID\\n455: }\\n456: \\n457: // Start starts all components of the error serving tap http serverPilot discovery service on the port specified in DiscoveryServerOptions.\\n458: // If Port == 0, a port number is automatically chosen. Content serving is started by this method,\\n459: // but is executed asynchronously. Serving can be canceled at any time by closing the provided stop channel.\\n460: func (s *Server) Start(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\\n461: \\tlog.Infof(\\\"Starting Istiod Server with primary cluster %s\\\", s.clusterID)\\n462: \\n463: \\tif features.UnsafeFeaturesEnabled() {\\n464: \\t\\tlog.Warn(\\\"Server is starting with unsafe features enabled\\\")\\n465: \\t}\\n466: \\n467: \\t// Now start all of the components.\\n468: \\tif err := s.server.Start(stop); err != nil {\\n469: \\t\\treturn err\\n470: \\t}\\n471: \\tif !s.waitForCacheSync(stop) {\\n472: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to sync cache\\\")\\n473: \\t}\\n474: \\t// Inform Discovery Server so that it can start accepting connections.\\n475: \\ts.XDSServer.CachesSynced()\\n476: \\n477: \\t// Race condition - if waitForCache is too fast and we run this as a startup function,\\n478: \\t// the grpc server would be started before CA is registered. Listening should be last.\\n479: \\tif s.secureGrpcAddress != \\\"\\\" {\\n480: \\t\\tgrpcListener, err := net.Listen(\\\"tcp\\\", s.secureGrpcAddress)\\n481: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n482: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n483: \\t\\t}\\n484: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n485: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"starting secure gRPC discovery service at %s\\\", grpcListener.Addr())\\n486: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.secureGrpcServer.Serve(grpcListener); err != nil {\\n487: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"error serving secure GRPC server: %v\\\", err)\\n488: \\t\\t\\t}\\n489: \\t\\t}()\\n490: \\t}\\n491: \\n492: \\tif s.grpcAddress != \\\"\\\" {\\n493: \\t\\tgrpcListener, err := net.Listen(\\\"tcp\\\", s.grpcAddress)\\n494: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n495: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n496: \\t\\t}\\n497: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n498: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"starting gRPC discovery service at %s\\\", grpcListener.Addr())\\n499: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.grpcServer.Serve(grpcListener); err != nil {\\n500: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"error serving GRPC server: %v\\\", err)\\n501: \\t\\t\\t}\\n502: \\t\\t}()\\n503: \\t}\\n504: \\n505: \\tif s.httpsServer != nil {\\n506: \\t\\thttpsListener, err := net.Listen(\\\"tcp\\\", s.httpsServer.Addr)\\n507: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n508: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n509: \\t\\t}\\n510: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n511: \\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"starting webhook service at %s\\\", httpsListener.Addr())\\n512: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.httpsServer.ServeTLS(httpsListener, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\"); network.IsUnexpectedListenerError(err) {\\n513: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"error serving https server: %v\\\", err)\\n514: \\t\\t\\t}\\n515: \\t\\t}()\\n516: \\t\\ts.httpsAddr = httpsListener.Addr().String()\\n517: \\t}\\n518: \\n519: \\ts.waitForShutdown(stop)\\n520: \\n521: \\treturn nil\\n522: }\\n523: \\n524: // WaitUntilCompletion waits for everything marked as a \\\"required termination\\\" to complete.\\n525: // This should be called before exiting.\\n526: func (s *Server) WaitUntilCompletion() {\\n527: \\ts.server.Wait()\\n528: }\\n529: \\n530: // initSDSServer starts the SDS server\\n531: func (s *Server) initSDSServer() {\\n532: \\tif s.kubeClient == nil {\\n533: \\t\\treturn\\n534: \\t}\\n535: \\tif !features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck {\\n536: \\t\\t// Make sure we have security\\n537: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"skipping Kubernetes credential reader; PILOT_ENABLE_XDS_IDENTITY_CHECK must be set to true for this feature.\\\")\\n538: \\t} else {\\n539: \\t\\tcreds := kubecredentials.NewMulticluster(s.clusterID, s.multiclusterController)\\n540: \\t\\tcreds.AddSecretHandler(func(k kind.Kind, name string, namespace string) {\\n541: \\t\\t\\ts.XDSServer.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\\n542: \\t\\t\\t\\tFull:           false,\\n543: \\t\\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: k, Name: name, Namespace: namespace}),\\n544: \\t\\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.SecretTrigger),\\n545: \\t\\t\\t})\\n546: \\t\\t})\\n547: \\t\\ts.environment.CredentialsController = creds\\n548: \\t}\\n549: }\\n550: \\n551: // initKubeClient creates the k8s client if running in a k8s environment.\\n552: // This is determined by the presence of a kube registry, which\\n553: // uses in-context k8s, or a config source of type k8s.\\n554: func (s *Server) initKubeClient(args *PilotArgs) error {\\n555: \\tif s.kubeClient != nil {\\n556: \\t\\t// Already initialized by startup arguments\\n557: \\t\\treturn nil\\n558: \\t}\\n559: \\thasK8SConfigStore := false\\n560: \\tif args.RegistryOptions.FileDir == \\\"\\\" {\\n561: \\t\\t// If file dir is set - config controller will just use file.\\n562: \\t\\tif _, err := os.Stat(args.MeshConfigFile); !os.IsNotExist(err) {\\n563: \\t\\t\\tmeshConfig, err := mesh.ReadMeshConfig(args.MeshConfigFile)\\n564: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n565: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed reading mesh config: %v\\\", err)\\n566: \\t\\t\\t}\\n567: \\t\\t\\tif len(meshConfig.ConfigSources) == 0 && args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig != \\\"\\\" {\\n568: \\t\\t\\t\\thasK8SConfigStore = true\\n569: \\t\\t\\t}\\n570: \\t\\t\\tfor _, cs := range meshConfig.ConfigSources {\\n571: \\t\\t\\t\\tif cs.Address == string(Kubernetes)+\\\"://\\\" {\\n572: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\thasK8SConfigStore = true\\n573: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n574: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n575: \\t\\t\\t}\\n576: \\t\\t} else if args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig != \\\"\\\" {\\n577: \\t\\t\\thasK8SConfigStore = true\\n578: \\t\\t}\\n579: \\t}\\n580: \\n581: \\tif hasK8SConfigStore || hasKubeRegistry(args.RegistryOptions.Registries) {\\n582: \\t\\t// Used by validation\\n583: \\t\\tkubeRestConfig, err := kubelib.DefaultRestConfig(args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig, \\\"\\\", func(config *rest.Config) {\\n584: \\t\\t\\tconfig.QPS = args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.KubernetesAPIQPS\\n585: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Burst = args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.KubernetesAPIBurst\\n586: \\t\\t})\\n587: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n588: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed creating kube config: %v\\\", err)\\n589: \\t\\t}\\n590: \\n591: \\t\\ts.kubeClient, err = kubelib.NewClient(kubelib.NewClientConfigForRestConfig(kubeRestConfig), s.clusterID)\\n592: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n593: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed creating kube client: %v\\\", err)\\n594: \\t\\t}\\n595: \\t\\ts.kubeClient = kubelib.EnableCrdWatcher(s.kubeClient)\\n596: \\t}\\n597: \\n598: \\treturn nil\\n599: }\\n600: \\n601: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go%3FL350-600\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "350: \t// Secure gRPC Server must be initialized after CA is created as may use a Citadel generated cert.\n351: \tif err := s.initSecureDiscoveryService(args, s.environment.Mesh().GetTrustDomain()); err != nil {\n352: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error initializing secure gRPC Listener: %v\", err)\n353: \t}\n354: \n355: \t// common https server for webhooks (e.g. injection, validation)\n356: \tif s.kubeClient != nil {\n357: \t\ts.initSecureWebhookServer(args)\n358: \t\twh, err := s.initSidecarInjector(args)\n359: \t\tif err != nil {\n360: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error initializing sidecar injector: %v\", err)\n361: \t\t}\n362: \t\ts.readinessFlags.sidecarInjectorReady.Store(true)\n363: \t\ts.webhookInfo.mu.Lock()\n364: \t\ts.webhookInfo.wh = wh\n365: \t\ts.webhookInfo.mu.Unlock()\n366: \t\tif err := s.initConfigValidation(args); err != nil {\n367: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error initializing config validator: %v\", err)\n368: \t\t}\n369: \t}\n370: \n371: \t// This should be called only after controllers are initialized.\n372: \ts.initRegistryEventHandlers()\n373: \n374: \ts.initDiscoveryService()\n375: \n376: \t// Notice that the order of authenticators matters, since at runtime\n377: \t// authenticators are activated sequentially and the first successful attempt\n378: \t// is used as the authentication result.\n379: \tauthenticators := []security.Authenticator{\n380: \t\t&authenticate.ClientCertAuthenticator{},\n381: \t}\n382: \tif args.JwtRule != \"\" {\n383: \t\tjwtAuthn, err := initOIDC(args, s.environment.Watcher)\n384: \t\tif err != nil {\n385: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error initializing OIDC: %v\", err)\n386: \t\t}\n387: \t\tif jwtAuthn == nil {\n388: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"JWT authenticator is nil\")\n389: \t\t}\n390: \t\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, jwtAuthn)\n391: \t}\n392: \t// The k8s JWT authenticator requires the multicluster registry to be initialized,\n393: \t// so we build it later.\n394: \tif s.kubeClient != nil {\n395: \t\tauthenticators = append(authenticators,\n396: \t\t\tkubeauth.NewKubeJWTAuthenticator(\n397: \t\t\t\ts.environment.Watcher,\n398: \t\t\t\ts.kubeClient.Kube(),\n399: \t\t\t\ts.clusterID,\n400: \t\t\t\targs.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.ClusterAliases,\n401: \t\t\t\ts.multiclusterController))\n402: \t}\n403: \tif len(features.TrustedGatewayCIDR) > 0 {\n404: \t\tauthenticators = append(authenticators, &authenticate.XfccAuthenticator{})\n405: \t}\n406: \tif features.XDSAuth {\n407: \t\ts.XDSServer.Authenticators = authenticators\n408: \t}\n409: \tcaOpts.Authenticators = authenticators\n410: \n411: \t// Start CA or RA server. This should be called after CA and Istiod certs have been created.\n412: \ts.startCA(caOpts)\n413: \n414: \t// TODO: don't run this if galley is started, one ctlz is enough\n415: \tif args.CtrlZOptions != nil {\n416: \t\t_, _ = ctrlz.Run(args.CtrlZOptions, nil)\n417: \t}\n418: \n419: \t// This must be last, otherwise we will not know which informers to register\n420: \tif s.kubeClient != nil {\n421: \t\ts.addStartFunc(\"kube client\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\n422: \t\t\ts.kubeClient.RunAndWait(stop)\n423: \t\t\treturn nil\n424: \t\t})\n425: \t}\n426: \n427: \treturn s, nil\n428: }\n429: \n430: func initOIDC(args *PilotArgs, meshWatcher mesh.Watcher) (security.Authenticator, error) {\n431: \t// JWTRule is from the JWT_RULE environment variable.\n432: \t// An example of json string for JWTRule is:\n433: \t// `{\"issuer\": \"foo\", \"jwks_uri\": \"baz\", \"audiences\": [\"aud1\", \"aud2\"]}`.\n434: \tjwtRule := &v1beta1.JWTRule{}\n435: \terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(args.JwtRule), jwtRule)\n436: \tif err != nil {\n437: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to unmarshal JWT rule: %v\", err)\n438: \t}\n439: \tlog.Infof(\"Istiod authenticating using JWTRule: %v\", jwtRule)\n440: \tjwtAuthn, err := authenticate.NewJwtAuthenticator(jwtRule, meshWatcher)\n441: \tif err != nil {\n442: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create the JWT authenticator: %v\", err)\n443: \t}\n444: \treturn jwtAuthn, nil\n445: }\n446: \n447: func getClusterID(args *PilotArgs) cluster.ID {\n448: \tclusterID := args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.ClusterID\n449: \tif clusterID == \"\" {\n450: \t\tif hasKubeRegistry(args.RegistryOptions.Registries) {\n451: \t\t\tclusterID = cluster.ID(provider.Kubernetes)\n452: \t\t}\n453: \t}\n454: \treturn clusterID\n455: }\n456: \n457: // Start starts all components of the error serving tap http serverPilot discovery service on the port specified in DiscoveryServerOptions.\n458: // If Port == 0, a port number is automatically chosen. Content serving is started by this method,\n459: // but is executed asynchronously. Serving can be canceled at any time by closing the provided stop channel.\n460: func (s *Server) Start(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\n461: \tlog.Infof(\"Starting Istiod Server with primary cluster %s\", s.clusterID)\n462: \n463: \tif features.UnsafeFeaturesEnabled() {\n464: \t\tlog.Warn(\"Server is starting with unsafe features enabled\")\n465: \t}\n466: \n467: \t// Now start all of the components.\n468: \tif err := s.server.Start(stop); err != nil {\n469: \t\treturn err\n470: \t}\n471: \tif !s.waitForCacheSync(stop) {\n472: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to sync cache\")\n473: \t}\n474: \t// Inform Discovery Server so that it can start accepting connections.\n475: \ts.XDSServer.CachesSynced()\n476: \n477: \t// Race condition - if waitForCache is too fast and we run this as a startup function,\n478: \t// the grpc server would be started before CA is registered. Listening should be last.\n479: \tif s.secureGrpcAddress != \"\" {\n480: \t\tgrpcListener, err := net.Listen(\"tcp\", s.secureGrpcAddress)\n481: \t\tif err != nil {\n482: \t\t\treturn err\n483: \t\t}\n484: \t\tgo func() {\n485: \t\t\tlog.Infof(\"starting secure gRPC discovery service at %s\", grpcListener.Addr())\n486: \t\t\tif err := s.secureGrpcServer.Serve(grpcListener); err != nil {\n487: \t\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"error serving secure GRPC server: %v\", err)\n488: \t\t\t}\n489: \t\t}()\n490: \t}\n491: \n492: \tif s.grpcAddress != \"\" {\n493: \t\tgrpcListener, err := net.Listen(\"tcp\", s.grpcAddress)\n494: \t\tif err != nil {\n495: \t\t\treturn err\n496: \t\t}\n497: \t\tgo func() {\n498: \t\t\tlog.Infof(\"starting gRPC discovery service at %s\", grpcListener.Addr())\n499: \t\t\tif err := s.grpcServer.Serve(grpcListener); err != nil {\n500: \t\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"error serving GRPC server: %v\", err)\n501: \t\t\t}\n502: \t\t}()\n503: \t}\n504: \n505: \tif s.httpsServer != nil {\n506: \t\thttpsListener, err := net.Listen(\"tcp\", s.httpsServer.Addr)\n507: \t\tif err != nil {\n508: \t\t\treturn err\n509: \t\t}\n510: \t\tgo func() {\n511: \t\t\tlog.Infof(\"starting webhook service at %s\", httpsListener.Addr())\n512: \t\t\tif err := s.httpsServer.ServeTLS(httpsListener, \"\", \"\"); network.IsUnexpectedListenerError(err) {\n513: \t\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"error serving https server: %v\", err)\n514: \t\t\t}\n515: \t\t}()\n516: \t\ts.httpsAddr = httpsListener.Addr().String()\n517: \t}\n518: \n519: \ts.waitForShutdown(stop)\n520: \n521: \treturn nil\n522: }\n523: \n524: // WaitUntilCompletion waits for everything marked as a \"required termination\" to complete.\n525: // This should be called before exiting.\n526: func (s *Server) WaitUntilCompletion() {\n527: \ts.server.Wait()\n528: }\n529: \n530: // initSDSServer starts the SDS server\n531: func (s *Server) initSDSServer() {\n532: \tif s.kubeClient == nil {\n533: \t\treturn\n534: \t}\n535: \tif !features.EnableXDSIdentityCheck {\n536: \t\t// Make sure we have security\n537: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"skipping Kubernetes credential reader; PILOT_ENABLE_XDS_IDENTITY_CHECK must be set to true for this feature.\")\n538: \t} else {\n539: \t\tcreds := kubecredentials.NewMulticluster(s.clusterID, s.multiclusterController)\n540: \t\tcreds.AddSecretHandler(func(k kind.Kind, name string, namespace string) {\n541: \t\t\ts.XDSServer.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n542: \t\t\t\tFull:           false,\n543: \t\t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: k, Name: name, Namespace: namespace}),\n544: \t\t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.SecretTrigger),\n545: \t\t\t})\n546: \t\t})\n547: \t\ts.environment.CredentialsController = creds\n548: \t}\n549: }\n550: \n551: // initKubeClient creates the k8s client if running in a k8s environment.\n552: // This is determined by the presence of a kube registry, which\n553: // uses in-context k8s, or a config source of type k8s.\n554: func (s *Server) initKubeClient(args *PilotArgs) error {\n555: \tif s.kubeClient != nil {\n556: \t\t// Already initialized by startup arguments\n557: \t\treturn nil\n558: \t}\n559: \thasK8SConfigStore := false\n560: \tif args.RegistryOptions.FileDir == \"\" {\n561: \t\t// If file dir is set - config controller will just use file.\n562: \t\tif _, err := os.Stat(args.MeshConfigFile); !os.IsNotExist(err) {\n563: \t\t\tmeshConfig, err := mesh.ReadMeshConfig(args.MeshConfigFile)\n564: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n565: \t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed reading mesh config: %v\", err)\n566: \t\t\t}\n567: \t\t\tif len(meshConfig.ConfigSources) == 0 && args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig != \"\" {\n568: \t\t\t\thasK8SConfigStore = true\n569: \t\t\t}\n570: \t\t\tfor _, cs := range meshConfig.ConfigSources {\n571: \t\t\t\tif cs.Address == string(Kubernetes)+\"://\" {\n572: \t\t\t\t\thasK8SConfigStore = true\n573: \t\t\t\t\tbreak\n574: \t\t\t\t}\n575: \t\t\t}\n576: \t\t} else if args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig != \"\" {\n577: \t\t\thasK8SConfigStore = true\n578: \t\t}\n579: \t}\n580: \n581: \tif hasK8SConfigStore || hasKubeRegistry(args.RegistryOptions.Registries) {\n582: \t\t// Used by validation\n583: \t\tkubeRestConfig, err := kubelib.DefaultRestConfig(args.RegistryOptions.KubeConfig, \"\", func(config *rest.Config) {\n584: \t\t\tconfig.QPS = args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.KubernetesAPIQPS\n585: \t\t\tconfig.Burst = args.RegistryOptions.KubeOptions.KubernetesAPIBurst\n586: \t\t})\n587: \t\tif err != nil {\n588: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed creating kube config: %v\", err)\n589: \t\t}\n590: \n591: \t\ts.kubeClient, err = kubelib.NewClient(kubelib.NewClientConfigForRestConfig(kubeRestConfig), s.clusterID)\n592: \t\tif err != nil {\n593: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed creating kube client: %v\", err)\n594: \t\t}\n595: \t\ts.kubeClient = kubelib.EnableCrdWatcher(s.kubeClient)\n596: \t}\n597: \n598: \treturn nil\n599: }\n600: \n601: ",

  }
