Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"600: \\n601: // A single container can't have two readiness probes. Make this readiness probe a generic one\\n602: // that can handle all istiod related readiness checks including webhook, gRPC etc.\\n603: // The \\\"http\\\" portion of the readiness check is satisfied by the fact we've started listening on\\n604: // this handler and everything has already initialized.\\n605: func (s *Server) istiodReadyHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {\\n606: \\tfor name, fn := range s.readinessProbes {\\n607: \\t\\tif ready := fn(); !ready {\\n608: \\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"%s is not ready\\\", name)\\n609: \\t\\t\\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\\n610: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n611: \\t\\t}\\n612: \\t}\\n613: \\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\\n614: }\\n615: \\n616: // initServers initializes http and grpc servers\\n617: func (s *Server) initServers(args *PilotArgs) {\\n618: \\ts.initGrpcServer(args.KeepaliveOptions)\\n619: \\tmultiplexGRPC := false\\n620: \\tif args.ServerOptions.GRPCAddr != \\\"\\\" {\\n621: \\t\\ts.grpcAddress = args.ServerOptions.GRPCAddr\\n622: \\t} else {\\n623: \\t\\t// This happens only if the GRPC port (15010) is disabled. We will multiplex\\n624: \\t\\t// it on the HTTP port. Does not impact the HTTPS gRPC or HTTPS.\\n625: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"multiplexing gRPC on http addr %v\\\", args.ServerOptions.HTTPAddr)\\n626: \\t\\tmultiplexGRPC = true\\n627: \\t}\\n628: \\th2s := &http2.Server{\\n629: \\t\\tMaxConcurrentStreams: uint32(features.MaxConcurrentStreams),\\n630: \\t}\\n631: \\tmultiplexHandler := h2c.NewHandler(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n632: \\t\\t// If we detect gRPC, serve using grpcServer\\n633: \\t\\tif r.ProtoMajor == 2 && strings.HasPrefix(r.Header.Get(\\\"content-type\\\"), \\\"application/grpc\\\") {\\n634: \\t\\t\\ts.grpcServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n635: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n636: \\t\\t}\\n637: \\t\\t// Otherwise, this is meant for the standard HTTP server\\n638: \\t\\ts.httpMux.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n639: \\t}), h2s)\\n640: \\ts.httpServer = &http.Server{\\n641: \\t\\tAddr:        args.ServerOptions.HTTPAddr,\\n642: \\t\\tHandler:     s.httpMux,\\n643: \\t\\tIdleTimeout: 90 * time.Second, // matches http.DefaultTransport keep-alive timeout\\n644: \\t\\tReadTimeout: 30 * time.Second,\\n645: \\t}\\n646: \\tif multiplexGRPC {\\n647: \\t\\t// To allow the gRPC handler to make per-request decision,\\n648: \\t\\t// use ReadHeaderTimeout instead of ReadTimeout.\\n649: \\t\\ts.httpServer.ReadTimeout = 0\\n650: \\t\\ts.httpServer.ReadHeaderTimeout = 30 * time.Second\\n651: \\t\\ts.httpServer.Handler = multiplexHandler\\n652: \\t}\\n653: \\n654: \\tif args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr == \\\"\\\" {\\n655: \\t\\ts.monitoringMux = s.httpMux\\n656: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"initializing Istiod admin server multiplexed on httpAddr %v\\\", s.httpServer.Addr)\\n657: \\t} else {\\n658: \\t\\tlog.Info(\\\"initializing Istiod admin server\\\")\\n659: \\t}\\n660: }\\n661: \\n662: // initIstiodAdminServer initializes monitoring, debug and readiness end points.\\n663: func (s *Server) initIstiodAdminServer(args *PilotArgs, whc func() map[string]string) error {\\n664: \\t// Debug Server.\\n665: \\tinternalMux := s.XDSServer.InitDebug(s.monitoringMux, args.ServerOptions.EnableProfiling, whc)\\n666: \\ts.internalDebugMux = internalMux\\n667: \\n668: \\t// Debug handlers are currently added on monitoring mux and readiness mux.\\n669: \\t// If monitoring addr is empty, the mux is shared and we only add it once on the shared mux .\\n670: \\tif args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr != \\\"\\\" {\\n671: \\t\\ts.XDSServer.AddDebugHandlers(s.httpMux, nil, args.ServerOptions.EnableProfiling, whc)\\n672: \\t}\\n673: \\n674: \\t// Monitoring Server.\\n675: \\tif err := s.initMonitor(args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr); err != nil {\\n676: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"error initializing monitor: %v\\\", err)\\n677: \\t}\\n678: \\n679: \\t// Readiness Handler.\\n680: \\ts.httpMux.HandleFunc(\\\"/ready\\\", s.istiodReadyHandler)\\n681: \\n682: \\treturn nil\\n683: }\\n684: \\n685: // initDiscoveryService initializes discovery server on plain text port.\\n686: func (s *Server) initDiscoveryService() {\\n687: \\tlog.Infof(\\\"starting discovery service\\\")\\n688: \\t// Implement EnvoyXdsServer grace shutdown\\n689: \\ts.addStartFunc(\\\"xds server\\\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\\n690: \\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Starting ADS server\\\")\\n691: \\t\\ts.XDSServer.Start(stop)\\n692: \\t\\treturn nil\\n693: \\t})\\n694: }\\n695: \\n696: // Wait for the stop, and do cleanups\\n697: func (s *Server) waitForShutdown(stop <-chan struct{}) {\\n698: \\tgo func() {\\n699: \\t\\t<-stop\\n700: \\t\\tclose(s.internalStop)\\n701: \\t\\t_ = s.fileWatcher.Close()\\n702: \\n703: \\t\\tif s.cacertsWatcher != nil {\\n704: \\t\\t\\t_ = s.cacertsWatcher.Close()\\n705: \\t\\t}\\n706: \\t\\t// Stop gRPC services.  If gRPC services fail to stop in the shutdown duration,\\n707: \\t\\t// force stop them. This does not happen normally.\\n708: \\t\\tstopped := make(chan struct{})\\n709: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n710: \\t\\t\\t// Some grpcServer implementations do not support GracefulStop. Unfortunately, this is not\\n711: \\t\\t\\t// exposed; they just panic. To avoid this, we will recover and do a standard Stop when its not\\n712: \\t\\t\\t// support.\\n713: \\t\\t\\tdefer func() {\\n714: \\t\\t\\t\\tif r := recover(); r != nil {\\n715: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\ts.grpcServer.Stop()\\n716: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\\n717: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\\n718: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n719: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tclose(stopped)\\n720: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n721: \\t\\t\\t}()\\n722: \\t\\t\\ts.grpcServer.GracefulStop()\\n723: \\t\\t\\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\\n724: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.secureGrpcServer.GracefulStop()\\n725: \\t\\t\\t}\\n726: \\t\\t\\tclose(stopped)\\n727: \\t\\t}()\\n728: \\n729: \\t\\tt := time.NewTimer(s.shutdownDuration)\\n730: \\t\\tselect {\\n731: \\t\\tcase <-t.C:\\n732: \\t\\t\\ts.grpcServer.Stop()\\n733: \\t\\t\\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\\n734: \\t\\t\\t\\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\\n735: \\t\\t\\t}\\n736: \\t\\tcase <-stopped:\\n737: \\t\\t\\tt.Stop()\\n738: \\t\\t}\\n739: \\n740: \\t\\t// Stop HTTP services.\\n741: \\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), s.shutdownDuration)\\n742: \\t\\tdefer cancel()\\n743: \\t\\tif err := s.httpServer.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {\\n744: \\t\\t\\tlog.Warn(err)\\n745: \\t\\t}\\n746: \\t\\tif s.httpsServer != nil {\\n747: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.httpsServer.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {\\n748: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Warn(err)\\n749: \\t\\t\\t}\\n750: \\t\\t}\\n751: \\n752: \\t\\t// Shutdown the DiscoveryServer.\\n753: \\t\\ts.XDSServer.Shutdown()\\n754: \\t}()\\n755: }\\n756: \\n757: func (s *Server) initGrpcServer(options *istiokeepalive.Options) {\\n758: \\tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\\n759: \\t\\t// setup server prometheus monitoring (as final interceptor in chain)\\n760: \\t\\tgrpcprom.UnaryServerInterceptor,\\n761: \\t}\\n762: \\tgrpcOptions := istiogrpc.ServerOptions(options, xdspkg.RecordRecvSize, interceptors...)\\n763: \\ts.grpcServer = grpc.NewServer(grpcOptions...)\\n764: \\ts.XDSServer.Register(s.grpcServer)\\n765: \\treflection.Register(s.grpcServer)\\n766: }\\n767: \\n768: // initialize secureGRPCServer.\\n769: func (s *Server) initSecureDiscoveryService(args *PilotArgs, trustDomain string) error {\\n770: \\tif args.ServerOptions.SecureGRPCAddr == \\\"\\\" {\\n771: \\t\\tlog.Info(\\\"The secure discovery port is disabled, multiplexing on httpAddr \\\")\\n772: \\t\\treturn nil\\n773: \\t}\\n774: \\n775: \\tpeerCertVerifier, err := s.createPeerCertVerifier(args.ServerOptions.TLSOptions, trustDomain)\\n776: \\tif err != nil {\\n777: \\t\\treturn err\\n778: \\t}\\n779: \\tif peerCertVerifier == nil {\\n780: \\t\\t// Running locally without configured certs - no TLS mode\\n781: \\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"The secure discovery service is disabled\\\")\\n782: \\t\\treturn nil\\n783: \\t}\\n784: \\tlog.Info(\\\"initializing secure discovery service\\\")\\n785: \\n786: \\tcfg := &tls.Config{\\n787: \\t\\tGetCertificate: s.getIstiodCertificate,\\n788: \\t\\tClientAuth:     tls.VerifyClientCertIfGiven,\\n789: \\t\\tClientCAs:      peerCertVerifier.GetGeneralCertPool(),\\n790: \\t\\tVerifyPeerCertificate: func(rawCerts [][]byte, verifiedChains [][]*x509.Certificate) error {\\n791: \\t\\t\\terr := peerCertVerifier.VerifyPeerCert(rawCerts, verifiedChains)\\n792: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n793: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Infof(\\\"Could not verify certificate: %v\\\", err)\\n794: \\t\\t\\t}\\n795: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n796: \\t\\t},\\n797: \\t\\tMinVersion:   tls.VersionTLS12,\\n798: \\t\\tCipherSuites: args.ServerOptions.TLSOptions.CipherSuites,\\n799: \\t}\\n800: \\t// Compliance for xDS server TLS.\\n801: \\tsec_model.EnforceGoCompliance(cfg)\\n802: \\n803: \\ttlsCreds := credentials.NewTLS(cfg)\\n804: \\n805: \\ts.secureGrpcAddress = args.ServerOptions.SecureGRPCAddr\\n806: \\n807: \\tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\\n808: \\t\\t// setup server prometheus monitoring (as final interceptor in chain)\\n809: \\t\\tgrpcprom.UnaryServerInterceptor,\\n810: \\t}\\n811: \\topts := istiogrpc.ServerOptions(args.KeepaliveOptions, xdspkg.RecordRecvSize, interceptors...)\\n812: \\topts = append(opts, grpc.Creds(tlsCreds))\\n813: \\n814: \\ts.secureGrpcServer = grpc.NewServer(opts...)\\n815: \\ts.XDSServer.Register(s.secureGrpcServer)\\n816: \\treflection.Register(s.secureGrpcServer)\\n817: \\n818: \\ts.addStartFunc(\\\"secure gRPC\\\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\\n819: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n820: \\t\\t\\t<-stop\\n821: \\t\\t\\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\\n822: \\t\\t}()\\n823: \\t\\treturn nil\\n824: \\t})\\n825: \\n826: \\treturn nil\\n827: }\\n828: \\n829: // addStartFunc appends a function to be run. These are run synchronously in order,\\n830: // so the function should start a go routine if it needs to do anything blocking\\n831: func (s *Server) addStartFunc(name string, fn server.Component) {\\n832: \\ts.server.RunComponent(name, fn)\\n833: }\\n834: \\n835: // adds a readiness probe for Istiod Server.\\n836: func (s *Server) addReadinessProbe(name string, fn readinessProbe) {\\n837: \\ts.readinessProbes[name] = fn\\n838: }\\n839: \\n840: // addTerminatingStartFunc adds a function that should terminate before the serve shuts down\\n841: // This is useful to do cleanup activities\\n842: // This is does not guarantee they will terminate gracefully - best effort only\\n843: // Function should be synchronous; once it returns it is considered \\\"done\\\"\\n844: func (s *Server) addTerminatingStartFunc(name string, fn server.Component) {\\n845: \\ts.server.RunComponentAsyncAndWait(name, fn)\\n846: }\\n847: \\n848: func (s *Server) waitForCacheSync(stop <-chan struct{}) bool {\\n849: \\tstart := time.Now()\\n850: \\tlog.Info(\\\"Waiting for caches to be synced\\\")\\n851: \\tif !kubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\\\"server\\\", stop, s.cachesSynced) {\\n852: \\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"Failed waiting for cache sync\\\")\\n853: \\t\\treturn false\\n854: \\t}\\n855: \\tlog.Infof(\\\"All controller caches have been synced up in %v\\\", time.Since(start))\\n856: \\n857: \\t// At this point, we know that all update events of the initial state-of-the-world have been\\n858: \\t// received. We wait to ensure we have committed at least this many updates. This avoids a race\\n859: \\t// condition where we are marked ready prior to updating the push context, leading to incomplete\\n860: \\t// pushes.\\n861: \\texpected := s.XDSServer.InboundUpdates.Load()\\n862: \\treturn kubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\\\"push context\\\", stop, func() bool { return s.pushContextReady(expected) })\\n863: }\\n864: \\n865: // pushContextReady indicates whether pushcontext has processed all inbound config updates.\\n866: func (s *Server) pushContextReady(expected int64) bool {\\n867: \\tcommitted := s.XDSServer.CommittedUpdates.Load()\\n868: \\tif committed < expected {\\n869: \\t\\tlog.Debugf(\\\"Waiting for pushcontext to process inbound updates, inbound: %v, committed : %v\\\", expected, committed)\\n870: \\t\\treturn false\\n871: \\t}\\n872: \\treturn true\\n873: }\\n874: \\n875: // cachesSynced checks whether caches have been synced.\\n876: func (s *Server) cachesSynced() bool {\\n877: \\tif s.multiclusterController != nil && !s.multiclusterController.HasSynced() {\\n878: \\t\\treturn false\\n879: \\t}\\n880: \\tif !s.ServiceController().HasSynced() {\\n881: \\t\\treturn false\\n882: \\t}\\n883: \\tif !s.configController.HasSynced() {\\n884: \\t\\treturn false\\n885: \\t}\\n886: \\treturn true\\n887: }\\n888: \\n889: // initRegistryEventHandlers sets up event handlers for config and service updates\\n890: func (s *Server) initRegistryEventHandlers() {\\n891: \\tlog.Info(\\\"initializing registry event handlers\\\")\\n892: \\t// Flush cached discovery responses whenever services configuration change.\\n893: \\tserviceHandler := func(prev, curr *model.Service, event model.Event) {\\n894: \\t\\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\\n895: \\t\\t\\tFull:           true,\\n896: \\t\\t\\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: string(curr.Hostname), Namespace: curr.Attributes.Namespace}),\\n897: \\t\\t\\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ServiceUpdate),\\n898: \\t\\t}\\n899: \\t\\ts.XDSServer.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\\n900: \\t}\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go%3FL600-900\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "600: \n601: // A single container can't have two readiness probes. Make this readiness probe a generic one\n602: // that can handle all istiod related readiness checks including webhook, gRPC etc.\n603: // The \"http\" portion of the readiness check is satisfied by the fact we've started listening on\n604: // this handler and everything has already initialized.\n605: func (s *Server) istiodReadyHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, _ *http.Request) {\n606: \tfor name, fn := range s.readinessProbes {\n607: \t\tif ready := fn(); !ready {\n608: \t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"%s is not ready\", name)\n609: \t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n610: \t\t\treturn\n611: \t\t}\n612: \t}\n613: \tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n614: }\n615: \n616: // initServers initializes http and grpc servers\n617: func (s *Server) initServers(args *PilotArgs) {\n618: \ts.initGrpcServer(args.KeepaliveOptions)\n619: \tmultiplexGRPC := false\n620: \tif args.ServerOptions.GRPCAddr != \"\" {\n621: \t\ts.grpcAddress = args.ServerOptions.GRPCAddr\n622: \t} else {\n623: \t\t// This happens only if the GRPC port (15010) is disabled. We will multiplex\n624: \t\t// it on the HTTP port. Does not impact the HTTPS gRPC or HTTPS.\n625: \t\tlog.Infof(\"multiplexing gRPC on http addr %v\", args.ServerOptions.HTTPAddr)\n626: \t\tmultiplexGRPC = true\n627: \t}\n628: \th2s := &http2.Server{\n629: \t\tMaxConcurrentStreams: uint32(features.MaxConcurrentStreams),\n630: \t}\n631: \tmultiplexHandler := h2c.NewHandler(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n632: \t\t// If we detect gRPC, serve using grpcServer\n633: \t\tif r.ProtoMajor == 2 && strings.HasPrefix(r.Header.Get(\"content-type\"), \"application/grpc\") {\n634: \t\t\ts.grpcServer.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n635: \t\t\treturn\n636: \t\t}\n637: \t\t// Otherwise, this is meant for the standard HTTP server\n638: \t\ts.httpMux.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n639: \t}), h2s)\n640: \ts.httpServer = &http.Server{\n641: \t\tAddr:        args.ServerOptions.HTTPAddr,\n642: \t\tHandler:     s.httpMux,\n643: \t\tIdleTimeout: 90 * time.Second, // matches http.DefaultTransport keep-alive timeout\n644: \t\tReadTimeout: 30 * time.Second,\n645: \t}\n646: \tif multiplexGRPC {\n647: \t\t// To allow the gRPC handler to make per-request decision,\n648: \t\t// use ReadHeaderTimeout instead of ReadTimeout.\n649: \t\ts.httpServer.ReadTimeout = 0\n650: \t\ts.httpServer.ReadHeaderTimeout = 30 * time.Second\n651: \t\ts.httpServer.Handler = multiplexHandler\n652: \t}\n653: \n654: \tif args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr == \"\" {\n655: \t\ts.monitoringMux = s.httpMux\n656: \t\tlog.Infof(\"initializing Istiod admin server multiplexed on httpAddr %v\", s.httpServer.Addr)\n657: \t} else {\n658: \t\tlog.Info(\"initializing Istiod admin server\")\n659: \t}\n660: }\n661: \n662: // initIstiodAdminServer initializes monitoring, debug and readiness end points.\n663: func (s *Server) initIstiodAdminServer(args *PilotArgs, whc func() map[string]string) error {\n664: \t// Debug Server.\n665: \tinternalMux := s.XDSServer.InitDebug(s.monitoringMux, args.ServerOptions.EnableProfiling, whc)\n666: \ts.internalDebugMux = internalMux\n667: \n668: \t// Debug handlers are currently added on monitoring mux and readiness mux.\n669: \t// If monitoring addr is empty, the mux is shared and we only add it once on the shared mux .\n670: \tif args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr != \"\" {\n671: \t\ts.XDSServer.AddDebugHandlers(s.httpMux, nil, args.ServerOptions.EnableProfiling, whc)\n672: \t}\n673: \n674: \t// Monitoring Server.\n675: \tif err := s.initMonitor(args.ServerOptions.MonitoringAddr); err != nil {\n676: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error initializing monitor: %v\", err)\n677: \t}\n678: \n679: \t// Readiness Handler.\n680: \ts.httpMux.HandleFunc(\"/ready\", s.istiodReadyHandler)\n681: \n682: \treturn nil\n683: }\n684: \n685: // initDiscoveryService initializes discovery server on plain text port.\n686: func (s *Server) initDiscoveryService() {\n687: \tlog.Infof(\"starting discovery service\")\n688: \t// Implement EnvoyXdsServer grace shutdown\n689: \ts.addStartFunc(\"xds server\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\n690: \t\tlog.Infof(\"Starting ADS server\")\n691: \t\ts.XDSServer.Start(stop)\n692: \t\treturn nil\n693: \t})\n694: }\n695: \n696: // Wait for the stop, and do cleanups\n697: func (s *Server) waitForShutdown(stop <-chan struct{}) {\n698: \tgo func() {\n699: \t\t<-stop\n700: \t\tclose(s.internalStop)\n701: \t\t_ = s.fileWatcher.Close()\n702: \n703: \t\tif s.cacertsWatcher != nil {\n704: \t\t\t_ = s.cacertsWatcher.Close()\n705: \t\t}\n706: \t\t// Stop gRPC services.  If gRPC services fail to stop in the shutdown duration,\n707: \t\t// force stop them. This does not happen normally.\n708: \t\tstopped := make(chan struct{})\n709: \t\tgo func() {\n710: \t\t\t// Some grpcServer implementations do not support GracefulStop. Unfortunately, this is not\n711: \t\t\t// exposed; they just panic. To avoid this, we will recover and do a standard Stop when its not\n712: \t\t\t// support.\n713: \t\t\tdefer func() {\n714: \t\t\t\tif r := recover(); r != nil {\n715: \t\t\t\t\ts.grpcServer.Stop()\n716: \t\t\t\t\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\n717: \t\t\t\t\t\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\n718: \t\t\t\t\t}\n719: \t\t\t\t\tclose(stopped)\n720: \t\t\t\t}\n721: \t\t\t}()\n722: \t\t\ts.grpcServer.GracefulStop()\n723: \t\t\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\n724: \t\t\t\ts.secureGrpcServer.GracefulStop()\n725: \t\t\t}\n726: \t\t\tclose(stopped)\n727: \t\t}()\n728: \n729: \t\tt := time.NewTimer(s.shutdownDuration)\n730: \t\tselect {\n731: \t\tcase <-t.C:\n732: \t\t\ts.grpcServer.Stop()\n733: \t\t\tif s.secureGrpcServer != nil {\n734: \t\t\t\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\n735: \t\t\t}\n736: \t\tcase <-stopped:\n737: \t\t\tt.Stop()\n738: \t\t}\n739: \n740: \t\t// Stop HTTP services.\n741: \t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), s.shutdownDuration)\n742: \t\tdefer cancel()\n743: \t\tif err := s.httpServer.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {\n744: \t\t\tlog.Warn(err)\n745: \t\t}\n746: \t\tif s.httpsServer != nil {\n747: \t\t\tif err := s.httpsServer.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {\n748: \t\t\t\tlog.Warn(err)\n749: \t\t\t}\n750: \t\t}\n751: \n752: \t\t// Shutdown the DiscoveryServer.\n753: \t\ts.XDSServer.Shutdown()\n754: \t}()\n755: }\n756: \n757: func (s *Server) initGrpcServer(options *istiokeepalive.Options) {\n758: \tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\n759: \t\t// setup server prometheus monitoring (as final interceptor in chain)\n760: \t\tgrpcprom.UnaryServerInterceptor,\n761: \t}\n762: \tgrpcOptions := istiogrpc.ServerOptions(options, xdspkg.RecordRecvSize, interceptors...)\n763: \ts.grpcServer = grpc.NewServer(grpcOptions...)\n764: \ts.XDSServer.Register(s.grpcServer)\n765: \treflection.Register(s.grpcServer)\n766: }\n767: \n768: // initialize secureGRPCServer.\n769: func (s *Server) initSecureDiscoveryService(args *PilotArgs, trustDomain string) error {\n770: \tif args.ServerOptions.SecureGRPCAddr == \"\" {\n771: \t\tlog.Info(\"The secure discovery port is disabled, multiplexing on httpAddr \")\n772: \t\treturn nil\n773: \t}\n774: \n775: \tpeerCertVerifier, err := s.createPeerCertVerifier(args.ServerOptions.TLSOptions, trustDomain)\n776: \tif err != nil {\n777: \t\treturn err\n778: \t}\n779: \tif peerCertVerifier == nil {\n780: \t\t// Running locally without configured certs - no TLS mode\n781: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"The secure discovery service is disabled\")\n782: \t\treturn nil\n783: \t}\n784: \tlog.Info(\"initializing secure discovery service\")\n785: \n786: \tcfg := &tls.Config{\n787: \t\tGetCertificate: s.getIstiodCertificate,\n788: \t\tClientAuth:     tls.VerifyClientCertIfGiven,\n789: \t\tClientCAs:      peerCertVerifier.GetGeneralCertPool(),\n790: \t\tVerifyPeerCertificate: func(rawCerts [][]byte, verifiedChains [][]*x509.Certificate) error {\n791: \t\t\terr := peerCertVerifier.VerifyPeerCert(rawCerts, verifiedChains)\n792: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n793: \t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"Could not verify certificate: %v\", err)\n794: \t\t\t}\n795: \t\t\treturn err\n796: \t\t},\n797: \t\tMinVersion:   tls.VersionTLS12,\n798: \t\tCipherSuites: args.ServerOptions.TLSOptions.CipherSuites,\n799: \t}\n800: \t// Compliance for xDS server TLS.\n801: \tsec_model.EnforceGoCompliance(cfg)\n802: \n803: \ttlsCreds := credentials.NewTLS(cfg)\n804: \n805: \ts.secureGrpcAddress = args.ServerOptions.SecureGRPCAddr\n806: \n807: \tinterceptors := []grpc.UnaryServerInterceptor{\n808: \t\t// setup server prometheus monitoring (as final interceptor in chain)\n809: \t\tgrpcprom.UnaryServerInterceptor,\n810: \t}\n811: \topts := istiogrpc.ServerOptions(args.KeepaliveOptions, xdspkg.RecordRecvSize, interceptors...)\n812: \topts = append(opts, grpc.Creds(tlsCreds))\n813: \n814: \ts.secureGrpcServer = grpc.NewServer(opts...)\n815: \ts.XDSServer.Register(s.secureGrpcServer)\n816: \treflection.Register(s.secureGrpcServer)\n817: \n818: \ts.addStartFunc(\"secure gRPC\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\n819: \t\tgo func() {\n820: \t\t\t<-stop\n821: \t\t\ts.secureGrpcServer.Stop()\n822: \t\t}()\n823: \t\treturn nil\n824: \t})\n825: \n826: \treturn nil\n827: }\n828: \n829: // addStartFunc appends a function to be run. These are run synchronously in order,\n830: // so the function should start a go routine if it needs to do anything blocking\n831: func (s *Server) addStartFunc(name string, fn server.Component) {\n832: \ts.server.RunComponent(name, fn)\n833: }\n834: \n835: // adds a readiness probe for Istiod Server.\n836: func (s *Server) addReadinessProbe(name string, fn readinessProbe) {\n837: \ts.readinessProbes[name] = fn\n838: }\n839: \n840: // addTerminatingStartFunc adds a function that should terminate before the serve shuts down\n841: // This is useful to do cleanup activities\n842: // This is does not guarantee they will terminate gracefully - best effort only\n843: // Function should be synchronous; once it returns it is considered \"done\"\n844: func (s *Server) addTerminatingStartFunc(name string, fn server.Component) {\n845: \ts.server.RunComponentAsyncAndWait(name, fn)\n846: }\n847: \n848: func (s *Server) waitForCacheSync(stop <-chan struct{}) bool {\n849: \tstart := time.Now()\n850: \tlog.Info(\"Waiting for caches to be synced\")\n851: \tif !kubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\"server\", stop, s.cachesSynced) {\n852: \t\tlog.Errorf(\"Failed waiting for cache sync\")\n853: \t\treturn false\n854: \t}\n855: \tlog.Infof(\"All controller caches have been synced up in %v\", time.Since(start))\n856: \n857: \t// At this point, we know that all update events of the initial state-of-the-world have been\n858: \t// received. We wait to ensure we have committed at least this many updates. This avoids a race\n859: \t// condition where we are marked ready prior to updating the push context, leading to incomplete\n860: \t// pushes.\n861: \texpected := s.XDSServer.InboundUpdates.Load()\n862: \treturn kubelib.WaitForCacheSync(\"push context\", stop, func() bool { return s.pushContextReady(expected) })\n863: }\n864: \n865: // pushContextReady indicates whether pushcontext has processed all inbound config updates.\n866: func (s *Server) pushContextReady(expected int64) bool {\n867: \tcommitted := s.XDSServer.CommittedUpdates.Load()\n868: \tif committed < expected {\n869: \t\tlog.Debugf(\"Waiting for pushcontext to process inbound updates, inbound: %v, committed : %v\", expected, committed)\n870: \t\treturn false\n871: \t}\n872: \treturn true\n873: }\n874: \n875: // cachesSynced checks whether caches have been synced.\n876: func (s *Server) cachesSynced() bool {\n877: \tif s.multiclusterController != nil && !s.multiclusterController.HasSynced() {\n878: \t\treturn false\n879: \t}\n880: \tif !s.ServiceController().HasSynced() {\n881: \t\treturn false\n882: \t}\n883: \tif !s.configController.HasSynced() {\n884: \t\treturn false\n885: \t}\n886: \treturn true\n887: }\n888: \n889: // initRegistryEventHandlers sets up event handlers for config and service updates\n890: func (s *Server) initRegistryEventHandlers() {\n891: \tlog.Info(\"initializing registry event handlers\")\n892: \t// Flush cached discovery responses whenever services configuration change.\n893: \tserviceHandler := func(prev, curr *model.Service, event model.Event) {\n894: \t\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\n895: \t\t\tFull:           true,\n896: \t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: string(curr.Hostname), Namespace: curr.Attributes.Namespace}),\n897: \t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ServiceUpdate),\n898: \t\t}\n899: \t\ts.XDSServer.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\n900: \t}\n901: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/bootstrap/server.go%3FL600-900"
  }
}

\\n957: // initIstiodCerts creates Istiod certificates to be used by gRPC and webhook TLS servers\\n958: // and also sets up watches to them. It also detects and sets the root certificates\\n959: // for replication.\\n960: //\\n961: // This will also detect the root CAs (mesh trust) and set it up for Istiod as well as\\n962: // namespace replication, if the controller is enabled.\\n963: //\\n964: // Will prefer local certificates, and fallback to using the CA to sign a fresh, temporary\\n965: // certificate.\\n966: func (s *Server) initIstiodCerts(args *PilotArgs, host string) error {\\n967: \\t// Skip all certificates\\n968: \\tvar err error\\n969: \\n970: \\ts.dnsNames = getDNSNames(args, host)\\n971: \\tif \\t\\t\\tCertFile:   tlsCertPath,\\n977: \\t\\t})\\n978: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n979: \\t\\t\\t// Not crashing istiod - This typically happens if certs are missing and in tests.\\n980: \\t\\t\\tlog.Errorf(\\\"error initializing certificate watches: %v\\\", err)\\n981: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n982: \\t\\t}\\n983: \\t} else if features.EnableCAServer && features.PilotCertProvider == early.\\n988: \\t\\t// Unlike other failures that we can recover from, this has no mitigation, user must\\n989: \\t\\t// choose a different source.\\n990: \\t\\t// The feature didn't work for few the\\n1003: \\t\\t// namespace controller.\\n1004: \\t\\t// The new behavior appears safer - IMO we may also do a fatal unless provider is\\n1005: \\t\\t// set to \\\"none\\\" because it is not clear what the user intends.\\n1006: \\n1007: \\t\\t// Skip invoking initIstiodCertLoader - we have no cert.\\n1008: \\t\\treturn nil\\n1009: \\t}\\n1010: \\n1011: \\tif err == nil {\\n1012: \\t\\terr = s.initIstiodCertLoader()\\n1013: \\t}\\n1014: \\n1015: \\treturn err\\n1016: }\\n1017: \\n1018: func getDNSNames(args *PilotArgs, host string) []string {\\n1019: \\t// Append custom hostname if there     "content": "900: \t}\n901: \ts.ServiceController().AppendServiceHandler(serviceHandler)\n902: \n903: \tif s.configController != nil {\n904: \t\tconfigHandler := func(prev config.Config, curr config.Config, event model.Event) {\n905: \t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Handle event %s for configuration %s\", event, curr.Key())\n906: \t\t\t// For update events, trigger push only if spec has changed.\n907: \t\t\tif event == model.EventUpdate && !needsPush(prev, curr) {\n908: \t\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"skipping push for %s as spec has not changed\", prev.Key())\n909: \t\t\t\treturn\n910: \t\t\t}\n911: \t\t\tpushReq := &model.PushRequest{\n912: \t\t\t\tFull:           true,\n913: \t\t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: gvk.MustToKind(curr.GroupVersionKind), Name: curr.Name, Namespace: curr.Namespace}),\n914: \t\t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.ConfigUpdate),\n915: \t\t\t}\n916: \t\t\ts.XDSServer.ConfigUpdate(pushReq)\n917: \t\t}\n918: \t\tschemas := collections.Pilot.All()\n919: \t\tif features.EnableGatewayAPI {\n920: \t\t\tschemas = collections.PilotGatewayAPI().All()\n921: \t\t}\n922: \t\tfor _, schema := range schemas {\n923: \t\t\t// This resource type was handled in external/servicediscovery.go, no need to rehandle here.\n924: \t\t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.ServiceEntry {\n925: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n926: \t\t\t}\n927: \t\t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.WorkloadEntry {\n928: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n929: \t\t\t}\n930: \t\t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind() == gvk.WorkloadGroup {\n931: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n932: \t\t\t}\n933: \t\t\t// Already handled by gateway controller\n934: \t\t\tif schema.GroupVersionKind().Group == gvk.KubernetesGateway.Group {\n935: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n936: \t\t\t}\n937: \n938: \t\t\ts.configController.RegisterEventHandler(schema.GroupVersionKind(), configHandler)\n939: \t\t}\n940: \t}\n941: }\n942: \n943: // initIstiodCertLoader will make sure istiodCertBundleWatcher is updating\n944: // the certs and updates Server.istiodCert - which is returned on all TLS requests.\n945: func (s *Server) initIstiodCertLoader() error {\n946: \tif err := s.loadIstiodCert(); err != nil {\n947: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"first time load IstiodCert failed: %v\", err)\n948: \t}\n949: \t_, watchCh := s.istiodCertBundleWatcher.AddWatcher()\n950: \ts.addStartFunc(\"reload certs\", func(stop <-chan struct{}) error {\n951: \t\tgo s.reloadIstiodCert(watchCh, stop)\n952: \t\treturn nil\n953: \t})\n954: \treturn nil\n955: }\n956: \n957: // initIstiodCerts creates Istiod certificates to be used by gRPC and webhook TLS servers\n958: // and also sets up watches to them. It also detects and sets the root certificates\n959: // for replication.\n960: //\n961: // This will also detect the root CAs (mesh trust) and set it up for Istiod as well as\n962: // namespace replication, if the controller is enabled.\n963: //\n964: // Will prefer local certificates, and fallback to using the CA to sign a fresh, temporary\n965: // certificate.\n966: func (s *Server) initIstiodCerts(args *PilotArgs, host string) error {\n967: \t// Skip all certificates\n968: \tvar err error\n969: \n970: \ts.dnsNames = getDNSNames(args, host)\n971: \tif hasCustomCertArgsOrWellKnown, tlsCertPath, tlsKeyPath, caCertPath := hasCustomTLSCerts(args.ServerOptions.TLSOptions); hasCustomCertArgsOrWellKnown {\n972: \t\t// Use the DNS certificate provided via args or in well known location.\n973: \t\terr = s.initFileCertificateWatches(TLSOptions{\n974: \t\t\tCaCertFile: caCertPath,\n975: \t\t\tKeyFile:    tlsKeyPath,\n976: \t\t\tCertFile:   tlsCertPath,\n977: \t\t})\n978: \t\tif err != nil {\n979: \t\t\t// Not crashing istiod - This typically happens if certs are missing and in tests.\n980: \t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"error initializing certificate watches: %v\", err)\n981: \t\t\treturn nil\n982: \t\t}\n983: \t} else if features.EnableCAServer && features.PilotCertProvider == constants.CertProviderIstiod {\n984: \t\tlog.Infof(\"initializing Istiod DNS certificates host: %s, custom host: %s\", host, features.IstiodServiceCustomHost)\n985: \t\terr = s.initDNSCertsIstiod()\n986: \t} else if features.PilotCertProvider == constants.CertProviderKubernetes {\n987: \t\t// This will not work - better to fail Istiod startup so it can be detected early.\n988: \t\t// Unlike other failures that we can recover from, this has no mitigation, user must\n989: \t\t// choose a different source.\n990: \t\t// The feature didn't work for few releases, but a skip-version upgrade may still\n991: \t\t// encounter it.\n992: \t\tlog.Fatal(\"PILOT_CERT_PROVIDER=kubernetes is no longer supported by upstream K8S\")\n993: \t} else if strings.HasPrefix(features.PilotCertProvider, constants.CertProviderKubernetesSignerPrefix) {\n994: \t\tlog.Infof(\"initializing Istiod DNS certificates using K8S RA:%s  host: %s, custom host: %s\", features.PilotCertProvider,\n995: \t\t\thost, features.IstiodServiceCustomHost)\n996: \t\terr = s.initDNSCertsK8SRA()\n997: \t} else {\n998: \t\tlog.Warnf(\"PILOT_CERT_PROVIDER=%s is not implemented\", features.PilotCertProvider)\n999: \n1000: \t\t// In Istio 1.22 - we return nil here - the old code in s.initDNSCerts used to have\n1001: \t\t// an 'else' to handle the unknown providers by not initializing the TLS certs but\n1002: \t\t// still setting the root from /etc/certs/root-cert.pem for distribution in the\n1003: \t\t// namespace controller.\n1004: \t\t// The new behavior appears safer - IMO we may also do a fatal unless provider is\n1005: \t\t// set to \"none\" because it is not clear what the user intends.\n1006: \n1007: \t\t// Skip invoking initIstiodCertLoader - we have no cert.\n1008: \t\treturn nil\n1009: \t}\n1010: \n1011: \tif err == nil {\n1012: \t\terr = s.initIstiodCertLoader()\n1013: \t}\n1014: \n1015: \treturn err\n1016: }\n1017: \n1018: func getDNSNames(args *PilotArgs, host string) []string {\n1019: \t// Append custom hostname if there is any\n1020: \tcustomHost := features.IstiodServiceCustomHost\n1021: \tvar cHosts []string\n1022: \n1023: \tif customHost != \"\" {\n1024: \t\tcHosts = strings.Split(customHost, \",\")\n1025: \t}\n1026: \tsans := sets.New(cHosts...)\n1027: \tsans.Insert(host)\n1028: \t// The first is the recommended one, also used by Apiserver for webhooks.\n1029: \t// add a few known hostnames\n1030: \tknownHosts := []string{\"istiod\", \"istiod-remote\", \"istio-pilot\"}\n1031: \t// In some conditions, pilot address for sds is different from other xds,\n1032: \t// like multi-cluster primary-remote mode with revision.\n1033: \tif args.Revision != \"\" && args.Revision != \"default\" {\n1034: \t\tknownHosts = append(knownHosts, \"istiod\"+\"-\"+args.Revision)\n1035: \t}\n1036: \tknownSans := make([]string, 0, 2*len(knownHosts))\n1037: \tfor _, altName := range knownHosts {\n1038: \t\tknownSans = append(knownSans,\n1039: \t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s.svc\", altName, args.Namespace))\n1040: \t}\n1041: \tsans.InsertAll(knownSans...)\n1042: \tdnsNames := sets.SortedList(sans)\n1043: \tlog.Infof(\"Discover server subject alt names: %v\", dnsNames)\n1044: \treturn dnsNames\n1045: }\n1046: \n1047: // createPeerCertVerifier creates a SPIFFE certificate verifier with the current istiod configuration.\n1048: func (s *Server) createPeerCertVerifier(tlsOptions TLSOptions, trustDomain string) (*spiffe.PeerCertVerifier, error) {\n1049: \tcustomTLSCertsExists, _, _, caCertPath := hasCustomTLSCerts(tlsOptions)\n1050: \tif !customTLSCertsExists && s.CA == nil && !s.isK8SSigning() {\n1051: \t\t// Running locally without configured certs - no TLS mode\n1052: \t\treturn nil, nil\n1053: \t}\n1054: \tpeerCertVerifier := spiffe.NewPeerCertVerifier()\n1055: \tvar rootCertBytes []byte\n1056: \tvar err error\n1057: \tif caCertPath != \"\" {\n1058: \t\tif rootCertBytes, err = os.ReadFile(caCertPath); err != nil {\n1059: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n1060: \t\t}\n1061: \t} else {\n1062: \t\tif s.RA != nil {\n1063: \t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(features.PilotCertProvider, constants.CertProviderKubernetesSignerPrefix) {\n1064: \t\t\t\tsignerName := strings.TrimPrefix(features.PilotCertProvider, constants.CertProviderKubernetesSignerPrefix)\n1065: \t\t\t\tcaBundle, _ := s.RA.GetRootCertFromMeshConfig(signerName)\n1066: \t\t\t\trootCertBytes = append(rootCertBytes, caBundle...)\n1067: \t\t\t} else {\n1068: \t\t\t\trootCertBytes = append(rootCertBytes, s.RA.GetCAKeyCertBundle().GetRootCertPem()...)\n1069: \t\t\t}\n1070: \t\t}\n1071: \t\tif s.CA != nil {\n1072: \t\t\trootCertBytes = append(rootCertBytes, s.CA.GetCAKeyCertBundle().GetRootCertPem()...)\n1073: \t\t}\n1074: \t}\n1075: \n1076: \tif len(rootCertBytes) != 0 {\n1077: \t\t// TODO: trustDomain here is static and will not update if it dynamically changes in mesh config\n1078: \t\terr := peerCertVerifier.AddMappingFromPEM(trustDomain, rootCertBytes)\n1079: \t\tif err != nil {\n1080: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"add root CAs into peerCertVerifier failed: %v\", err)\n1081: \t\t}\n1082: \t}\n1083: \n1084: \treturn peerCertVerifier, nil\n1085: }\n1086: \n1087: func checkPathsExist(paths ...string) bool {\n1088: \tfor _, path := range paths {\n1089: \t\tfInfo, err := os.Stat(path)\n1090: \n1091: \t\tif err != nil || fInfo.IsDir() {\n1092: \t\t\treturn false\n1093: \t\t}\n1094: \t}\n1095: \treturn true\n1096: }\n1097: \n1098: // hasCustomTLSCerts returns the tls cert paths, used both if custom TLS certificates are configured via args or by mounting in well known.\n1099: // while tls args should still take precedence the aim is to encourage loading the DNS tls cert in the well known path locations.\n1100: func hasCustomTLSCerts(tlsOptions TLSOptions) (ok bool, tlsCertPath, tlsKeyPath, caCertPath string) {\n1101: ",
